Mine's in the front of the show 'cause mine are radiant action. Yellow orange red and yellow and green. Why a Name Study? As K teachers, we recognize that the one thing the kids can read and write when entering K is their names. We put their names on bulletin boards, on their cubbies, in the meeting area and class chart. From the very first day of school, we sing songs to learn each others names. A name study unit encompasses the entire individual and helps us focus on bringing the children out of their shells. By using their names, we may find that they are more focused and less distracted. The main language / literacy goal is to learn letter names/sounds, rhymes, sentence segmentation, syllabification and initial/end sounds. The Name Game The name game examines one child's name at a time. We use the smart board and dissect a child's name on it. The smart board allows us to save and compare later. Initially, the students names are written on large cardstock and placed on the floor in front of the class. We count the letters, vowels and consonants. Then we talk about how to cut the name into its syllables or parts. Example: Na-o-mi. The first cut would happen after the a, the second after the o. Then we cut up the entire thing and look at each of the letters. We then move the letters around and make words. We find rhymes, both nonsensical and real. To complete the experience, we ask each child three interview questions in order to find out more about them. It is our hope that those children that are weak with some of these skills will get stronger as the weeks go on, we choose their names last so as to give them time to process and learn. Box o' Memories This activity is tied to our name game. We schedule each parent to come and talk about the origins of their child's name and share their fondest memories. Our parents readily enter the classroom excited and ready to share about their child. Parents bring everything: swatches of hair, umbilical cords, bronzed shoes, digital slide shows, clothing, worn blankets, old rattles, stuffed animals and even diapers (clean). The final component of the share is the question and answer session between the child and their classmates. Our kids this year asked some of the funniest questions...What was your first candy? When did you stop wearing diapers?, What was your first/favorite food? We manage to do our box o' memories in twenty minutes at the start of the day. We try our best to get our parents off to work by 9 am in the morning. Occasionally, we have afternoon shares. These tend to work great as they are scheduled close to dismissal which allows our parents to take the kids home with you at the end of the day. Today, let's talk about something called "R-squared" in a fun and easy way that even grade-schoolers can understand. Imagine you are trying to guess your friends' heights using their shoe sizes. Some of your friends might have bigger feet and also be taller, so you could do pretty well guessing those friends' heights based on their shoe sizes. But other friends might not follow that pattern; maybe they have big feet but are shorter or vice versa. So, while you did great guessing some friends' heights, your overall guesses won't be super accurate because of the others who didn't fit the pattern. Now, back to our original question: why can the individual R-squared (your success guessing heights for just one friend) be higher than the overall R-squared (your success guessing everyone's height)? Well, sometimes patterns we find in small groups don't hold up when we combine them into larger groups. It's like having lots of little puzzles; solving each puzzle by itself may be easier, but putting all the pieces together to form a giant puzzle might make things more complicated. In short, R-squared shows us how good our predictions are compared to simply guessing the average value. When looking at many subgroups, the overall R-squared tells us how well our prediction method works for the entire group, whereas the individual R-squared measures show how effective the same method was for smaller groups. Sometimes these individual ones appear better than the overall number, which means that the chosen approach fits certain parts of the data quite nicely but struggles with the rest combined. Hopefully, now you get the idea behind R-squared and its differences between individual and overall calculations! But my milk had poured all over the place. The HMG-CoA Reductase enzyme causes the body to make more cholesterol. If it is inhibited, the body makes "less" cholesterol. So statin drugs lower the amount of LDL (bad) cholesterol in the blood which stops atherosclerosis from getting worse. Statin drugs can even help make atherosclerosis better. However, statins are not as good at increasing the HDL (good) cholesterol. Low HDL is hard to treat with medicines, but goes up with more exercise. The Function Machine Game Math concepts: odd numbers, even numbers, greater-than/less-than, rounding off, addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, fractions, negative numbers, prime numbers, square numbers, problem solving, mental math Number of players: two or more Equipment: pencil (or pen) and paper for every player Each player needs pencil and paper of his own. One person plays the role of the function machine, and he must make up a rule for calculating with whatever numbers the other players will give him. In math, a function is any rule that turns one number into another*, such as: - Double the number, and then add seven. The function machine should write his function rule on a piece of paper and slip it in his pocket. All the other players make themselves a chart to keep track of the game. *[In reality, the definition of a function is a bit more complicated than that, but it suffices for this game.] How to Play Play proceeds to the left around the table. - Each player in turn says any number he wishes, and all players write that number in their Input columns. The function machine calculates with that number, mentally or on scratch paper, and says only the answer to his calculation. - If that input number is too difficult for the player to work with, he should say, "That's too hard," and ask for a different number. Occasionally, players will give an input number that does not make sense with the chosen rule, in which case the machine answers, "That does not compute." - Whatever answer the machine gives, all other players write it in their Output columns. - After receiving an answer, the player whose turn it is may make a guess at what calculation the machine is doing. Other players may not guess at the function rule until their own turns. The first player to guess the rule of the function machine wins that round. If you have time to play a full game, where every player has a chance to be the machine, then the winner of the game is the player who gave the most output numbers before the others guessed his function rule. My math club students have always enjoyed this game, especially when it is their turn to be the function machine with the secret rule. But some freeze mentally at the task of creating their function rule, while other students make up rules that are so complex they are nearly impossible to guess. You may want to make a list of suggested function rules in advance, writing the rules on index cards and letting each player draw a rule from your deck. Here are some function ideas to get you started: - Add 15 to the number. - Subtract 4 from the number. - Multiply the number by 8. - Cut the number in half. - Cut the number in half, and then add one. - Double the number, and then subtract three. - Add the next larger number, so an input of 3 gives an output of 3 + 4 = 7. - Multiply by the next larger number, so an input of 5 gives an output of 5 6 = 30. - Triple the next larger number. - Subtract the number from 25. - Square the number, and then add one. - Say the largest multiple of 3 that is less than the number. - Round off to the nearest hundred. - Double the odd numbers, but cut the even numbers in half. - All odd numbers are 17, but all even numbers are 12. - Say the ones digit of the number. - Add the digits in the number together. - If the number is prime, say the number. If the number is not prime, say 1. Remember that one, zero, and all negative numbers are not prime. - Say the tenths digit of the number (the first digit after the decimal place). The student may need a calculator to rapidly convert fractions to decimal numbers. This is a great game to play in the car on family trips, since there are no little game pieces and the paper does not need to be passes around. Even the driver can join in the fun. Beware that some function rules can be described in more than one way. For example, the rule: - Double the number, and then add two. could also be written as: - Double the next higher number. It helps to have a referee who knows algebra to judge the guesses. "Double the number, and then add two" is written as , while "double the next higher number" is , and anyone who knows algebra can easily see these are the same. The player who tries to guess the rule does not have to put it in the exact words the function machine used, as long as the statements are equivalent. Chondrichhye is the class of fish to which sharks belong. The shark is the largest fish in the sea. Sharks have been around for more than 300 million years. They were around even before dinosaurs. There are about 350 different types of sharks, but researchers think there are other sharks that haven't been discovered yet. 2. A shark does not have a single bone in it body, instead it has a skeleton made up of cartilage. Cartilage is a tough material, like the material that shapes your ear. A shark has two dorsal fins on its back, one beneath its body, which is called the pectoral fin (all species do not have that) and the tail which is the caudal fin. Sharks are strong, healthy creatures. No other living thing can take better care of itself than a shark can. Sharks never get cancer, so their cartilage is being studied with the hope of developing anti-cancer drugs. 3. Most sharks live for about 25 years, while some can live to be a 100 years too. 4. Most kinds of sharks can swim up to 20-40 miles per hour. The Mayo shark is said to be able to swim at more than 60 miles per hour. Not all species of sharks are required to be in continuous motion to breathe, however a shark's body is heavier than the sea, so if it stops moving it will sink. Sharks do not sleep in the same way as humans. Even if they seem to be sleeping they are not, instead they are just resting. 5. A shark's teeth are usually replaced every eight days. Some species of sharks shed about 30,000 teeth in their lifetime. When a shark loses a tooth, one replaces it. A Whale Shark has more than 4,000 teeth, but each is less than 1/8 inch long. The largest shark teeth found belonged to a Carcharodon Magaloden shark and are 6 inches long. This shark is extinct and lived more than 4.5 million years ago. 6. Sharks have very good senses. Sometimes a shark is called "a swimming nose", for its great sense of smell. Sharks can easily detect prey that is in the sand, as well as at night. A shark also has a remarkable sensitivity to vibrations in the water. It can feel the movements made by other animals that are hundreds of feet away. They can hear sounds from thousands of feet away. Sharks can tell the direction from where the sound is coming from, too. 7. A shark's skin is covered by small, razor-sharp teeth called denticles. These can be removed from the shark's body. Once removed, the remaining skin is made into strong leather. 8. Some sharks lay eggs, while most give birth to babies. The Hammerhead and Tiger shark can have as many as 40 babies at one time, while the others can have six to twelve. 9. One good meal is enough to satisfy a shark's hunger. The meal can last a shark for a long period because it uses very little energy to swim. Some sharks hold food in their stomachs without it being digested. If they eat a big meal, it can last three or more months. Weird things have been found inside a shark, such as a nail, a bottle of wine, a treasure chest, coats, a suit of armor, a drum and a torpedo. Like humans, sharks are at the top of the food chain. More people are killed by bee stings than by shark attacks. 10. The Whale shark is considered the biggest fish in the world. The Whale shark is about 90,000 pounds in weight. The Basking shark is the second largest. It can be as long as 40 feet. The Pygmy shark is about 11 inches in length. The Dwarf shark is as tiny as a human hand. Great white sharks can grow about 10 inches every year, which means they can grow to be 12 to 14 feet long. I had hoped that you would have spared me the necessity. But since I must be plain, it is because I have just learnt that you, Madame Rocada, are the lady known abroad as the White Countess, the keeper of the Paris gaming-house in which the son of one of my friends, young Hugh Grey, committed suicide two years ago." CHAPTER VII Audrey was struck dumb. As if by a flash of lightning, she found her whole mind illuminated, and saw at once the meaning of the visit paid to her showrooms by the lady in white. The pale, thin woman was, she felt sure, the real Madame Rocada, and it was the discovery that another woman was setting up in business under her name which brought the vengeful stranger on the visit which had turned out so fatally for herself. The sudden understanding of the strange events in which she had been forced to take so active a part not only paralysed Audrey, but made her feel as if this direct attack were quite a secondary matter. And being brought before the seate of Justice, according as _Andreana_ was, freed her selfe from the power of the Law; yet neither by force, or her owne vertue, but by her sodaine and inopinate death. And although the nature of Love is such (according as wee have oftentimes heeretofore maintained) to make his abiding in the houses of the Noblest persons; yet men and women of poore and farre inferiour quality, do not alwayes sit out of his reach, though enclosed in their meanest Cottages; declaring himselfe sometimes as powerfull a commaunder in those humble places, as he doth in the richest and most imperious Palaces. Now, albeit shee was not supplied by any better meanes, then to maintaine her selfe by her owne painfull travell, & earne her bread before shee could eate it, by carding and spinning to such as employed her; yet was she not of so base or dejected a spirit, but had both courage and sufficient vertue, to understand the secret solicitings of love, and to distinguish the parts of well deserving, both by private behaviour and outward ceremony. My sing a got sparklers in it? From Egg to Adult Snails start life as an egg buried in a nest a few centimeters below the surface of the ground. Snail eggs hatch after about two to four weeks depending on the weather and environmental conditions (most importantly, temperature and soil moisture). After hatching, the newborn snail sets out on an urgent search for food. The young snails are so hungry, they feed on the leftover shell and any nearby eggs that have not yet hatched. As the snail grows, so does its shell. The oldest part of the shell is located at the center of the coil while the most recently added parts of the shell are at the rim. When the snail matures after a few years, the snail mates and lays eggs, thus completing the full life cycle of a snail. Title: How to Cancel HBO Now Subscription on Your iPhone or iPad Introduction: HBO Now is a popular streaming service that offers access to HBO's library of movies, TV shows, documentaries, and specials. If you have subscribed through your iPhone or iPad and wish to cancel, this step-by-step guide will help you do so with ease. Before starting, make sure you are logged into the correct Apple ID account associated with your HBO Now subscription. Step 1: Open Settings App The first step is to open the pre-installed Settings app on your home screen. This app allows you to manage all aspects related to device settings, including managing your subscriptions. Step 2: Tap on Your Name / Apple ID Banner At the top of the Settings main page, tap on the banner displaying your name or Apple ID email address. It might require entering your passcode or FaceID/TouchID depending upon your security settings. Step 3: Select 'Subscriptions' Option From the list displayed after tapping on your profile, scroll down until you find the option labeled 'Subscriptions'. Clicking here will take you to a new menu showing all active and expired subscriptions tied to your account. Tip: If you cannot see the 'Subscriptions' option directly under your personal details, try navigating from 'Settings > iTunes & App Store > Apple ID > View Apple ID', then select 'Subscriptions' from there. Step 4: Locate HBO NOW Subscription Once inside the Subscriptions section, look for 'HBO NOW' among other listed services. The status should indicate whether it's still Active or has already Expired. Step 5: Tap on HBO NOW Subscription After finding the HBO NOW subscription entry, tap on it which leads us to another window where we can modify our plan, view renewal dates, etc. Step 6: Choose 'Cancel Subscription' In this final stage, click on 'Cancel Subscription' located at the bottom of the screen. Confirm cancellation when prompted if necessary. A message confirming successful cancellation should appear along with the date till which your subscription remains valid before termination takes effect. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Unsubscribing removes future billings but doesn't refund any previously charged amounts. Therefore, consider unsubscribing towards the end of your current billing cycle. - After canceling, you'll continue having access to HBO NOW content until the last day of your paid period expires. - Remember to check the effective date of cancellation carefully as sometimes delays may occur due to system processing times. - Double-check that you've selected the right Apple ID account (especially relevant for those who operate multiple accounts). - Be aware of potential auto-renewals post free trials; always review terms & conditions prior to opting in to avoid unwanted charges. Conclusion: By following these simple steps meticulously, users can effortlessly cancel their HBO NOW subscriptions linked to their iPhone or iPad devices. Keeping track of one's digital expenditures promotes financial responsibility while ensuring only utilized platforms remain active, contributing positively to user experience and satisfaction. When Edison was twelve years old, he contracted scarlet fever. The effects of the fever, as well as getting picked up by the ears by a train conductor, caused Edison to become completely deaf in his left ear, and 80 percent deaf in the other. He learned Morse code of the telegraph, and began a job as a "brass pounder" (telegraph operator). At age sixteen, Edison made his first invention, which was called an "automatic repeater." [Mother continues telling Melissa about broken dishes] Creating a slideshow from selected photos on your iPhone or iPad is an excellent way to showcase your memories in a dynamic and engaging format. This tutorial will guide you through the process using Apple's built-in Photos app, providing detailed explanations, key tips, and best practices along the way. **Part 1: Selecting Your Photos** 1. **Open the Photos App:** The first step in creating a slideshow is to open the Photos app on your iPhone or iPad. This app stores all of your device's photos and videos, making it the perfect place to begin crafting your custom slideshow. 2. **Navigate to your desired album or Moment:** To create a slideshow with specific pictures, navigate to the folder or album containing those images. You can do this by tapping on 'Albums' at the bottom of the screen and selecting the appropriate collection. Alternatively, if you want to choose images from various locations, go back to the main library view ('Photos') and select individual pictures as needed. 3. **Select multiple photos:** Once you have chosen your starting point, tap on one photo to enlarge it. Then, tap on additional images while holding down the shift button (for iOS 13 and later) or tapping quickly without releasing the finger (iOS 12 and earlier). A small blue checkmark should appear on each selected image. Key tip: Remember that order matters! If you would like the photos to display in a particular sequence within the slideshow, ensure they are arranged accordingly before proceeding. **Part 2: Preparing Your Selection** 4. **Tap the 'Share' Button:** After selecting your desired photos, locate the share icon - represented by a square with an arrow pointing outwards - typically found in the lower left corner of the screen. Tap on this button to reveal sharing options. 5. **Choose 'Slideshow':** Scroll horizontally across the bottom row of available actions until you find 'Slideshow'. Tap on this option; doing so will initiate the creation of your new slideshow using the selected images. 6. **Customize Settings (Optional):** Before playing your slideshow, take advantage of any customization settings offered by tapping on the ellipsis ( ) next to 'Options.' Here, you may adjust transitions between photos, enable music playback, and modify theme styles. Keep in mind these preferences can be changed during future viewings if necessary. **Part 3: Playing & Saving Your Slideshow** 7. **Preview Your Slideshow:** With everything set up, simply press 'Play' located near the top right corner of the screen to start viewing your newly created slideshow. As mentioned previously, feel free to make changes to transition types, themes, or audio selections directly from here via the 'Options' menu. 8. **Save Your Slideshow:** Upon completing your initial preview, consider saving your customized slideshow for easy access later. Accomplish this by navigating back to the primary 'Sharing Options' page (where you initially chose 'Slideshow'). Instead, look for the 'Save Video' button - usually depicted as a rectangle with a downward facing arrow - towards the far right side. Taping this will save your current slideshow configuration as a video file within your Camera Roll. By following these eight steps, users can successfully curate memorable moments into captivating visual presentations on their iPhones or iPads. By understanding the nuances behind each stage, individuals gain both mastery over the tool itself and increased creative freedom when designing personalized digital experiences. Exporting PST (Personal Storage Table) files to PDF format can be useful for sharing or archiving your emails, contacts, and other items from Microsoft Outlook without requiring the recipient to have access to the original PST file. This tutorial will guide you through using Systools Outlook PST Viewer PRO to export PST to PDF. **Step 1: Download and Install Systools Outlook PST Viewer PRO** First, visit the official website of Systools Software and download the latest version of their Outlook PST Viewer PRO product. Once downloaded, run the installer and follow the prompts to complete installation. Make sure to read and accept any license agreements as necessary. Key Tip: Choose an appropriate location during installation where you can quickly locate the software later. **Step 2: Launch Systools Outlook PST Viewer PRO** After successful installation, open the application by clicking its icon on your desktop or via the Start menu. Familiarize yourself with the user interface so that you feel comfortable navigating within the program. Guideline: The main window should display options such as 'Open PST File', 'Save Converted Files', etc., which we'll be using shortly. **Step 3: Open Your PST File** Click on the 'Open PST File' button usually located at the top left corner of the interface. Navigate through your computer directories to find and select the target PST file you wish to convert into PDF format. Click 'Open' after selecting the file. Key Tip: If you cannot find your PST file, try looking inside your Documents folder or check if there is a default backup created by MS Outlook itself. **Step 4: Preview Contents of the PST File** Once opened, you'll see all folders contained within the selected PST file listed on the left side pane. You can browse these folders just like in actual Outlook. Take some time to review them and ensure everything looks correct before proceeding with conversion. Guideline: Pay attention to subfolders too since they might contain important data as well. They say it hurts workers, because businesses make more money by selling things than they pay the workers who make the things. Business owners become rich while workers remain poor and exploited (taken advantage of). They also argue society would be more efficient if people thought less about competing against one another for their own interests and thought more of working together for the overall good of society. Another argument is that each person has a right to basic needs (such as food and shelter). Within capitalism, sometimes people might not get everything they need to live. This high-flying condition of the tresses, and the singularity of the ornaments which appeared to be thrown at haphazard into them, suggested so oddly the idea of a bewitched person, that I could scarcely converse with any presence of mind, or realize that these really were the nice, well-informed, sensible little girls of my own neighborhood,--the good daughters, good sisters, Sunday-school teachers, and other familiar members of our best educated circles; and I came away from the party in a sort of blue maze, and hardly in a state to conduct myself with credit in the examination through which I knew Jenny would put me as to the appearance of her different friends. Title: How to Evolve Eevee into Its Seven Different Forms in Pok mon Platinum Introduction: In Pok mon Platinum, there are seven different forms that Eevee can evolve into - Vaporeon, Jolteon, Flareon, Espeon, Umbreon, Leafeon, and Glaceon. Each form has its unique abilities, strengths, and weaknesses. This tutorial will guide you through the process of evolving your Eevee into any one of these forms using various methods found within Pok mon Platinum. Section 1: Preparation Step 1: Capture an Eevee Before starting this evolution journey, first, you need to catch yourself an Eevee. You can find wild Eevees in tall grass areas like Route 203 or inside the Pok Mart at Hearthome City. Make sure your team has enough space for another member! Key Tips & Guidelines: - The level of the Eevee doesn't matter when trying to evolve it; however, higher levels mean stronger evolved versions. - If possible, try catching multiple Eevees so you have more chances to get all seven evolutions. Step 2: Level Up Your Eevee To initiate most of the evolutions, you must level up your Eevee. Therefore, engage them in battles, defeat other trainers, participate in Contests, etc., until they gain experience points (EXP) and eventually level up. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use EXP Share if available as it distributes EXP evenly among party members regardless of whether they participated in battle or not. - Feeding Eevee vitamins such as Protein, Iron, Carbos, HP Up, and Calcium also increases their base stats while training but does not replace actual battling. Play the recording to hear the proper pronunciation of the shapes. Then read the example sentences and take the quiz. (Listen to the pronunciation.) 1. The roof of a house is shaped like a triangle (noun). The roof is triangular (adjective). 2. The clock on the wall is shaped like a circle. The clock is circular. (or The clock is round.) 3. The table in the room is shaped like a rectangle. The table is rectangular. 4. The necklace I wear is shaped like a heart. My necklace is heart-shaped. 5. The picture on the wall is shaped like a square. The picture is square. 6. The rock in the garden is shaped like an oval. The rock is oval. 7. The nose on the pumpkin is shaped like a star. The pumpkin's nose is star-shaped. 8. The ornament on the tree is shaped like a diamond. The ornament is diamond-shaped. 9.The stop sign on my street is shaped like an octagon. The sign is octagonal. In the continuance of these proceedings, it came to passe, that master Doctor _Mazzeo_ (being not onely a most expert Physitian, but likewise as skilfull in Chirurgerie beside) hadde a Pacient in cure, who by great misfortune, had one of his legges broken all in pieces; which some weaker judgement having formerly dealt withall, the bones and sinewes were become so fowly putrified, as he tolde the parties friends, that the legge must bee quite cut off, or elsee the Pacient must needes dye: yet he intended so to order the matter, that the perrill should proceede no further, to prejudice any other part of the bodie. The case beeing thus resolved on with the Pacient and his Friends, the day and time was appointed when the deede should be done: and the Doctor conceyving, that except the Patient were sleepily entranced, hee could not by anie meanes endure the paine, but must needes hinder what he meant to do: by distillation hee made such an artificiall Water, as (after the Pacient hath receyved it) it will procure a kinde of dead sleepe, and endure so long a space, as necessity requireth the use thereof, in full performance of the worke. After he had made this sleepy water, he put it into a glasse, wherewith it was filled (almost) up to the brimme; and till the time came when hee should use it; hee set it in his owne Chamber-Windowe, never acquainting any one, to what purpose he had provided the water, nor what was his reason of setting it there; when it drew towards the evening, and he was returned home from his pacients, a Messenger brought him Letters from _Malfy_, concerning a great conflict hapning there between two Noble Families, wherein divers were very dangerously wounded on either side, and without his speedy repairing thither, it would prove to the losse of many lives. Vector Dot Product and Vector Length Definitions of the vector dot product and vector length Vector Dot Product and Vector Length Use this menu to view and help create subtitles for this video in many different languages. and you've seen that. - If you just have two vectors, say a1, a2, all the - way down to a n. - We defined the addition of this vector and let's say some - other vector, b1, b2, all the way down to - bn as a third vector. - If you add these two, we defined the addition operation - to be a third-- you will result in a third vector where - each of its components are just the sum of the - corresponding components of the two vectors you're adding. - So it's going to be a1 plus b1, a2 plus b2, all the way - down to a n plus bn. - We knew this and we've done multiple videos where we use - this definition of vector addition. - We also know about scalar multiplication. - Maybe we should just call it scaling multiplication. - And that's the case of look, if I have some real number c - and I multiply it times some vector, a1, a2, all the way - down to a n, we defined scalar multiplication of a vector to - be-- some scalar times its vector will result in - essentially, this vector were each of its components are - multiplied by the scalar. - ca1, ca2, all the way down to c a n. - And so after seeing these two operations, you might be - tempted to say, gee, wouldn't it be nice if there was some - way to multiply two vectors. - This is just a scalar times a vector, just scaling it up. - And that's actually the actual effect of what it's doing if - you visualize it in three dimensions or less. - It's actually scaling the size of the vector. - And we haven't defined size, very precisely just yet. - But you understand at least this operation. - For multiplying vectors or taking the product, there's - actually two ways. - And I'm going to define one of them in this video. - And that's the dot product. - And you signify the dot product by saying a dot v. - So they borrowed one of the types of multiplication - notations that you saw, but you can't write across here. - That'll be actually a different type of vector - So the dot product is-- it's almost fun to take because - it's mathematically pretty straightforward, unlike the - cross product. - But it's fun to take and it's interesting because it - results-- so this is a1, a2, all the way down to a n. - That vector dot my b vector: b1, b2, all the way down to bn - is going to be equal to the product of each of their - corresponding components. - So a1 b1 added together plus a2 b2 plus a3 b3 plus all the - way to a n, bn. - So what is this? - Is this a vector? - Well no, this is just a number. - This is just going to be a real number. - You're just taking the product and adding together a bunch of - real numbers. - So this is just going to be a scalar, a real scalar. - So this is just going to be a scalar right there. - So in the dot product you multiply two vectors and you - end up with a scalar value. - Let me show you a couple of examples just in case this was - a little bit too abstract. - So let's say that we take the dot product of the vector 2, 5 - and we're going to dot that with the vector 7, 1. - Well, this is just going to be equal to 2 times 7 plus 5 - times 1 or 14 plus 6. - No, sorry. - 14 plus 5, which is equal to 19. - So the dot product of this vector and this vector is 19. - Let me do one more example, although I think this is a - pretty straightforward idea. - Let me do it in mauve. - Say I had the vector 1, 2, 3 and I'm going to dot that with - the vector minus 2, 0, 5. - So it's 1 times minus 2 plus 2 times 0 plus 3 times 5. - So it's minus 2 plus 0 plus 15. - Minus 2 plus 15 is equal to 13. - That's the dot product by this definition. - Now, I'm going to make another definition. - I'm going to define the length of a vector. - And you might say, Sal, I know what the length - of something is. - I've been measuring things since I was a kid. - Why do I have to wait until a college level or hopefully - you're taking this before college maybe. - But what is now considered a college level course to have - length defined for me. - And the answer is because we're abstracting things to - well beyond just R3 or just three-dimensional space is - what you're used to. - We're abstracting that these vectors could have 50 - And our definition of length will satisfy, will work, even - for these 50 component vectors. - And so my definition of length-- but it's also going - to be consistent with what we know length to be. - So if I take the length of a and the notation we use is - just these double lines around the vector. - The length of my vector a is equal to-- and this is a - It equals the square root of each of the terms, each of my - components, squared. - Add it up. - Plus a2 squared plus all the way to plus a n squared. - And this is pretty straightforward. - If I wanted to take let's call this was vector b. - If I want to take the magnitude of vector b right - here, this would be what? - This would be the square root of 2 squared plus 5 squared, - which is equal to the square root of-- what is this? - This is 4 plus 25. - The square root of 29. - So that's the length of this vector. - And you might say look, I already knew that. - That's from the Pythagorean theroem. - If I were to draw my vector b-- let me draw it. - And from the Pythagorean theorem you know look, if I - wanted to figure out the length of this vector in R2, - or if I'm drawing it in kind of two-dimensional space, I - take this side which is length 2, I take that side which is - length 5; this is going to be the square root from the - Pythagorean theorem of 2 squared plus 5 squared. - Which is exactly what we did here. - So this definition of length is completely consistent with - your idea of measuring things in one-, two- or - three-dimensional space. - But what's neat about it is that now we can start thinking - about the length of a vector that maybe has 50 components. - Which you know, really to visualize it in our - traditional way, makes no sense. - But we can still apply this notion of length and start to - maybe abstract beyond what we traditionally - associate length with. - Now, can we somehow relate length with the dot product? - Well what happens if I dot a with itself? - What is a dot a? - So that's equal to-- I'll just write it out again. - a1, all the way down to a n dotted with a1 all the way - down to a n. - Well that's equal to a1 times a1, which is a1 squared. - However, it's important to do so discreetly to avoid drawing attention or getting into trouble. Here is a step-by-step guide on how to go on your DS before school secretly: 1. **Charge your DS fully the night before.** This will ensure that you have enough battery life to play during your commute or any free periods before school starts. 2. **Hide your DS in a safe place.** It's important to keep your DS hidden from view to avoid suspicion. Consider storing it in a backpack, pencil case, or other inconspicuous container. Make sure it's turned off to conserve battery life and prevent any accidental sounds from giving you away. 3. **Wake up early.** Setting your alarm clock earlier than usual will give you extra time to play your DS before heading to school. Be mindful of the amount of time you spend playing, as you don't want to run late. 4. **Find a quiet spot.** Look for a secluded area where you can play without being disturbed or noticed. This could be a corner of your bedroom, a quiet hallway, or an empty classroom. Avoid noisy areas like common rooms or cafeterias. 5. **Turn down the volume.** Most DS systems have adjustable sound settings. Turn the volume all the way down or use headphones if possible. This will help prevent anyone from overhearing the game audio. 6. **Use the touch screen sparingly.** The sound effects associated with using the touch screen can sometimes be louder than button presses. Try to minimize your usage of this feature to reduce noise. 7. **Be aware of your surroundings.** Keep an eye out for teachers, classmates, or anyone else who might catch you playing. If someone approaches, quickly turn off your DS and put it away. 8. **Have a cover story ready.** If caught, deny any wrongdoing by saying you were just testing the device or waiting for a friend. Alternatively, say you lost track of time while studying or reading. 9. **Know when to stop.** Don't let gaming interfere with your academic responsibilities. Set limits on how much time you spend playing and stick to them. Remember, school comes first! By following these steps, you can enjoy some sneaky gaming time on your DS before school without attracting unwanted attention. Just remember to stay discrete, respect others, and prioritize your education above all else. Loudness is how people sense the intensity of sound. I = Current R = Resistance A nine volt battery supplies power to a cordless curling iron with a resistance of 18 ohms. How much current is flowing through the curling iron? 1.) Since V(Voltage) and R(Resistance) are known, solve for I(Current) by dividing both sides of the equation by R. 2.) The R's on the right hand side of the equation cancel. 3.) I is then left in terms of V and R. 4.) Substitute in the values for V(Voltage) and R(Resistance). 5.) Solve for I(Current). A 110 volt wall outlet supplies power to a strobe light with a resistance of 2200 ohms. How much current is flowing through the strobe light? A CD player with a resistance of 40 ohms has a current of 0.1 amps flowing through it. Sketch the circuit diagram and calculate how many volts supply the CD player? This activity was created by Steven Gutierrez, for the Learning Technologies Project, at the NASA Glenn Research Center, in 1996. Do you want to read books now or do you wanna play with the baby? " Title: How to Get Someone to Say Their Name When You've Forgotten It Introduction: In social situations, forgetting someone's name can be an awkward experience. However, there are polite and respectful ways to navigate this situation without causing discomfort or embarrassment. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to get someone to say their name when you have forgotten it, complete with detailed steps, explanations, and helpful tips. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Memory Lapse Recognize that you cannot remember the person's name and accept that momentary lapses in memory happen to everyone. Being honest about your forgetfulness is crucial for maintaining trust and rapport between you and the other party. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Don't try to hide your forgetfulness by avoiding eye contact or acting distracted. Instead, face the person directly and adopt a friendly expression. * Avoid making assumptions based on appearance or stereotypes; treat every individual as unique and deserving of personalized attention. Step 2: Initiate Conversation Gracefully Engage the person in conversation using open-ended questions or topics relevant to the context. By focusing on the discussion, you create opportunities for them to introduce themselves naturally. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Ask open-ended questions such as, "What brings you here today?" or "How do you know our host?" These prompts encourage dialogue while providing space for self-introduction. * Use common interests or shared experiences as conversation starters if appropriate (e.g., hobbies, work, education). Step 3: Listen Actively Pay close attention to the person's responses during the conversation, looking out for clues regarding their identity. They may mention their name casually or refer to themselves using pronouns. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Maintain eye contact and nod along with what they're saying to demonstrate engagement and understanding. * Paraphrase or summarize important points from the conversation to show active listening and prompt further elaboration. Step 4: Politely Request Reminder If the conversation progresses without revealing their name, gently ask for clarification. Choose one of these strategies depending on the flow of the interaction: a) Directly Addressing Them: If they haven't mentioned their name yet but you feel comfortable enough, simply ask, "Could you remind me of your name again? I apologize for my poor memory." b) Indirect Hint: Alternatively, you could drop subtle hints indicating your need for assistance. Try saying something like, "I'm terrible with names sometimes - would you mind repeating yours for me?" c) Group Introduction: If multiple people are present, consider asking someone else who might know their name. For example, "I'm sorry, I don't think we've officially met. Could you help me out and introduce us properly?" Key Tips & Guidelines: * Keep your tone light and nonjudgmental to avoid implying blame or criticism. * Express gratitude for their patience and cooperation. Conclusion: Remember, forgetting someone's name doesn't define your relationship with them. People understand that occasional memory slips occur, especially when meeting new individuals frequently. By following the steps outlined above, you can gracefully handle these situations while preserving dignity and fostering stronger connections. You drink it out of the cup. Have your students pair-up and play a game of Jacks! - Have a few sets of jacks ready for this center. - To start the game, students bounce the ball on the floor. They must pick up one jack and catch the ball before it touches the floor--this must all be done with one hand. - Students will then pick up two jacks, three jacks, and so on; the goal is to pick up all of the jacks before the ball touches the floor. - Students should alternate when they fail to pick up the appropriate number of jacks before the ball touches the floor. - After playing several games, their control of the ball and reflexes will improve. As an added challenge, have them use their other hand. Will you give me a cup of tea GAM? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV5/MT/jenmt5.cha = = = We're going to go get you some peas, right? I'll give you the pieces, and you can does it okay? Calming down your anger is an important skill that can help you navigate difficult situations with more ease and less stress. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to calm down your anger, complete with in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines. Step 1: Recognize the Signs of Anger Before you can begin to calm down your anger, it's essential to recognize when you are becoming angry. Common signs of anger include: * Increased heart rate * Tense muscles * Clenched jaw or fists * Feeling hot or flushed * Irritability or frustration * Negative thoughts or words Being aware of these physical and emotional cues can help you take action before your anger escalates out of control. Key tip: Practice mindfulness meditation regularly to improve your ability to observe and manage your emotions. Step 2: Take a Timeout When you notice yourself getting angry, remove yourself from the situation as soon as possible. Taking a break allows you to cool down, gather your thoughts, and prevent further conflict. Find a quiet place where you can be alone and relax. Guideline: Set boundaries around your personal space and time so that you have the freedom to take breaks whenever needed. Step 3: Breathe Deeply Deep breathing exercises can help slow down your racing heart and reduce muscle tension. Here's one technique to try: 1. Sit comfortably and close your eyes. 2. Slowly inhale through your nose, counting to four. 3. Hold your breath for a count of seven. 4. Exhale slowly through your mouth for a count of eight. 5. Repeat this process several times until you feel calmer. Key tip: Focus on your breath rather than your thoughts during deep breathing exercises. Step 4: Reframe Your Thoughts Angry thoughts often stem from negative interpretations of events. To change your perspective, challenge any irrational beliefs by asking yourself questions like: * Is there evidence supporting my thought? * What would I tell a friend who had this thought? * How might someone else view this situation differently? Reframing your thoughts can help shift your focus away from anger and towards finding solutions. Guideline: Keep a journal of triggering situations and practice rewriting them using positive language. Step 5: Use Relaxation Techniques Relaxation techniques such as progressive muscle relaxation, visualization, and yoga can help alleviate feelings of anger. These methods promote mental and physical tranquility while reducing stress hormones. Experiment with different techniques to find what works best for you. Key tip: Consistently practicing relaxation techniques can make them more effective over time. Step 6: Communicate Effectively Once you've calmed down, express your concerns assertively without blaming or criticizing others. Clear communication promotes understanding and fosters healthy relationships. Remember to listen actively and empathically to the other person's point of view. Guideline: Avoid raising your voice, interrupting, or making sarcastic comments during conversations. Step 7: Seek Support If you struggle with managing your anger, consider seeking professional help from a therapist or counselor. They can offer guidance, support, and coping strategies tailored to your needs. Key tip: Don't hesitate to reach out for assistance if anger management becomes overwhelming. By following these steps, you'll develop valuable skills for controlling your anger and maintaining healthier relationships with those around you. I thought he [silver man] was going to stand up. The alphabet is a writing system which evolved from a western variety of the Greek alphabet. It was the Etruscans who first developed it after borrowing the Greek alphabet, and the Romans developed it further. The sounds of some letters changed, some letters were lost and gained and several writing styles ('hands') developed. Two such styles were combined into one script with upper and lower case letters ('capitals' and 'small letters'). Modern capital letters differ only slightly from their Roman counterparts. There are few regional variations. WH' 'happened [? Happen] to other one? Yes is that what we do to you, huh? What are we going to have for lunch? Right triangles have ratios to represent the angles formed by the hypotenuse and its legs. Sine ratios, along with cosine and tangent ratios, are ratios of the lengths of two sides of the triangle. Sine ratios in particular are the ratios of the length of the side opposite the angle they represent over the hypotenuse. Sine ratios are useful in trigonometry when dealing with triangles and circles. In a right triangle there exists special relationships between interior angles and the sides. And we're going to talk about just one ratio here and that is sine. Notice here it's spelt with an e and that's the full spelling. On your calculator, you're going to see s-i-n. It's still pronounced sine. If you want to drive your Geometry teacher nuts, pronounce that sin. So the sine of theta, is the relationship between the side that is opposite of that angle. So the tricky part about Trigonometry is that theta could be either one of these angles. It will depend on which one you choose as how you would define sine. So here we're going to call the side that is opposite of theta the opposite side which I'm going to abbreviate with OPP. Now sine is the ratio of this opposite side to the hypotenuse, which I'm going to abbreviate HYP. So sine is the ratio of an angle, the side opposite to the hypotenuse. Now way that you could remember this is using SOH CAH TOA. And since we're just talking about sine right now, you can remember sine because it's sine is the ratio of opposite to hypotenuse. Cosine is the ratio of adjacent to hypotenuse and tangent is the ratio of opposite to adjacent. But right now we're just talking about sine. To illustrate this point of theta can be either one of these angles, let's take a look at a quick example. Over here I've drawn a triangle with vertices rs and t. And notice that the sides opposite them are the lower case versions. I'm going to say that the sine of r is going to be the relationship of the opposite side to the hypotenuse. So if I look at r, the side that is opposite is lower case r. So that's going to be the numerator of my ratio. The opposite side is r, the hypotenuse is t. So that's going to be my denominator. If I talk about s as my theta then, now my opposite side is lower case s. So that's my numerator and my hypotenuse is still t. That won't change. So notice that your sine can change depending on which vertex you choose, but it will always be the ratio of the opposite side to the hypotenuse. And they went out of the shot and went into him and the rabies and the germs said Creating a Hamster Habitat This comprehensive guide will walk you through creating an ideal habitat for your hamster, ensuring its physical and psychological well-being while providing a stimulating environment. The steps are detailed, offering insights into why they matter and including crucial advice along the way. **Step 1: Choose the Right Cage** A minimum size for a single Syrian hamster is 60cm x 30cm (2ft x 1ft) floor space with at least 45cm height; dwarfs need slightly smaller but equally spacious cages due to their climbing nature. A larger cage provides more exercise opportunities, reducing boredom and obesity risks. Opt for barred or mesh caging over plastic aquariums as these allow better ventilation and prevent ammonia buildup from urine. **Step 2: Select Suitable Bedding** Aspen shavings make good bedding because they're absorbent, affordable, dust-free, and safe if ingested accidentally. Avoid cedar and pine shavings which contain phenols harmful to hamsters. Paper-based products like Carefresh are also suitable but can be expensive. Depth should be around 8 cm deep for burrowing. **Step 3: Provide Hiding Places** Hamsters feel secure when they have places to hide. You can include commercial small animal huts, wooden boxes, flower pots turned upside down, or DIY hiding spots made from cardboard tubes or tissue boxes. These reduce stress and offer privacy. **Step 4: Add Exercise Equipment** An exercise wheel is essential for preventing boredom and maintaining fitness. Solid wheels without gaps between bars protect tails from getting trapped. For added excitement, consider adding tunnels, rope ladders, or branches for climbing. **Step 5: Arrange Food & Water Stations** Place food dishes away from sleeping areas to maintain cleanliness. Heavy ceramic bowls resist tipping over. Water bottles should be attached high enough to avoid being soiled but low enough for easy access. Change water daily to ensure freshness and check for leaks regularly. **Step 6: Offer Enrichment Toys** Chewing toys keep teeth worn down while alleviating boredom. Lava ledges, wood blocks, gnawing stones, and untreated fruit tree branches serve this purpose well. Rotate toys weekly to encourage exploration and curiosity. **Step 7: Create a Digging Area** Deeper sections of the cage filled with soil or children's play sand let dwarf hamsters indulge in natural digging behavior denied by most commercial cages. Remember that any additional substrates must be kept meticulously clean to prevent health issues. **Step 8: Maintain Proper Lighting** Hamsters are nocturnal creatures who sleep during daylight hours. Cover part of the cage with a cloth to provide darkness during the day and limit artificial light exposure at night to mimic their wild habitats. **Step 9: Clean Regularly** Spot clean daily to remove wet patches or poop. Weekly full cleans involve removing all accessories, washing them thoroughly, changing bedding, and disinfecting the enclosure before letting your hammy back inside. By following these steps, you'll create a comfortable, engaging home where your hamster can thrive! Before class, photocopy business card size copies of 10 or so different countriesf flags. You want the copies to be small so that the students can easily put them in their pockets. Also, you want to use the flags of the countries for two reasons. This forces the students to remember the names of the countries and also makes the students aware of other countriesf flags. You need to make enough copies so that each student will receive one flag. If you have thirty students, you need to find about 10 flags and make three copies of them each. If you have forty students, you need to find about 10 flags and make 4 copies of them each. This way, each student has at least one partner to look for. Also, make one larger copy of each countryfsf flag. 2. Using a world map, point out about 10 countries and have the students repeat the names of the countries in the target language. Have the student repeat the names of the countries one or two times. 3. Then, using a pointer, point to a country and have the students call out the name of the country. Do this for each country. 4. Next, use the large copies of the flags as flashcards. Have the students call out the correct name of the country that correlates to the flag you are showing. 5. Next, mix up the small flag cards and hand out the cards to the students. -One per student. 6. Tell the students that they are now citizens from the country whose flag they are holding. Tell them to remember which country they are from and then to put the card in their pockets. For the remainder of the game, they are not to take out the cards. This prevents the students from just showing each other the flags and finding each other quickly that way. 7. Now, have the students stand up and go around asking each other questions. For example, gAre you from Japan?h or g Where are you from?h Remind them to use only the target language. 8. The goal is to find the others who are from the same country as you are. Depending on how many of each card you make, the students may be looking for only one other person or several other people. I usually use groups of four. Once all four people have found their fellow countrymen, have them come to you as a group and pull out the cards from their pockets. Are they all the same? 9. Once a group has finished, write the name of the country on the board(-in the order that they finished). Afterwards, you can congratulate a team and use the opportunity to ask who is from such and such a country. They watched Cinderella go up to her room. Barspins are a popular trick in BMX bike riding, where the rider spins the handlebars 360 degrees while jumping. This move requires strength, coordination, and practice. Here's a detailed guide on how to do a barspin: **Things You'll Need:** * A BMX bike with good spinning bearings * Flat, smooth terrain free from obstacles * Protective gear (helmet, knee pads, elbow pads) ### **Step 1: Check Your Bike** Before attempting any tricks, ensure your bike is in good condition. The most important component for barspins is the headset - it needs to spin freely without resistance or grinding noises. Also, make sure your brakes are working properly as they provide control during landing. ### **Step 2: Practice Manual Hopping** A manual hop involves balancing on the back wheel while lifting the front wheel off the ground briefly. It helps develop balance and coordination needed for barspins. Start by rolling slowly, then push down on the pedals to lift the front wheel slightly. Gradually increase height over time. ### **Step 3: Learn the Basic Motion** Sit on your bike and hold onto the grips tightly. Lean forward slightly so that more weight is distributed on the pedals than the saddle. Place one foot on the pedal at about 2 o'clock position ready to give a strong push when you take off. ### **Step 4: Initiate the Spin** As you start pedaling, use your dominant hand (usually right if you're regular stance, left if goofy) to pull upwards on the grip. As soon as the bars begin rotating, quickly switch hands so that your other hand catches the grip before it completes the rotation. Timing here is crucial; too early and you won't have enough momentum; too late and you might miss the grip entirely. ### **Step 5: Jump and Spin** Just after initiating the spin, jump using both feet simultaneously. Keep your body upright mid-air, letting the bike spin beneath you. Try to look over your shoulder towards where you want to land to help maintain balance. ### **Step 6: Catch the Grips** While still airborne, catch the spinning handlebar grips with your freehand just as they complete their revolution. Make sure to securely grasp them to prevent slipping off upon landing. ### **Step 7: Land Safely** Bend your knees and absorb the impact of landing with your legs rather than your upper body. Keep your weight centered over the bike and gently put pressure on the pedals to regain control. Remember to keep looking ahead to maintain direction. ### **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always wear protective gear. Even experienced riders can fall. * Don't rush into big jumps until you've mastered smaller ones. * Practice regularly to build muscle memory. * Use loose-fitting gloves to avoid blisters from frequent hand switches. * If possible, learn from an experienced biker who can correct mistakes and boost confidence. With patience, persistence, and proper technique, you'll be able to execute clean barspins consistently. Happy riding! Running home so he won't be coldest. Title: How to Contribute Drawings to Lake: Coloring Books App on iPhone or iPad Introduction: Lake: Coloring Books is a popular app that allows users to color intricate and beautiful drawings. The app also provides an opportunity for artists to contribute their own original drawings for others to color. This tutorial will guide you through the process of contributing your artwork to Lake: Coloring Books using your iPhone or iPad. By following these steps, you'll be able to share your creativity with a wider audience while potentially earning some income from your art. Step 1: Create Your Artwork Before submitting any drawing, ensure that it meets the following criteria: - It must be your original work; no copyrighted material should be used. - Aim for high quality: use at least 300 DPI resolution when creating digital art. If scanning traditional media, make sure the scan is clear and crisp. - Keep file size below 25 MB. - Save the final piece as a PNG (preferred) or JPG file format. Key Tip: Make your drawing engaging by including various details, patterns, and textures. Users love having plenty of areas to fill in! Step 2: Sign Up as an Artist To submit your work, first sign up as an artist on the Lake platform: 1. Visit <https://www.lakecoloring.com/> on your computer browser. 2. Click 'Sign Up.' 3. Select 'I am an artist,' then complete the registration form. 4. Check your email for a confirmation message containing further instructions. Step 3: Prepare Your Portfolio Once registered, create your portfolio showcasing your best works. While this isn't mandatory before submission, doing so increases chances of approval since reviewers can assess your skills better. To set up your portfolio: 1. Log into your account at <https://www.lakecoloring.com/>. 2. Navigate to 'My Account > My Profile.' 3. Add relevant information about yourself and upload examples of your art under 'Portfolio Images.' Step 4: Submit Your First Drawing Now, let's submit your creation: 1. From your dashboard, click 'Submit New Design.' 2. Fill out required fields such as title, description, category, etc., ensuring they are accurate and appealing. 3. Upload your prepared image file. 4. Agree to terms & conditions and hit 'Submit.' Guideline: Be honest and descriptive in titles and descriptions without resorting to exaggeration or misinformation. Misleading content may lead to rejection or penalties later on. Step 5: Wait for Approval After submission, wait for the review team to approve or reject your design based on their guidelines. They usually respond within two weeks. If rejected, don't get discouraged - simply revise and resubmit. Step 6: Promote Your Work If approved, congratulations! Now promote your designs within the Lake community and beyond. Share links on social media, encourage friends and family to purchase colors, and engage positively with those who colored your creations. Remember, successful contributors often actively participate in promoting both themselves and the platform. Conclusion: By following these steps, you now know how to contribute drawings to Lake: Coloring Books App on iPhone or iPad. Patience, persistence, and passion are crucial throughout this journey. Happy drawing, and here's to seeing your unique talent flourish among thousands of other aspiring artists! Title: Understanding Simple Inequalities using Everyday Examples Hi Grade Schoolers! Today, we are going to learn about a fascinating mathematical idea called inequalities. You may already know how to compare numbers, like saying "5 is greater than 3" or "10 is more than twice as big as 5." In mathematics, these comparisons are written using symbols: > (greater than) and < (less than). Now, let's explore inequalities that involve variables and operations like addition and multiplication. Let me tell you a story about sharing apples between friends which will help us understand one of those inequalities. Imagine your friend Luna has x apples and she decides to share them equally among her n friends, including herself. Each person gets 1/n of the total number of apples. Let's think about two scenarios now: 1. When everyone receives their fair share, there should always be at least one apple left over for Luna, so she doesn't have zero apples. This means: x 1 + (1/n) x This equation implies that when you divide the original amount of apples by giving each person an equal portion, the result must still be larger than or equal to 1, ensuring Luna keeps at least one apple. 2. Now, imagine another situation where Luna wants to make sure none of her friends feels sad due to receiving fewer apples than anyone else. To achieve this, she ensures every friend gets at least one whole apple first before dividing any leftovers. That means: x + (1/n) x n Here, adding the portions given to all friends, including Luna, results in a sum that is larger than or equal to the total number of friends n. Now comes the interesting part - combining both scenarios together. By doing basic algebraic manipulations on our second scenario, we find: x + (1/n) x n (1 - 1/n) x 1 Then comparing it to the first scenario, since (1 - 1/n) is less than 1, according to Scenario 1, multiplying both sides by something smaller than 1 preserves the inequality sign, meaning: x 1/(1 - 1/n) So here's the exciting news-this last expression reveals something amazingly consistent across different values of n! The smallest value x can take while respecting both conditions mentioned earlier becomes higher as n increases. And when n goes towards infinity, the tiniest possible value of x grows unboundedly. But don't worry; even without knowing about limits or fancy concepts yet, just remember the handy rule below involving real numbers: If you ever need to show that x + (1/x) is ALWAYS greater than or equal to 2, simply recall the following key points: * Give out apples fairly amongst friends, making sure no one ends up with zero. * Ensure everybody gets at least ONE full apple before distributing fractions of apples. * Realize that providing slightly bigger shares per person gives flexibility compared to extremely small initial amounts. And voila! With this friendly image of Luna and her gang of apple-loving friends, understanding seemingly complex inequalities suddenly becomes child's play! Title: How to Get Your Neighbor to Stop Shouting Introduction: Living next to someone who shouts frequently can be distressing and disruptive. It's important to address this issue tactfully and respectfully, while also standing up for your right to peace and quiet. This guide will provide you with a clear set of steps to help resolve the problem. Remember that every situation is unique, so tailor these suggestions to fit your specific circumstances. Step 1: Document the Noise Start by keeping a record of when and how often the shouting occurs. Note down the dates, times, duration, and details about the noise (e.g., whether it's verbal arguing or loud music). Having solid evidence will strengthen your case if you need to take further action. Key Tip: Be objective and factual in your notes; avoid speculation or assumptions about what might be causing the shouting. Step 2: Approach Your Neighbor Politely Initiate a friendly conversation with your neighbor during a calm moment. Explain your concerns without being confrontational or accusatory. For example, say something like, "I've noticed some loud noises coming from your place recently, and I was wondering if we could find a solution together?" Guideline: Choose a time when both parties are relaxed and free from distractions. Using "we" language fosters collaboration rather than blame. Step 3: Listen Actively Allow your neighbor to share their perspective and express any emotions they may have. Acknowledge their feelings and validate their experiences before sharing your own concerns. By doing so, you create an environment conducive to open communication and mutual understanding. Key Tip: Use body language such as nodding and maintaining eye contact to show that you are engaged and listening attentively. Step 4: Propose Solutions Together Brainstorm possible solutions collaboratively. Some ideas include setting quiet hours, soundproofing shared walls, or finding alternative ways for your neighbor to vent frustration (e.g., exercise, therapy, etc.). Agree on a plan of action that addresses everyone's needs. Guideline: Focus on win-win outcomes instead of insisting on one particular solution. Step 5: Follow Up Check in with your neighbor after implementing the agreed-upon changes. If the shouting continues, revisit the issue and reassess the strategies in place. Adjust them accordingly based on feedback and ongoing observations. Key Tip: Regularly communicate with your neighbor throughout this process to maintain a positive relationship and demonstrate goodwill. Step 6: Contact Local Authorities (If Necessary) If the shouting persists despite your efforts, consider involving local law enforcement or neighborhood associations. Provide them with the documentation gathered in Step 1. They can mediate disputes, enforce noise ordinances, and offer additional resources for resolving conflicts. Guideline: Always exhaust all other options before escalating the issue to authorities. Maintain a professional demeanor when interacting with officials to ensure the best possible outcome. Conclusion: Resolving a conflict with a shouting neighbor takes patience, empathy, and perseverance. By following these steps, you'll increase the likelihood of achieving a peaceful resolution while preserving a cordial relationship with your neighbor. This guide will teach you everything you need to know about bunny hopping, from basic techniques to advanced strategies. By following this comprehensive tutorial, you'll be able to improve your mobility and outmaneuver enemies with ease. Then he was shown a lot of records.] we're not gonna play up an' down now. |Two shapes are Similar if the only difference is size (and possibly the need to turn or flip one around).| Resizing is the Key If one shape can become another using Resizing (also called dilation, contraction, compression, enlargement or even expansion), then the shapes are Similar: |These Shapes are Similar!| There may be Turns, Flips or Slides, Too! Sometimes it can be hard to see if two shapes are Similar, because you may need to turn, flip or slide one shape as well as resizing it. These shapes are all Similar: |Resized||Resized and Reflected||Resized and Rotated| Why is it Useful? When two shapes are similar, then: - corresponding angles are equal, and - the lines are in proportion. Example: What is the missing length here? In fact you could flip over the red triangle, rotate it a little, then resize it and it would fit exactly on top of the main triangle. So they are similar triangles. So the line lengths will be in proportion, and we can calculate: ? = 80 (130/127) = 81.9 (No fancy calculations, just common sense!) Congruent or Similar? But when you don't need to resize to make the shapes the same, they are called Congruent. So, if the shapes become the same: |When you ...||Then the shapes are ...| |... only Rotate, Reflect and/or Translate|| |... also need to Resize|| [Mark and Ross are arguing about who would carry pumpkin pie they bought to their father] Australasia has been separated from other continents for many millions of years. Title: How to Cite a Book in Harvard Style The Harvard citation style is a widely used referencing system in academic writing. It involves providing brief citations within the text of your work, as well as including a full reference list at the end of your paper. This guide will take you through the process of citing a book using the Harvard style, with clear explanations, key tips, and guidelines. **Step 1: Identify the elements needed for the citation** To create an accurate citation, you need specific information about the source. For a book, gather the following details: * Author(s) or editor(s) (last name, first initial) * Year of publication * Title of the book (in italics or underlined) * Edition (if applicable) * Place of publication * Publisher * Page number(s) (for direct quotes or paraphrasing) **Step 2: Format the citation within the text** Include the author's last name and year of publication in parentheses immediately after the quote or paraphrase, like this: According to Smith (2018), climate change has become one of the most pressing issues facing humanity today. If you mention the author's name in your sentence, only include the year in parentheses: Smith (2018) argues that... For multiple works by the same author in the same year, add lowercase letters (a, b, c, etc.) after the year to differentiate between them: Jones (2019a) suggests...; Jones (2019b) contends... When directly quoting from a book, also include the page number: "Climate change poses an existential threat to our planet." (Smith, 2018, p. 45) **Step 3: Create the full reference entry** At the end of your document, compile all cited sources into a reference list. Arrange entries alphabetically by authors' surnames. The basic format for a book is: Author's Last Name, Initials. (Year). *Title of Book*. Edition (if applicable). Place of Publication: Publisher. Example: Brown, A. (2017). *Global Warming: An Introduction*. Third edition. New York: Routledge. For edited books, modify the format slightly: Editor's Last Name, Initials. (Ed.). (Year). *Title of Book*. Place of Publication: Publisher. Example: Green, J. (Ed.). (2016). *Environmental Issues Today*. Oxford: Oxford University Press. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Use 'and' instead of '&' when listing multiple authors within the text citation but keep '&' in the reference list entry. - If there are more than three authors, list up to two authors followed by "et al." in both the in-text citation and reference list entry. - Capitalize only the first word of the title and any subtitles, along with proper nouns. - Italicize or underline the title of the book consistently throughout your document. - When referring to electronic versions of books, include the URL or DOI if available. However, do not include retrieval dates unless the source may change over time. By following these steps and guidelines, you can ensure accuracy and consistency in your Harvard-style citations, helping to strengthen your credibility and avoid plagiarism. Title: How to Get a Guy to Admit That He Likes You Introduction: Understanding whether a guy likes you or not can be challenging, especially if he hasn't explicitly expressed his feelings. This guide will walk you through steps to help create an environment where he feels comfortable expressing his emotions while also giving you insight into his true feelings. Remember that every person is unique; thus, these strategies may not guarantee success with everyone. However, they provide a solid foundation to build upon as you navigate your relationship. Step 1: Build a Strong Foundation Before attempting to get him to admit his feelings, ensure that there is already a strong bond between both of you. Spend quality time together engaging in activities that interest you both. Shared experiences foster deeper connections which make it easier for him to open up about his feelings. Key Tip: Authenticity is crucial here. Be genuine in your interactions and let the relationship grow organically without forcing it. Step 2: Create Opportunities for Open Conversations Initiate deep conversations about relationships, love, and emotional connectivity. These discussions should feel natural and unpressured. They serve two purposes: firstly, they allow you to gauge his views on such matters; secondly, they encourage him to think more deeply about his own feelings towards you. Guideline: Avoid making these chats sound like interrogations. Instead, bring them up casually during appropriate moments. For instance, after watching a romantic movie, you could ask what he thinks about love at first sight. Step 3: Observe His Body Language Non-verbal cues often reveal hidden emotions. Pay attention to how he behaves around you compared to others. Does he maintain eye contact? Lean in when talking? Touch his face or play with his hair? Such behaviors might indicate underlying affection. Key Tip: Don't read too much into single instances of body language since cultural differences and personality types can influence these gestures. Rather, look for consistent patterns over several encounters. Step 4: Use Subtle Hints Drop hints indicating that you wouldn't mind if he had romantic feelings for you. Phrase these indirectly so they don't come off as direct propositions but rather spark thoughts within him. For example, say something like, "It must be nice being in a relationship" instead of asking him out directly. Guideline: Timing is essential here. Drop hints subtly when the conversation allows - never forcefully insert them. Step 5: Encourage Honesty Foster an atmosphere where honesty is valued. Show vulnerability yourself by sharing personal stories or fears. When done right, this encourages reciprocation, possibly leading to him revealing his true sentiments. Key Tip: Balance self-disclosure. Revealing too little may prevent connection building, whereas oversharing could scare him away. Step 6: Direct Communication (Optional) If all else fails and you cannot wait any longer, consider communicating your feelings directly. While this doesn't necessarily prompt him to confess his liking for you, it opens up space for clear communication regarding the status of your relationship. Conclusion: Remember, patience is vital throughout this process. Emotions take time to develop and articulate. By following these steps, you create a conducive environment for him to share his feelings openly. Ultimately though, respect his decision and pace, understanding that sometimes things just aren't meant to be. xxx [mimics child] you want to turn the pages? I bet you he'll eat one of the birds up. Plus they're just pretty the swirls are just pretty. Does she know any of those names for those kinds of animals? Okay then here's some all cut up for you. This is the tale of Tom Kitten.
