Under the International System of Units, the second is currently defined as the duration of 9,192,631,770 periods of the radiation corresponding to the transition between the two hyperfine levels of the ground state of the caesium-133 atom. This definition refers to a caesium atom at rest at a temperature of (absolute zero). The ground state is defined at zero magnetic field. The second thus defined is equivalent to the ephemeris second. [Nina yells and bounces the plastic dog around.] This Asian cow elephant lives in Nepal a small country north of India. Saturn's rotational period is calculated by the rotation speed of radio waves released by the planet. The Cassini-Huygens spacecraft discovered that the radio waves slowed down, suggesting that the rotational period increased. Science Main Index There are about 10,000 different species of crab. The crab is native to all of the world's oceans. There are also freshwater crabs, and even some crabs that live on land. Crabs have a large, hard shell. Extending from the front of its shell are the eyes, mouth and two pairs of antennae. The crab has 5 pairs of legs extending from the side of its shell. The first pair of legs have claws or pincers used to catch and hold food. The other pairs of legs are used for walking. Most crabs don't swim, they use their legs to walk. However, some crabs such as the Blue Crab can use their legs as paddles to swim. Play the following videos to learn more about the crab. yeah, and he didn't even height take a pitcher or something. Title: How to Add a Member to a Limited Liability Company (LLC) Introduction: Limited Liability Companies (LLCs) are popular business structures due to their flexibility and limited liability protection. As your business grows, you may want to add new members to your LLC. This guide will walk you through the process of adding a member to an existing LLC. **Important note:** Laws governing LLCs vary by state. Always consult with a legal professional or your Secretary of State's office to ensure compliance with local regulations. Step 1: Review Your Operating Agreement Before making any changes to your LLC, review your operating agreement. The operating agreement is a document that outlines the management structure, ownership percentages, profit distribution methods, and other important aspects of your LLC. Check if there are specific procedures for adding new members. If so, follow those steps closely. Key tip: It's crucial to have a well-drafted operating agreement before adding a new member to avoid potential conflicts or misunderstandings. Consult a lawyer if necessary. Step 2: Obtain Consent from Current Members Most states require unanimous consent from all current members when adding a new member. To obtain this consent, prepare a written resolution stating your intention to admit a new member, along with details such as membership percentage, contribution, and share of profits and losses. Have every current member sign the resolution. Guideline: Make sure the signed resolution includes the date and clearly indicates approval from all members regarding the addition of a new member. Step 3: Prepare a New Membership Interest Certificate Once the current members approve the new membership, create a certificate that outlines the terms of the new member's interest in the company. Information should include: * Name of the LLC * Name of the new member * Date of admission * Percentage of membership interest * Any special rights or restrictions related to the membership interest Have both the new member and at least one current member sign the certificate. Step 4: Update the LLC's Records Update your LLC records to reflect the change in membership. Revise the following documents accordingly: * Operating agreement * Membership ledger * List of members maintained by the Secretary of State (if required by your state) Keep these updated records in your LLC's official minute book or file them electronically according to your state's requirements. Step 5: Notify Relevant Parties Inform relevant parties about the new member, including: * Banks and financial institutions where the LLC has accounts * Vendors and suppliers * Clients and customers * Tax authorities, accountants, and attorneys Updating these contacts ensures smooth continuation of operations and proper tax filing processes. Step 6: Issue IRS Form 1099-MISC (If Applicable) If the new member contributes cash or property worth $600 or more during the tax year, issue IRS Form 1099-MISC to report the transaction. File copies with the IRS and provide the form to the new member by January 31st of the following year. Key tip: Keep accurate records of contributions made by the new member to facilitate future tax filings and audits. Conclusion: By carefully following these six steps, you can successfully add a new member to your LLC while maintaining legal compliance and avoiding unnecessary complications. Regularly updating your LLC's records, keeping open communication channels among members, and seeking expert advice when needed contribute to the success and longevity of your business. Assessing statistical significance is an essential skill in data analysis that allows you to determine whether your observations could have occurred by chance or if they are genuinely meaningful. This tutorial will guide you through the process of assessing statistical significance using a hypothetical example: comparing test scores between two teaching methods. **Step 1: Define Your Research Question** The first step in any data analysis project is defining your research question. Here's our question: Is there a significant difference in students' average test scores when taught with Method A versus Method B? **Step 2: Collect Data** Next, collect relevant data from a sample of participants (students) who have experienced both teaching methods. Record their respective test scores. Ideally, this should be done randomly to minimize selection bias. For simplicity, let's assume we already have this dataset. **Step 3: Check Assumptions** Before performing a hypothesis test, ensure your data meet certain assumptions. The most common assumption is normality - that your data follows a normal distribution. You can check this visually via histograms or Q-Q plots, or statistically using tests like Shapiro-Wilk. Also, verify homoscedasticity (equal variances) across groups using Levene's Test. **Step 4: Formulate Hypotheses** Now formulate null and alternative hypotheses. Null means no effect; alternatives suggest some effect exists. Our null hypothesis would be "there is no significant difference in mean test scores between the two teaching methods." The alternative would be "there is a significant difference." **Step 5: Choose Appropriate Test** Choose an appropriate statistical test based on your variables and hypotheses. Since we're dealing with one continuous dependent variable (test score) and one categorical independent variable (teaching method), t-tests are suitable here. If we had more than two groups, we'd need ANOVA instead. **Step 6: Perform Calculations** Perform calculations either manually or using software tools like R, Python, SPSS etc. We calculate the t statistic and degrees of freedom (df). Then find p value which indicates probability under null hypothesis. Lower values indicate stronger evidence against the null hypothesis. Manual calculation formulae: t = (M1 - M2) / sqrt[(SD^2/n1 + SD^2/n2)] df = n1 + n2 - 2 where M1 & M2 are means of group 1 and 2 respectively, SD is standard deviation and ni is number of samples in corresponding group. **Step 7: Interpret Results** Compare obtained p-value with chosen level of significance (commonly 0.05). If p < alpha, reject null hypothesis implying observed differences unlikely due to chance, suggesting real impact of teaching method on student performance. Otherwise, fail to reject null hypothesis concluding insufficient evidence to support claim about effectiveness of teaching method over another. Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Always start with clearly defined questions and objectives. 2. Understand your data before testing. Know its shape, variability, and other characteristics. 3. Determine appropriate levels of significance depending upon context and needs. 4. Be cautious interpreting results. Avoid making causal claims unless proven otherwise. 5. Remember that failing to reject null doesn't prove absence of relationship but rather lack of sufficient proof at given significance level. That's a truck and that's a tractor. Fun questions getting students to talk |:: esl resource center :: Activity type: : this activity involves students asking each other personal questions in order to develop fluency. Level: Intermediate students and above - There are a variety of ways that you can get the students to ask the questions which are listed below. - You can dictate the questions i.e. read out some of the questions and get the students to write them down. Once you have done this, ask the students to read the questions out to you. Check that they have written the questions down correctly. - You can stick the questions around the classroom. Tell the students to mingle around the room. When they get to a question, they should stop and ask the nearest student to them the question. - If you want to add novelty value, play music. When you stop the music ask the students to find the question nearest to them on the wall and to discuss it. - Alternatively you can hand out one question per student and ask them to mingle and ask as many people they can in class the question. At the end of the mingle activity, tell the whole class to give feedback on the questions that they asked. NOTE: all of the above ideas are useful for students who feel inhibited when speaking. When mingling, they do not feel so much on the spot and are more likely to speak. - Once the students have mingled, do necessary error corrections. For homework, you can ask the students to write their own answers to the questions that they would like to write about. This will further practice the language that they have used. - What was the happiest event in your life? - What three things could you not live with out? - Who is your best friend? How long have you been friends for? - What would you say was the happiest time in your life? - What achievement are you most proud of? - What is your favorite time of day? - What's your favorite type of weather? - What is your favorite word and why? - What is your favorite sound and why? - Who do you most respect? - Who would you like to change lives with for a day, if you could? - Who do you think you take after in your family? - What is your favorite place and why? - What do you want to achieve in the next 5 years? - What do you like about yourself? Title: Find Out If Your Best Friend Forever (BFF) Is a Liar Introduction: Trust is an essential part of any friendship, especially with your best friend forever (BFF). However, sometimes you might find yourself questioning their honesty. It's crucial to approach this situation delicately as confronting them without evidence can harm your relationship. This wiki-style guide will provide detailed steps to help you discover whether your BFF is lying to you while maintaining trust and respect. Step 1: Observe Their Behavior Key Tips: - Look for changes in body language. - Pay attention to inconsistencies in stories. Explanation: People often reveal deception through subtle cues in their behavior. When someone lies, they may avoid eye contact or exhibit nervous behaviors such as fidgeting. Additionally, listen carefully to what they say; notice if there are contradictions between different versions of the same story. Remember that these signs alone do not prove deceit but should raise suspicion. Step 2: Analyze Conversations Guidelines: - Keep track of conversations related to suspicious topics. - Note down discrepancies in statements over time. Explanation: By documenting interactions about potentially dishonest matters, you create a clear picture of the situation. Discrepancies in statements made at various times could indicate untruthfulness. Again, be cautious about jumping to conclusions based solely on conversational analysis. Step 3: Engage Them in Casual Conversation Key Tips: - Ask open-ended questions. - Use indirect approaches to gather information. Explanation: Instead of directly accusing your BFF of lying, engage them in casual conversation surrounding the topic in question. Open-ended questions encourage detailed responses which can either confirm or alleviate suspicions. Indirect methods like asking mutual friends about the issue might also yield valuable insights. Step 4: Seek Outside Opinions Guidelines: - Consult reliable sources who know both you and your BFF well. - Avoid spreading rumors or gossiping about the situation. Explanation: Sometimes, people outside the immediate circle can offer objective perspectives. They may have observed similar patterns of behavior or noticed something unusual. Respect confidentiality when seeking advice from others, ensuring that you maintain privacy and uphold your BFF's dignity. Step 5: Evaluate Evidence Thoroughly Key Tips: - Consider all possible explanations before drawing conclusions. - Factor in mitigating circumstances that might explain perceived lies. Explanation: Before deciding that your BFF is indeed lying, ensure you've evaluated all available evidence objectively. There could be plausible reasons behind their actions or words. Always leave room for ambiguity until you're absolutely certain. Step 6: Approach Them Gently Guidelines: - Choose a private setting for the discussion. - Express your concerns honestly yet tactfully. Explanation: If after thorough investigation, you still believe your BFF is being dishonest, approach them gently. Select a quiet place where you won't be interrupted. Share your worries politely, emphasizing how much their friendship means to you and expressing hope for resolution. Conclusion: Determining if your BFF is lying requires patience, observation, and diplomacy. While finding out the truth is important, preserving your bond should remain paramount. Follow these steps thoughtfully, giving your friend the benefit of doubt whenever possible. yeah, that's not probably something you should put in your mouth. And he's puttin' his shoe on! You're post copy it down here like you did last night on that birthday card. Drawing Samus Aran from the Metroid video game series can be both challenging and rewarding. This detailed tutorial will guide you through every step of creating an accurate and dynamic drawing of this iconic character. **Step 1: Research and Gather Reference Materials** Before starting your drawing, study various official artworks and screenshots featuring Samus Aran. Title: How to Convert Micrograms to Milligrams Converting measurements from one unit to another is a fundamental skill in many fields, including chemistry, biology, medicine, and cooking. This tutorial will focus on converting micrograms (mcg) to milligrams (mg). By following these steps, you'll be able to perform this conversion confidently and accurately every time. **Step 1:** Understand the Relationship Between Micrograms and Milligrams Milligrams and micrograms are both units used to measure weight or mass, but they belong to different parts of the metric system. A milligram (mg) is equal to 1000 micrograms (mcg), meaning that there are always 1000 micrograms in a single milligram. Thus, when converting from mcg to mg, we need to divide the number of micrograms by 1000. Key tip: Remembering the relationship between these two units as "1 mg = 1000 mcg" can help simplify conversions. **Step 2:** Identify the Number of Micrograms You Want To Convert Before performing any calculations, make sure you have clearly identified the amount of weight expressed in micrograms (mcg) that needs to be converted into milligrams (mg). For example, let us say we want to convert 4500 micrograms to milligrams. **Step 3:** Perform Division To Complete the Conversion Since 1 milligram equals 1000 micrograms, you simply divide the given quantity in micrograms by 1000 to obtain its equivalent value in milligrams. Using our previous example, if we have 4500 micrograms, then dividing this number by 1000 gives us: 4500 mcg / 1000 = 4.5 mg So, 4500 micrograms is equal to 4.5 milligrams. Guideline: When doing manual calculations, remember to include all necessary zeros while retaining precision and accuracy. Use a calculator if needed to avoid errors due to rounding or miscalculation. **Step 4:** Verify Your Calculation After completing your calculation, double-check your work to ensure accuracy. If possible, cross-verify using an online tool or calculator designed specifically for such conversions. Keep practicing until you feel comfortable with the process without needing external verification. Key tip: Always carry out conversions carefully, especially when dealing with medical dosages or scientific experiments where accurate measurement is crucial. In conclusion, understanding how to convert micrograms to milligrams involves knowing their relative values within the metric system and applying simple arithmetic operations like division. With practice, this task becomes second nature, enabling you to handle more complex measurements and applications across various disciplines. Once upon a time there were two friends, Molly and Max. Whenever they went to the store, Molly liked to buy jam. One day, when the two of them went to the store, there was no jam available. "No jam!" yelled Molly in disappointment. "No jam?" asked Max. When he does hum he is simultaneous with Susan.] [mother attaches various felt pieces to the felt board.] [CHI is happily eating her chips watching MOT and SIB do the puzzle] Title: How to Choose Your Stripper or Exotic Dancer Stage Name Introduction: Choosing a stage name is an essential part of being a stripper or exotic dancer. It allows you to maintain your privacy and create a unique persona that can help you stand out in the industry. This guide will walk you through the process of selecting a stage name that suits you, with clear explanations, key tips, and guidelines for each step. Step 1: Brainstorm Ideas ----------------------- Start by brainstorming ideas for your stage name. Consider using a combination of your middle name, a favorite actor's last name, or even a character from a book or movie you love. Reflect on hobbies, interests, or any other aspect of your personality that could inspire a creative and memorable stage name. Remember, this should be fun! Allow yourself time to think freely before narrowing down options. ### Key Tips: * Avoid using your real first name or initial as part of your stage name; maintaining anonymity is crucial in this line of work. * Stay away from names already taken by famous celebrities or well-known strippers. You want something original yet easy to remember. Step 2: Check Availability ------------------------- Once you have a list of potential names, check their availability online and offline. Search social media platforms like Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook to ensure no one else has claimed them. Also, consider searching local club directories or websites to confirm none of the performers there are currently using those names. ### Guidelines: * If someone does share your chosen name, go back to Step 1 and come up with more alternatives. Duplicate names may lead to confusion among clients and colleagues. Step 3: Evaluate Each Option ---------------------------- Look at each option carefully and evaluate its suitability based on several factors such as memorability, uniqueness, and appropriateness. Here's what to keep in mind: **Memorability:** A good stage name should stick in people's minds after they hear it once. Simple but catchy combinations tend to work best. **Uniqueness:** Standing out is important in the entertainment industry. Opt for less common words or spellings if possible. **Appropriateness:** Make sure your choice doesn't carry negative connotations, especially within the context of adult entertainment. Run your picks by friends or family members whose opinions you trust. Step 4: Test Out Names ---------------------- Try testing out different names during conversations with friends or coworkers who understand your situation. Pay attention to which ones generate positive reactions and feel natural when said aloud. Sometimes hearing a name used in casual conversation can reveal whether it truly fits. ### Key Tip: * Practice introducing yourself with your new stage name until it feels second nature. Confidence goes a long way in making a strong impression. Step 5: Finalize Your Choice --------------------------- After considering all factors and getting feedback, pick the name that resonates most strongly with you while also meeting the criteria discussed earlier (memorable, unique, appropriate). Once decided, commit fully to embracing your new identity. Consistency is vital for building brand recognition. ### Guideline: * Be prepared to stick with your chosen stage name throughout your career. Changing identities frequently can confuse regular customers and make establishing a solid reputation harder. Conclusion: Selecting a stage name requires thoughtfulness and creativity. By following these steps, evaluating various aspects of your potential choices, and seeking input from trusted sources, you'll find a moniker fitting of your personal style and professional goals. Title: How to Run While Doing Law in RuneScape Introduction: Running while doing law is an essential skill in RuneScape that can save you time and make your training more efficient. This guide will provide a comprehensive, step-by-step approach with clear explanations and additional tips to help you master this technique. Step 1: Preparation Before starting, ensure you have completed the following prerequisites: * Completed the quest "The Knight's Sword" (to unlock the Varrock Teleport spell). * At least level 20 Magic (for using Varrock Teleport tablets as an alternative method if no runes are available). * A high Agility level or access to Staminas / Energy Potions to maintain energy levels. * Familiarize yourself with the layout of the courts by visiting them before attempting to run during law tasks. Step 2: Understanding the Basics In order to run during law tasks, you need to know when and where running is necessary. Typically, there are two scenarios when you would want to run: A) When traveling between the courtroom and the exit point (the jail cell, stairs, etc.). B) When navigating through obstacles within the courthouse, such as desks, benches, or other players. Step 3: Timing Your Runs Timing is crucial when trying to run during law tasks. You cannot continuously sprint throughout the entire process; instead, you must strategically choose moments to activate your 'run' option based on the task at hand. For example: A) Exiting the Courthouse - As soon as you submit evidence or deliver verdicts, start running towards the exit point immediately. Once inside the jail cell or reaching the top of the staircase, deactivate the run feature to conserve energy. B) Navigating Obstacles - If possible, wait until you reach an open space before activating your run mode. This way, you avoid collisions with objects or other players that might slow down your progress. Remember to turn off the run feature once you pass any potential obstructions. Step 4: Managing Energy Levels Maintaining enough energy to complete your law tasks efficiently is vital. Here are some ways to manage your energy effectively: A) Train Agility - Higher Agility levels increase your character's energy regeneration rate, making it easier to sustain runs during law activities. B) Use Energy Potions - Consuming these potions provides instant energy boosts, enabling you to run further without worrying about exhaustion. C) Utilize Stamina Pots - Similar to energy pots, stamina potions give temporary increases in your character's movement speed, which indirectly conserves energy while running. D) Bring Food - Eating food replenishes both health points and energy. It serves as a backup source of energy when your current reserves are low. Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Prioritize running only when necessary, focusing on maximizing efficiency rather than constantly maintaining a high speed. 2. Learn shortcuts around the courthouses to reduce travel distances and minimize energy consumption. 3. Practice multitasking by performing actions like drinking potions or eating food while moving to save even more time. 4. Be mindful of other players who may also be completing their law tasks; avoiding collisions reduces wasted motion and keeps everyone's experience smooth. 5. Keep spare teleport methods handy in case you need to quickly leave the area due to low energy or other unforeseen circumstances. Newton's Second Law In the presence of a NET FORCE, an object experiences an ACCELERATION directly proportional to the NET FORCE inversely proportional to the MASS of the object. -- F is the NET force -- m is the mass which that net force acts on. We often turn this around and write it as F is the NET force acting on an object m is the mass of the object which the force F acts upon. What are the UNITS of force in F = m a A force of ONE unit will give an object of 1.0 kg mass an acceleration of 1.0 m/s/s ; this force is known as ONE NEWTON (1.0 N) . 1 N = ( 1 kg ) ( 1 m/s/s ) F = m a Force will be measured in newtons A force of 1 N will give a mass of 1 kg an accelertion of 1 m/s/s. 12 N = ( 3 kg ) ( 4 m/s/s ) A force of 12 N could give a mass of 3 kg an accelertion of 4 m/s/s 12 N = ( 2 kg ) ( 6 m/s/s ) A force of 12 N could give a mass of 2 kg an accelertion of 6 m/s/s . We have already seen that all objects fall with the same acceleration, 9.8 m/s/s (which we approximate as nearly 10 m/s/s). We call this free fall. When such objects fall, the only force acting on them is their weight, the force of gravity. The only force on a body in freefall is the force of gravity. We call this its weight. Since it accelerates at 9.8 m/s/s, that weight must be w = (mass ) x (9.8 m/s/s), w = m g The weight of an object is the force of gravity on that object. Weight, since it is a force, will be measured in units of newtons (N). Mass will be measured in kilograms (kg). If an object is in equilibrium -- at rest -- then, the net force on the object must be zero. That is, the sum of all the forces on an object is zero when the object is in equilibrium. Title: How to Delete Messages on Snapchat Snapchat is a popular multimedia messaging app that allows users to send photos, videos, and messages (known as "snaps") that disappear after they're viewed. However, you might want to delete a message you've sent if you made a mistake or said something you regret. While Snapchat doesn't have an official way to unsend messages, there are workarounds to hide or delete them. This wikiHow will guide you through the process of deleting messages on Snapchat. ### Deleting Sent Messages from Your Device 1. Open Snapchat: Locate the Snapchat app on your device and tap on its icon to open the application. The icon usually has a white ghost on a yellow background. 2. Go to the chat screen: Tap on the speech bubble icon at the bottom left corner of the screen. It will take you to the 'Chats' section where all conversations with friends are stored. 3. Find the conversation containing the message you wish to delete: Scroll up or down until you find the correct conversation thread. If needed, you can search for a specific friend by tapping the magnifying glass icon at the top right corner of the screen and typing their username into the search bar. 4. Long press on the message: Once you've found the message you want to remove, lightly press and hold your finger down on the particular message without lifting it off. A menu should appear with different options. 5. Select 'Delete': From the pop-up window, choose 'Delete'. Confirmation dialog boxes may show up asking if you really want to delete this message - confirm these prompts to proceed. Keep in mind that only the sender sees the deleted message; recipients still see the content unless they took a screenshot beforehand. 6. Optional Tip: Clear Cache Regularly - After deleting unwanted messages, clear your cache regularly to ensure no residual data remains within the app. To do so, navigate to Settings > Account Actions > Clear Cache, then select 'Continue.' Remember, clearing the cache logs you out temporarily but does not affect any saved chats or memories. ### Unopened Messages & Expired Snaps If the recipient hasn't opened the snap yet, you can simply let it expire naturally. By default, snaps disappear once viewed, while text messages vanish after they're read or 24 hours pass since sending. There isn't a surefire method to recall unread texts or unexpired pictures after being delivered, unlike deleting already-viewed messages explained earlier. Therefore, managing timely delivery prevents unnecessary misunderstandings. Key Guidelines: * Be cautious when sending sensitive information over Snapchat, given its inherently ephemeral nature. * Understand that even though you can delete a message, the recipient could potentially capture a screenshot prior to removal. * Familiarize yourself with privacy settings and reporting mechanisms provided by Snapchat to maintain safe online interactions. [BLA turns away and rubs her eyes] Title: How to Dress for a Summer Scene Summer is a time to enjoy the outdoors, attend various events, and showcase your personal style. However, dressing for summer can be challenging due to high temperatures and humidity. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to dress appropriately for different summer scenes while staying comfortable and stylish. **Step 1: Understand Your Environment** Before choosing an outfit, consider where you'll be going and what activities you'll partake in. A day at the beach requires different attire than a backyard barbecue or a night out in town. Knowing the setting allows you to make informed decisions about materials, colors, and styles that are suitable for the occasion. **Step 2: Select Appropriate Fabrics** Fabric choice plays a significant role in keeping cool during hot weather. Opt for natural fibers like cotton, linen, rayon, and silk as they allow better air circulation than synthetic fabrics. They also absorb moisture from sweat quickly, helping keep you dry and fresh throughout the day. *Key Tip:* Avoid heavy materials such as denim or wool, especially for bottoms and outer layers. **Step 3: Prioritize Light Colors** Light-colored clothing reflects sunlight rather than absorbing it, making them cooler to wear in direct sun. Choose pastels, whites, soft grays, or light neutrals over dark hues which tend to retain heat. **Step 4: Consider Loose Fits** Tight clothes may look fashionable but they trap body heat, leading to discomfort in warm conditions. Instead, go for loose-fitting garments that offer breathability and ease of movement. Remember, this doesn't mean wearing oversized clothing; maintain balance by pairing looser tops with more fitted bottoms or vice versa. **Step 5: Accessorize Thoughtfully** Accessories can elevate any outfit, but some types might not suit hot weather well. Here are some accessory suggestions based on their functionality and comfort level: - Hats: Broad-brimmed hats protect against harmful UV rays and add a chic touch to your ensemble. Straw boaters, floppy sun hats, or wide-brim fedoras are great options. - Sunglasses: Not only do sunglasses shield your eyes from bright sunlight, but they also prevent squinting, reducing wrinkles around the eye area. Look for polarized lenses offering UVA/UVB protection. - Footwear: Open-toed shoes like sandals let feet breathe, alleviating perspiration issues. For slightly formal occasions, espadrilles or leather slides work well. If physical activity is involved, consider athletic sneakers or waterproof flip-flops. - Bags: opt for lighter materials (straw, canvas) instead of leather or plastic bags. Crossbody styles distribute weight evenly across the body, leaving hands free. **Step 6: Don't Neglect Sunscreen** Even when fully clothed, sunburn remains possible through thin fabric. Apply broad-spectrum sunscreen liberally before stepping outside, focusing particularly on exposed areas. Reapply every two hours if swimming or sweating excessively. **Step 7: Stay Hydrated** Drinking plenty of fluids prevents dehydration caused by excessive sweating. Carry a reusable water bottle wherever you go and take regular sips. By following these steps, you can create stylish yet functional outfits perfect for any summer scene without compromising comfort. Happy dressing! yeah, and it made noises too to me. oh, is he flying up my coat? I knew him slightly and liked him not at all. Frog bones are a lot like trees. The bones form a growth ring every year when the frog is hibernating. Scientists can count these rings to find out the age of the frog. Frogs absorb water through their skin, so they do not need to drink. Tree frogs have developed disks or suction pads on the toes of their feet to help them climb into the tree canopy. Frogs can live just as well in water or on land. Many brightly colored tropical frogs, called Dart Frogs, are colored in this way to warn predators that they are poisonous. Amphibians must shed their skin as they get bigger in size. The old skin comes off like a piece of clothing that has become too tight. Usually, this shed skin is eaten by the frog. Frogs have long back legs and webbed feet for jumping and swimming. A frog can change the color of its skin depending on its surroundings. Frogs use a number of ways to attract a mate, such as courtship calls, body color and limb movements. Frogs range in size from 1cm to 30cm long. Some frogs can live in environments well below freezing. The Grey tree frog, for example, can survive even though its heart stops. It does this by making its own antifreeze, which stops its body from totally freezing. The eyes and nose of a frog are on top of its head so it can breathe and see when most of its body is under water. People who study frogs and toads are called herpetologists. Herpetology is the study of amphibians and reptiles. There are more than 4,000 types of amphibians in the world! The Golden Dart Frog is the most poisonous frog on earth. The skin of one frog could kill up to 1,000 people! home | frog or toad | frog facts | protection |books | contact me back to top oh, good kisses and hugs you're such a lovey. Get Awesome Shine: A Comprehensive Guide ========================================= Achieving an awesome shine can be applied to various objects and surfaces around you - from your shoes, car, furniture, to even your hair! Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tweetie. Tweetie was very graceful when she flew in the sky. She loved to sing and play with her bird friends. One day, Tweetie's bird friend, Chirpy, came to her and said, "Hey Tweetie, let's go play a quiz game!" Tweetie was excited and said, "Yes, let's go!" Chirpy had a tricky quiz for Tweetie. She asked, "What is the thing that you use to poke things?" Tweetie thought for a moment and said, "Is it a stick?" Chirpy said, "No, it's something smaller." Tweetie thought again and said, "Is it a finger?" Chirpy said, "Yes, that's right!" Later that day, Tweetie was flying in the sky when she saw a big, juicy worm on the ground. Here you want to put the animals on here? Hello young learners! Today, we're going to talk about a concept called "rank" in mathematics, using a fun card game example. Let's say we have a deck of cards with pictures of different animals - lions, tigers, elephants, and giraffes. These cards represent a special kind of math tool called a "matrix," which is just a fancy name for a grid of numbers or values. Now, imagine we arrange these animal cards into groups based on their characteristics. Lions and tigers share some similarities, while elephants and giraffes belong to another group because they don't behave like big cats. We call this arrangement of cards (or matrix) a "reduced QR factorization." It means breaking down our original collection into two parts: one part contains information about the connections between elements (the lion-tiger relationship), and the second part shows distinct features within those subgroups (elephants and giraffes). In this process, we may find out that some animals simply repeat the same qualities found elsewhere, making them redundant. Our goal now becomes understanding the minimum number of unique traits required to describe all animals accurately. This idea connects directly to the concept of "rank" mentioned earlier. So, back to our question: How do we determine the "rank" of our card arrangement (or matrix)? Well, let's think of it as counting the smallest possible number of groups needed to categorize every single animal without repetition. Using our previous breakdown, suppose we discover that three categories - lions, tigers, and elephants - suffice to cover all distinctions among the four types of creatures. Then, our card arrangement would have a "rank" of three since there are three independent sets of attributes explaining the entire set. To sum up, finding the "rank" helps us understand the essential pieces of information necessary to define any given data structure (our card deck). By doing this, we also gain insight into hidden relationships between components, enabling better organization and problem solving! Just remember, ranks tell us about the fewest number of key features required to distinguish everything uniquely. Have fun exploring the world of matrices and ranks! Title: Find Out How Many Weeks Pregnant You Are Introduction: Determining how many weeks pregnant you are is crucial for prenatal care, tracking your baby's development, and preparing for delivery. This guide will walk you through various methods to calculate pregnancy duration accurately. Please consult with healthcare professionals for medical advice. Step 1: Last Menstrual Period (LMP) Calculation The most common method to estimate gestational age is by calculating from the first day of your last menstrual period (LMP). Conception typically occurs around two weeks after this date. Here's why we do it this way: - It provides an easy reference point as most women can recall their LMP more accurately than ovulation dates. - The exact date of conception isn't always known due to varying menstrual cycle lengths and irregularities. Key Tip: Even though conception usually happens about two weeks post-menstruation, consider using the first day of your LMP as Day One for simplicity and standardization. Step 2: Use a Due Date Calculator After identifying your LMP, input that information into an online due date calculator. These tools subtract two weeks from your LMP date and add nine months (40 weeks), giving you an estimated due date. Some websites also provide additional features like weekly updates or growth charts based on your calculated week. Guideline: Always double-check calculations with multiple reputable online tools or apps to ensure accuracy. Step 3: Confirm with Ultrasound Measurements While home estimations are helpful, ultrasounds offer more precise measurements during early pregnancy stages. During the initial ultrasound (usually between 8-12 weeks), sonographers measure the crown-rump length (CRL) of the fetus. CRL measurement correlates closely with gestational age, so doctors can adjust estimates if necessary. Important Note: While ultrasounds may refine the estimation, they cannot pinpoint down to the exact day when conception occurred because sperm survival varies and sometimes implantation takes longer than expected. Step 4: Regular Checkups and Adjustments Continue visiting your healthcare provider regularly throughout pregnancy. They might revise your due date based on subsequent ultrasounds or other factors such as changes in fundal height (the distance from the pubic bone to the top of the uterus). Remember, only approximately 5% of babies are born on their actual due date; most deliveries occur within two weeks before or after. Precautions & Guidelines: 1. If you have irregular periods, discuss alternative ways to estimate pregnancy week count with your doctor. 2. Keep track of any significant symptoms, appointments, tests, or milestones related to your pregnancy in a journal or app. 3. Never rely solely on self-calculated weeks; always confirm with healthcare professionals. 4. Be aware that certain conditions, like carrying multiples or having a history of premature births, could affect the calculation. Conclusion: Knowing how many weeks pregnant you are contributes significantly to informed decision-making and preparedness. By following these steps and consulting healthcare providers, you'll be able to monitor your pregnancy progress effectively. Title: How to Cross Out Words in a Microsoft Word Document Microsoft Word is a powerful word processing tool that offers various formatting options, including the ability to cross out words. This feature can be particularly useful when you want to indicate changes, corrections, or deletions without entirely removing the text. Here's a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to do this: 1. **Open your Microsoft Word document:** The first step is to launch the Microsoft Word application on your computer and open the document where you wish to cross out words. 2. **Select the word(s) you want to cross out:** Navigate through your document until you find the word or phrase you wish to modify. To select it, click and drag your cursor over the word(s). If the words are adjacent to each other, double-clicking on the first word will automatically select the entire string of words. 3. **Access the 'Font' group:** Once you have selected the word(s), look at the top menu bar of Word. You'll see several tabs - Home, Insert, Design, etc. Click on the 'Home' tab; this action highlights the Font group with different font styles and formats. 4. **Click on 'Strikethrough':** Within the Font group, there should be an option labeled 'Strikethrough'. It looks like a horizontal line (-) above the center of two shorter lines (//). When you hover your mouse over it, 'Strikethrough' should appear as a tooltip. Clicking on 'Strikethrough' applies a line through the middle of your selected text, effectively crossing it out. 5. **Verify the crossed-out effect:** After clicking 'Strikethrough', check whether the word(s) have been crossed out correctly. Remember, if you made a mistake, simply press Ctrl+Z (or Command+Z on Mac) immediately after applying strikethrough to undo the change. Key Tips & Guidelines: - *Multiple Formats:* Don't worry if your text already has bold, italicized, or underlined parts before crossing them out. Strikethrough works independently from these other formats. However, note that changing any part of the format (like color or size) might affect all characters equally, which could make the strike-through less noticeable. - *Undo Function:* Always remember the shortcut keys (Ctrl+Z / Command+Z) to quickly reverse actions. They come in handy when you accidentally cross out more than intended or choose the wrong format. - *Track Changes:* For collaborative editing scenarios involving multiple authors, consider using the Track Changes function instead. Not only does it visibly mark additions and removals, but it also allows others to accept or reject those edits individually. Access this feature via the Review tab > Track Changes dropdown button. By following these steps carefully, you can accurately cross out words in a Microsoft Word document while maintaining professional appearance and clear communication. Title: How to Dye Pasta for Crafts **Things You'll Need:** 1. Uncooked pasta (any shape or size) 2. Food coloring 3. Rubbing alcohol, vodka, or white vinegar 4. Ziploc bags or airtight containers 5. Paper towels or newspaper 6. Cookie sheet 7. Oven (optional) Step 1: Choose Your Pasta Select the type of pasta you want to dye based on your craft project. Different shapes and sizes can add interesting textures and visual appeal to various art projects. Step 2: Prepare the Pasta Pour the uncooked pasta into a colander and rinse under cold water to remove any residual starch that might interfere with the dye absorption process. Shake off excess water and spread the pasta out onto paper towels or newspapers to dry completely before proceeding to the next step. Key Tip: Make sure the pasta is thoroughly dried; otherwise, the moisture may dilute the dye. Step 3: Mix the Dye In a small bowl, combine rubbing alcohol, vodka, or white vinegar with several drops of food coloring. The amount of liquid needed depends on how much pasta you are dying. A good rule of thumb is one tablespoon of liquid per cup of pasta. Stir well until you reach your desired shade. Guideline: If you prefer more vibrant colors, add additional drops of food coloring. Keep in mind that darker shades may bleed when used in certain craft applications. Step 4: Color the Pasta Place the dried pasta into a Ziploc bag or an airtight container. Pour enough colored liquid over the pasta to evenly coat all pieces. Seal the bag or container tightly and gently shake or turn upside down to ensure every piece gets covered in dye. Key Tips: - Avoid overcrowding the bag or container as this could prevent some pieces from being properly coated. - Use gloves to protect your hands from temporary staining caused by the food coloring. Step 5: Dry the Pasta Spread the dyed pasta out in a single layer on a cookie sheet lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil. Allow the pasta to air-dry overnight, stirring occasionally to promote even drying. Alternatively, you can speed up the process by baking the pasta at its lowest temperature setting (around 200 F or 93 C) for about 15-20 minutes, checking frequently to prevent burning. Guidelines: - Ensure there is adequate space between pasta pieces to allow proper air circulation during drying. - Be cautious when handling hot trays and baked pasta to avoid burns. Step 6: Store the Dyed Pasta Once the pasta has fully dried, store it in an airtight container or Ziploc bag to preserve its vibrancy. When stored correctly, dyed pasta can last for several months. Key Tips: - Label the storage container with the date and color(s) to keep track of freshness and availability. - Discard any pasta that shows signs of mold or spoilage. By following these steps and guidelines, you will have beautifully dyed pasta perfect for various craft projects. Enjoy creating unique artwork while engaging in a fun and educational activity! Internet Explorer 5 was launched on March 18, 1999, later included with Windows 98 Second Edition and came with Office 2000. The creation of Internet Explorer 5 was another significant release that supported bi-directional text, ruby characters, XML, XSLT, and the ability to save web pages in MHTML format. IE5 was bundled with Outlook Express 5. Also, with the release of Internet Explorer 5.0, Microsoft released the first version of XMLHttpRequest, giving birth to Ajax (even though the term "Ajax" was not coined until years later). It was the last with a 16-bit version. Internet Explorer 5.01, a bug fix version, was released in December 1999. Windows 2000 includes this version. Internet Explorer 5.5 followed in July 2000, improving its print preview capabilities, CSS and HTML standards support, and developer APIs; this version was bundled with Windows Me. However, version 5 was the last version for Mac and UNIX. Version 5.5 was the last to have "Compatibility Mode", which allowed Internet Explorer 4 to be run side by side with the 5.x. The IE team consisted of over 1,000 people by 1999, with funding on the order of 100 million USD per year. Fine let's bring our friends over 'n' they're going to have a party with us. Today, we are going to learn about sequences and their limits. Have you ever noticed how some patterns of numbers get closer and closer to a certain number as you go on? That's what we call a "convergent sequence"! Let's explore this concept with a fun example. Imagine you have a special toy box where every night, your favorite stuffed animal grows a little bigger. The size of your stuffed animal on the first night is 1 unit (let's say centimeters), and each following night, it grows by a factor of \(t.\) So, on the second night, it will be \(t\) units, on the third night \(t*t,\) or \(t^2\) units, and so forth. Now, here comes the catch - while your stuffed animal keeps growing, you also receive a magical shrinking spray which reduces its size by 1/\[n\], where \(n\) represents the current night number (\(n = 1, 2, 3, ...\)). So, our sequence becomes: \(a\_n = t^n / n!\) Now let's talk about limits. A limit describes what value a sequence seems to approach as \(n\) gets larger and larger. In our case, we want to find out if the sequence \(a\_n = t^n / n!\) gets closer to a specific number as \(n\) goes to infinity. Our friend Aroosak asked if this limit goes to zero. Well, there's another interesting property of sequences: if a sequence converges to a particular number, then its terms must get arbitrarily close to that number as \(n\) increases. This means that if our sequence converged to zero, then all of its terms should become smaller than any given positive number as \(n\) grows. But there's one term in our sequence that doesn't follow this rule - the very first term, \(a\_1 = t\), stays constant and never approaches zero no matter how large \(n\) gets! However, don't worry; even though the limit isn't zero, it may still exist. Sometimes when working with these types of problems, it's easy to mix up concepts, just like Aroosak did initially. Remember, knowing that a sequence's individual terms go to zero (\(a\_n 0\)) does not guarantee that the sum of those terms will converge. It's simply a necessary but insufficient condition for convergence. In summary, today we learned about convergent sequences, their limits, and how determining if a sequence has a finite limit requires careful examination. We saw that sometimes, confusing different mathematical ideas could lead us to incorrect conclusions. Keep practicing identifying various sequences, finding their limits, and distinguishing between the series and sequence properties - you'll surely grow into proficient mathematicians! Title: How to Block Applications on Your iPhone or iPad Introduction: Blocking applications on your iPhone or iPad can be useful in various situations. Perhaps you want to limit your child's screen time, prevent access to distracting apps during work hours, or simply take control of your device usage. This tutorial will guide you through the process of blocking apps on iOS devices using Screen Time, a built-in feature introduced with iOS 12. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Make sure your device is updated to at least iOS 12 before following this tutorial. * Keep in mind that once an app is blocked, users cannot open it without entering a passcode set by you. * If you forget your Screen Time passcode, resetting it requires erasing all data from your device and setting it up as new. Be cautious when choosing your Screen Time passcode. Step 1: Enabling Screen Time And this little boy name jack is going to sit in here and write in the blackboard too. The polar bear is the world's largest meat-eating land animal. Polar bears are well adapted to hunt seals out on the ice. They are excellent swimmers, and the coldest water does not bother them. They wait by breathing holes if they pick up the scent of a seal. They can also break through the ice at thin areas. Sometimes they can sneak up on a sleeping seal. They have a very strong sense of smell, which they use to find prey out on the ice. Polar bears get most of their food when there is pack ice. They must eat and store fat before the ice is gone: they cannot catch seals in open water. After mating, females dig themselves into a den of snow on land for the rest of the winter. There, they have their cubs, usually twins. Bears usually eat fish, birds' eggs, seaweed, and dead whales, but they would rather have a meal of seal or walrus. In 1925, Werner Heisenberg described the Uncertainty principle, which says that the more we know about where a particle is, the less we can know about how fast it is going and in which direction. In other words, the more we know about the speed and direction of something small, the less we can know about its position. Physicists usually talk about the momentum in such discussions instead of talking about speed. Momentum is just the speed of something in a certain direction times its mass. [CHI gets up and crawls over to another toy] Let me see what you're going to draw. Imagine you and your friends are trying to guess how many candies are in a jar. Each of you comes up with a different estimate. The average (mean) of all your estimates could give a good idea of how many candies there really are. But what if some of you guessed very close to the actual number, while others were way off? This difference between the guesses can tell us something too - maybe some people had a better sense of how many candies there were than others. In math language, we call this difference the "variance." It measures how spread out the numbers are from the mean or average value. In our candy example, imagine you do this activity several times with different groups of friends. Even though the real number of candies stays the same, the variances of your group's guesses will probably change every time. So, just like how we wanted to know the distribution of the means (how likely we are to get different averages), we also want to know the distribution of these variances (how likely we are to get different levels of spread). Now, here's where things get more complicated. You might remember learning about the bell curve when studying probability distributions earlier this year. Well, for normally distributed data, there's actually a special name for the distribution of variances called the chi-square distribution. However, when dealing with Poisson distributions (which are often used for counting things, like guessing the number of candies), we don't have an easy answer like that yet! Scientists and mathematicians are still figuring out what kind of distribution describes the variance of Poisson data. They haven't found one single, perfect match like the chi-square distribution for normals. That's why the original question asks whether there's an "analogue for Poisson," meaning another type of distribution that works similarly well. As far as I know, no one has discovered one yet, so most folks resort to using computer simulations to understand how variances behave in Poisson situations. I saw her turn into a monster. He looked up and down the street. Solving quadratics can be difficult and solving quadratics using square roots is just one of the methods of solving a quadratic equation. Solving a quadratic equation using square roots works best when we have a quadratic equation that does not have a "b" term and by taking the square root of either side of the equation. Quadratic equations can also be solved by factoring, completing the square or the quadratic formula. When you're asked to solve a quadratic equation you have lots of options. One thing you can do is graph it and look for the x intercepts. You could try factoring and use a zero product property. You could use a quadratic formula. You can complete the square. Or you can do what we're going to look at here and that is taking the square root of both sides of an equation. This is a really useful technique in the following situation so listen up. If you don't hear anything else I hope you hear this. Take the square root of both sides if you have no B term. What that means is that nowhere in your equation do you have something that has an X attached to it. You might have X squared, X to the third, or whatever but nothing has an X to the first power. Take the square roots of both sides if there's no B term. I'll show you what I mean. Before we do that let's talk about what you know about square roots. You guys know that four squared is equal to sixteen. It's kind of tricky because negative four squared is also equal to sixteen. So in our problems where we are solving by taking the square root of both sides sometimes you're going to get something like the square root of sixteen but be careful you're going to have a positive and a negative answer. The square root of sixteen is equal to four and also negative four. So when you are doing problems like this and you are taking the square root of both sides in order to solve for X be really careful that you account for both positive and negative square roots. The last thing I am going to leave you with is that this is different from that. Notice how here I have the negative sign under the square root sign. This has no real solution. So if you have something when you are solving where you have this square root of a negative number then that means that you're not going to have a solution, at least in this class. You'll get to those solutions in your future math class. But for now if you have square root of a negative number you can just write "no real solution". Find the Shortcut on Coconut Mall in Mario Kart Wii Mario Kart Wii is a popular video game that allows players to race go-karts around various tracks, one of which is called Coconut Mall. This track has a well-known shortcut that can give racers an edge over their opponents. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to find and take this shortcut: 1. Start the Race: At the beginning of the race, make sure you are in first place or close to it. The shortcut is more difficult to execute if you are far behind, as you will have less time to catch up to your competitors. 2. Enter the First Section of the Track: As you begin the race, stay focused on navigating through the initial section of the track, which features a large fountain surrounded by palm trees. 3. Approach the Ramp: After exiting the first section, continue driving straight ahead until you reach a ramp located near some shops with glass windows. 4. Jump Off the Ramp: Drive up the ramp at full speed and jump off the edge, aiming towards the right side of the track. It's crucial to maintain high speed when jumping off the ramp to ensure enough momentum to reach the shortcut. 5. Land on the Roof: If executed correctly, your kart should land on top of a building's roof. You may need to adjust your angle slightly during the jump to successfully land on the roof. 6. Drive Across the Roofs: Once landed, drive across the rooftops carefully while avoiding any obstacles such as air vents or pipes sticking out from the buildings. Keep an eye out for arrows pointing you in the direction of the shortcut. 7. Descend the Stairs: When you see stairs leading down from the rooftop, position yourself so that you can descend them without falling off the edge or crashing into anything. Be cautious, as these steps can be tricky to navigate due to tight corners and narrow spaces. 8. Rejoin the Main Track: Upon reaching the bottom of the stairs, reenter the main part of the track just before the second ramp. By now, your competitors should still be making their way through the earlier parts of the course. 9. Gain Speed: Accelerate quickly after reentering the track to regain any lost speed from taking the shortcut. 10. Maintain Your Position: Focus on maintaining or improving your current position within the race. Taking the shortcut should provide you with a significant advantage over other racers, but it's essential to keep pushing forward to secure your lead. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Practice makes perfect: Mastering the Coconut Mall shortcut takes practice, so don't be discouraged if you fail to execute it flawlessly during your first few attempts. * Use lightweight characters: Lightweight characters like Toad and Pink Gold Peach tend to have better acceleration, making them ideal choices for attempting the shortcut. * Customize your kart: Consider using a kart setup that emphasizes speed and handling, as both attributes will help you navigate the rooftop terrain more efficiently. * Stay patient: Wait for the right moment to attempt the shortcut, ensuring that you have enough time to recover if something goes wrong. * Learn from others: Watch replays of skilled players who excel at taking the Coconut Mall shortcut to pick up helpful strategies and techniques. Backup Your Saved GIFs on Discord: A Comprehensive Tutorial Discord is an immensely popular platform that allows users to communicate with others through voice calls, video calls, text messaging, media and files in private chats or as part of communities called "servers." As you engage in conversations and participate in servers, you may come across numerous engaging GIFs that you'd like to save for future reference. However, Discord does not provide a built-in feature to directly download these saved GIFs from their servers. But don't worry - there are workarounds! Title: How to Care for a Spider Plant Spider plants (Chlorophytum comosum) are popular houseplants known for their air-purifying qualities and attractive, spider-like offshoots. They're low maintenance and adaptable, making them an excellent choice for both novice and experienced gardeners. Here is our comprehensive guide on caring for your spider plant: **Step 1: Choosing the Right Pot** A well-draining pot is crucial for a spider plant as it prevents root rot caused by excess moisture. Choose a container that has drainage holes at the bottom. The size should be proportional to the plant; too large can lead to overwatering issues, while too small may restrict growth. *Key Tip*: Clay pots are more breathable than plastic ones, which helps avoid waterlogging. **Step 2: Selecting Suitable Soil** Spider plants need loose, well-drained soil with plenty of organic matter. A standard commercial potting mix will suffice if amended with perlite or vermiculite for better drainage. The ideal pH range is between 6.0 and 7.2. *Guideline*: Avoid heavy soils high in clay content because they retain too much water. **Step 3: Proper Lighting Conditions** Although spider plants tolerate various light levels, they thrive best in bright but indirect sunlight. Direct sun exposure might scorch their leaves. If you notice yellowish leaves, this could indicate that your plant is receiving too much light. Conversely, insufficient light results in leggy plants. *Key Tip*: Rotate your spider plant every few weeks to ensure even growth. **Step 4: Watering Techniques** Water sparingly, keeping the top inch of soil slightly dry before watering again. Overwatering is harmful - it encourages fungal diseases and root decay. Use room temperature water to prevent shocking the roots. *Guideline*: During winter months, reduce watering since these plants become semi-dormant. **Step 5: Fertilization Schedule** Feed your spider plant once every two months during spring and summer using a balanced liquid fertilizer diluted to half strength. Refrain from feeding during fall and winter when the plant slows down its growth rate significantly. *Key Tip*: Always follow manufacturer instructions regarding application rates. **Step 6: Pruning & Maintenance** Prune regularly to remove dead or brown foliage and promote bushier growth. You can also pinch back longer runners to control the spread of the plant. Regular cleaning of the leaves keeps them dust-free and allows optimal photosynthesis. *Guideline*: Don't discard trimmings right away! Read below about propagation opportunities. **Step 7: Propagation Process** Propagating spider plants is straightforward through division or cuttings. When a runner develops roots, snip it off carefully along with some nodes and place it in moistened potting mix. Keep it humid until established, usually within four weeks. Division involves splitting mature clumps into smaller sections, replanting them separately. *Key Tip*: Late spring or early summer is the perfect time for propagation. By following these steps meticulously, you'll have healthy, lush spider plants adorning your indoor spaces, providing cleaner air and aesthetic appeal simultaneously. Remember, patience and consistency yield beautiful results with spider plants care! You know what you do with a pitcher? Who's going to bring them for me? 'F you don't want' 'o hear it. Word problems sometimes ask us to write a linear function to model a situation. The word problem may be phrased in such a way that we can easily find a linear function using the slope-intercept form of the equation for a line. Hannah's electricity company charges her $0.11 per kWh (kilowatt-hour) of electricity, plus a basic connection charge of $15.00 per month. Write a linear function that models her monthly electricity bill as a function of electricity usage. Here, when Hannah uses zero electricity (that is, when x = 0), the bill is $15.00. So, the y -intercept is 15. The rate of change is 0.11. That is, for each increase of x by 1 unit (in this case, kilowatt-hours), there is an increase in y by 0.11.Substitute in the slope-intercept form . In other problems, it may be easier to use the point-slope form of the equation. Roger has a house in Telluride, Colorado , but starts a new job in Denver . Every Monday, he drives his car 332 miles from Telluride to Denver , spends the week in a company apartment, and then drives back to Telluride on Friday. He doesn't use his car for anything else. After 20 weeks of this, his odometer shows that he has travelled 240,218 miles since he bought the car. Write a linear model which gives the odometer reading of the car as a function of the number of weeks since Roger started the new job. First, find the rate of change. Be sure to multiply the distance by 2: he has to go and come back! 2(332) = 664 miles per week This represents the slope of the line. But, since we are not given the odometer reading of the car before he starts the job, we don't know the y -intercept yet. But that's okay. We have the coordinates of a point: (20, 240218). So we can use the point-slope form . Note that you can use this equation to find the y -intercept if you want to. Can you put the little boy on top of the little girl? chuckled Jack. "Did he spot you?" "Not much! I had business elsewhere, and I dusted," answered his chum. "I've received an invitation for you three lads," said the master of the Hall. Page 1 of 2 What the hex? Hexadecimal is the most common way of displaying the raw data sitting in a machine's memory or even stored on disk. You can be happily programming away in a high level language without a care in the world and then suddenly an serious error occurs and you are faced with a line showing you the address of the problem and the contents of the processors registers etc. all in glorious hex. Even if you are not programming, the most usual format to dump a file in is as lines and lines of hex. Back in the dark old days of assembly language programming you had to be familiar with hex as well as with binary and occasionally octal. If you haven't mucked about with assembler or machine architecture, or if you fell asleep in the first term of the computer course, then you might think hex is just a programmer's curse. To make sure that you know better this is a short, and highly practical guide, to hexadecimal addressing and data. If only we had all been born with 16 fingers! Not only would typing have been a faster activity but we might have counted in hex naturally. Counting is a matter of using symbols to represent each number. For example, 0, 1, 2, 3 and as on. Using this simple system the problem is that you quickly run out of symbols. The solution is to use a place value counting method. In decimal we are all very happy with this method - you count up to 9 and then start again after recording the fact that you have got to 10 once by writing a 1 to the left. In school we are taught that each place to the left represents 10 times more than the previous digit location. That is, the first value represents units, the second lots of 10, the third lots of 100 and so on. This means that a number like 123 is really: or one lot of 100, two lots of 10 and three lots of 1. It is generally supposed, and I can think of no better explanation, that we count in lots of 10 because that's how many fingers we have. All of this is so easy that we tend to use it intuitively and without being able to explain what it going on. This makes the shock of changing to a different counting base even more traumatic. Hexadecimal uses 16 as the base. To put this another way the hex counting system uses `lots of 16' in the same way that the decimal system uses `lots of 10'. The first problem to be solved is how to count up to the first lot of 16 as there are only ten digits - 0 to 9. The solution is that we use the letters A, B, C, D, E and F to supplement the meager inheritance that having only ten fingers has bestowed upon us. This means that counting in hex up to 15 goes The only tricky bit being to remember not to say `ten' after 9. Now what happens after counting to F? This is, the same question as what happens after counting to 9 in decimal. The answer is that you write a 1 to the left to indicate that you have counted one lot of 16 and then carry on counting. That is after F comes 10 which isn't ten (decimal) and shouldn't really be said as ten but `one nought' or `one zero'. The next question is what comes after 10 in hex? The answer is 11, then 12 and so on all the way up to 1F. At this point we have another lot of 16 and we start counting again at 20 and so on. The only danger point happens when you reach 9F and the temptation is to accidentally make the next value 100. It isn't because in hex A comes after 9 and so the next value is A0 and so on. You only reach 100 in hex after counting all of the way to FF. Fun isn't it? Once you have learned to count to 100 hex there isn't anything more to see. You just keep counting up to F and adding one to the place to the left until it reaches F and so on. Many programming languages use the convention, first used in C, that a hex number is indicated by starting with 0x. So if you have counted to FF you can write this as 0xFF. Remember: the hex place value system works with lots of 16 and not lots of 10. Being able to count in hex is a great way to understand the how the system works but it isn't much use when you are confronted with a message such as `AX=AD45'. To really feel at home with hex you have to be able to understand it in a slightly different way. You certainly have to be able to convert hex to decimal and vice versa but there is something deeper. First, though, how do you convert hex to decimal? There are a number of standard algorithms that can be used to convert between different number bases but I have to admit that I prefer a more primitive approach. The place values used in hex are: and you can use these to work out the equivalent decimal value quite easily. For example, AD45 is simply: A*4096 + D*256 + 4*16 + 5*1 to express it in a mix of hex and decimal, or moving entirely to decimal: 10*4096 + 13*256 + 4*16 + 5*1 which works out to: 40960 + 3328 + 64 + 5 or 44357. To convert from decimal to hex is just a little more complicated in that you have to discover how many lots of 4096, 256 and 16 there are in a number. For example, 44357 contains 10 lots of 4096 because: 44357/4096 = 10.83 The remainder, i.e. 44357-4096*10 is 3397 and this contains 13 lots of 256 because: 3397/256 = 13.27 and so on to discover that the remainder contains 4 lots of 16 and 5 units. Writing 10 lots of 4096, 13 lots of 256, 4 lots of 16 and 5 units in standard hex gives AD45. Fortunately it isn't often that you have to convert decimal to hex! [same intonation as S; 5 sec silence] Title: How to Perform a Cross-Leg Turn on Rollerblades Performing a cross-leg turn on rollerblades is an exciting and impressive move that adds flair to your skating skills. This maneuver requires balance, strength, and coordination but with practice, patience, and persistence, you can master this technique. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you get started: **Step 1: Gear Up Properly** Before attempting any advanced moves like a cross-leg turn, ensure you have the right gear. You need quality rollerblades that fit well, along with safety equipment such as helmets, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards. These will protect you from falls and injuries during practice. **Step 2: Master Basic Skills** Practice fundamental skating techniques first - stopping, turning, balancing, striding. Familiarize yourself with transitions between forward and backward motions. Good control over these basics sets the foundation for more complex tricks. **Step 3: Learn the Crossovers** Crossovers are essential for building up speed needed for a cross-leg turn. Start by practicing inside crossovers where you cross one leg behind another while moving in a circle. Once comfortable, try outside crossovers (crossing one leg in front) at higher speeds. **Step 4: Get Comfortable With One-Foot Glides** Balance plays a crucial role in executing a successful cross-leg turn. Practice gliding on one foot without losing stability. Try lifting alternate feet off the ground while maintaining steady speed and direction. **Step 5: The Backward Lean** For a cross-leg turn, you must lean back slightly so that most of your weight rests on your heels. It might feel unnatural initially, but it becomes easier once you understand its importance. This position allows smoother rotation during the turn. **Step 6: Initiate the Cross-Leg Position** As you gain momentum through crossovers, lift the non-dominant leg and place it across the dominant leg at about waist height. Your thighs should be parallel to the ground. Keep both knees bent softly for better balance. **Step 7: Begin the Rotation** Now, push off gently using your dominant foot. Simultaneously, twist your upper body towards the planted (non-dominant) foot side. Remember to maintain your center of gravity above your hips. **Step 8: Complete the Turn** Continue rotating until you face the original direction. During this process, keep your eyes focused ahead; looking down or sideways could disrupt your equilibrium. As you complete the turn, bend your knees deeply to absorb the impact and regain balance quickly. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always warm up before starting any intense activity. Loosening muscles reduces risk of injury. * Choose a spacious area free from obstacles to practice. Smooth surfaces like tennis courts or skate parks are ideal. * Falling is inevitable when learning new tricks. Understanding how to fall safely minimizes potential harm. Bend your elbows and knees upon landing to distribute shock evenly across your body. * Never rush progress. Allow ample time to familiarise yourself with each stage before moving onto the next. * Consider enrolling in professional lessons if possible. Trained instructors provide valuable feedback and corrections tailored to individual needs. By following these steps meticulously, regularly practising, and staying patient throughout the process, performing a cross-leg turn on rollerblades will become second nature! Popular opinion in the state against this practice brought the end to this use. Navy has since been trying to cleanup unexploded ordnance (bombs and explosive shells) from the island. Summer usually lasts from May to early September, and temperatures can reach up to +35 C. Autumns are dark and rainy. Winter snow usually begins to fall in Helsinki in early December (in Lapland it can fall as early as October) and in the winter the temperature can drop to -30 C. Winter usually lasts to mid-March, when the snow melts in Helsinki (in Lapland the snow usually doesn't melt until early May), and Spring lasts till late May. Spring can be erratic, and the weather can change from frost to sunshine within a matter of days. The famed Northern Lights are common in Lapland. Sarah c'me 'ere 'n' look at your shoe. Unit 15: USING THE VERB FORMS Unit 14 tells you the names of the different forms and how to make them. This unit tells you when to use them. IN PRESENT TENSE (except for 3rd person singular) We live near you. Have you lived here long? Do you live here? AFTER AUXILIARY VERB DO (Unit 17) EXAMPLE: Did you live there? IN IMPERATIVES (Unit 18) EXAMPLE: Have a seat! EXAMPLE: I wanted to go home. AFTER MODAL VERBS (Unit 27) EXAMPLE: You must hurry. Use only in third person singular (Unit 2) of present tense. He lives near you. Has he lived here long? Does he live here? Use only in past tense. They lived near you. He had lived with them. Did you live here? He is living with them. She has been swimming. AS A NOUN (Unit 48) EXAMPLE: Living here is very nice. IN PERFECT FORMATIONS (Unit 24) EXAMPLE: He has lived in many places. IN PASSIVE FORMATIONS (Unit 43) EXAMPLE: Tea is drunk everywhere. NOTICE: The forms of be and their uses are in Unit 16. Slow feet quick feet well feet sick feet. Okay well they said we couldn't do that, okay. Hey GAM you know what we have one more thing to do. Draw a Story Age Range: 5 to 11 1) Give each child in your class a copy of the storyboard sheet found here. 2) Ask them to make up a story about anything that they want. 3) Ask them to draw the events of the story in the larger boxes in the sheet. The smaller boxes are for text, but they should not fill these in at the moment. 4) When the children have finished their drawings, collect in the storyboards, mix them up, and give them back (but not to the correct children). Now, each child should be looking at a storyboard which is not their own. 5) They should now look carefully at the pictures, and make up some text to go with them. 6) When this is done, the writer and the artist should get together and look at what each other have done.
