[Nina had seen a man making some penguins do tricks in a little pool at the zoo.] Title: How to Buy on eBay Without Using PayPal Buying on eBay is a popular way to find great deals on new and used items from sellers all over the world. While PayPal is one of the most common payment methods accepted by eBay sellers, there are alternatives available if you don't have or want to use a PayPal account. This step-by-step guide will walk you through purchasing an item on eBay using other payment options. ### Step 1: Search for an Item Go to the eBay homepage (www.ebay.com) and search for the item you would like to purchase. You can do this by entering keywords related to your product into the search bar at the top of the page. Once you hit enter, eBay will display a list of relevant results. Browse these results until you find an item that meets your needs and budget. Key tip: Read seller ratings and reviews before making a decision. These can provide valuable insight into their reliability as a seller and help ensure you receive quality products and customer service. ### Step 2: Select Your Item and Proceed to Checkout Once you've found the perfect item, click on its listing to view more details about it, such as shipping costs, estimated delivery time, and return policy. When ready, select 'Add to Cart'. If everything looks good with your order summary, proceed to checkout. Guideline: Make sure to double-check the total cost, including any additional fees or taxes, before moving forward. ### Step 3: Choose a Different Payment Method During the checkout process, eBay will first prompt you to log in or sign up for a PayPal account. Ignore this option and look further down the page where alternative payment methods should be listed, which may include credit cards, debit cards, or bank transfers. Click on your preferred method. Key Tip: Be aware that some sellers might only accept specific types of payments, so make sure to choose one that is accepted by the seller. ### Step 4: Enter Shipping Information Fill out the required fields with your name, address, phone number, and email address. Some sellers require this information even when they offer local pickup; however, always confirm whether local pickup is possible without providing contact info. Guideline: Provide accurate information since incorrect data could lead to delays or lost packages during shipment. ### Step 5: Confirm Order Details and Review Terms & Conditions Review your entire order once again, ensuring accuracy regarding the purchased item(s), quantities, prices, and shipping costs. Carefully read through the terms and conditions set forth by both eBay and the seller. Agreeing to them implies understanding and accepting responsibilities related to returns, refunds, disputes, etc. Key Tip: Keep a record of your order confirmation number and receipt for future reference. ### Step 6: Complete Purchase After verifying all details, submit your payment either by clicking 'Pay Now' or following prompts for manual authorization depending on the chosen payment method. Upon successful completion, you should see a message confirming your transaction along with an estimate of when you can expect your package to arrive. Guideline: Save all communication between yourself and the seller regarding your purchase as proof of sale and potential recourse if issues arise later. By following these steps, you can successfully buy an item on eBay without relying on PayPal as your primary payment method. Always remember to exercise caution and due diligence while shopping online to avoid scams and protect your personal information. You know how you know it's not a penny? Why do you height show off and let people listen to all your foolishness? The inner part of the earth is the core. This part of the earth is about 1,800 miles (2,900 km) below the earth's surface. The core is a dense ball of the elements iron and nickel. It is divided into two layers, the inner core and the outer core. The inner core - the center of earth - is solid and about 780 miles (1,250 km ) thick. The outer core is so hot that the metal is always molten, but the inner core pressures are so great that it cannot melt, even though temperatures there reach 6700 F (3700 C). The outer core is about 1,370 miles (2,200 km) thick. Because the earth rotates, the outer core spins around the inner core and that causes the earth's magnetism. What does the core consist of? It is 3,200 - 3,960 miles (5,150-6,370 km) below the earth's surface and mainly consists of iron, nickel and some lighter elements (probably sulphur, carbon, oxygen, silicon and potassium. The temperature in the inner core is about 9032 - 10832 F (5000-6000 C). Because of the high pressure, the core is solid. The average density of the core is about 15g/cm . The outer core is at 1,800 - 3,200 miles (2,890-5,150 km) below the earth's surface. The outer core is liquid and mainly consists of iron, some nickel and about 10% sulphur and oxygen. The temperature in the outer core is about 7200 - 9032 F (4000-5000 C). The density of the outer core is between the 10g/cm and 12,3g/cm . The outer core and inner core together cause the earth's magnetism. What Influence does the Core have? Because the core is so hot, it radiates a natural heat to the upper layers. Because of this a current of heat comes into being. Those are also known as the convection currents. The convection currents cause the movement of the tectonic plates. This movement is called plate tectonics. The outer core and the inner core together cause the earth's magnetism. Because the earth rotates, the outer core spins, the inner core doesn't spin because it's solid. This gives a kind of dynamo effect and causes the earth's magnetism. Magnetism has been used by sailors to find their way on earth for thousands and thousands of years. Magnetism also influences electro-particles outside the atmosphere of the earth, up to more than 37,000 miles (60,000 km) into space Home | Formation of the Earth | Earth's Structure | Life on Earth Earth's Structure: Structure | The Core | The Mantle | The Crust | The Atmosphere | The Influence of the Sun and Moon Plate Tectonics | Earthquakes | Volcanoes Copyright 2000, ThinkQuest team C003124 All rights reserved. This is called ionization. It results in negatively charged electrons, and positively charged ions. Unlike the other states of matter, the charged particles in a plasma will react strongly to electric and magnetic fields (i.e. electromagnetic fields). If a plasma loses heat, the ions will re-form into a gas, emitting the energy which had caused them to ionize. Title: How to Perform Number 2 of the Randori No Kata in Tomiki Aikido Introduction: Randori No Kata is a set of prearranged techniques used in Tomiki Aikido training. It consists of five attacking movements, which the defender responds to with specific Aikido techniques. This tutorial will focus on performing number 2 of the Randori No Kata as the defender. By following these steps, you'll learn how to execute an effective defense against a right-handed punch (tsuki). Step 1: Assume the Starting Position Start by facing your partner at a distance of about two arm lengths. Both partners should be standing naturally, feet shoulder-width apart, with their weight evenly distributed between both legs. Your hands should hang loosely by your sides. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Maintain eye contact with your partner throughout the exercise. * Keep your body relaxed but prepared to move quickly when needed. * Pay attention to your partner's posture and breathing patterns. Step 2: Prepare for the Attack As soon as your partner begins to raise their right hand, prepare yourself for the incoming attack. Pivot slightly on the balls of your feet so that you can react swiftly if necessary. Raise your left hand palm upwards, placing it just below your chest level, while keeping your right hand close to your waist area, also palm up. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Use this moment before the actual attack to practice situational awareness and anticipate your opponent's actions. * Make sure your arms are positioned correctly - high enough to protect your upper body yet low enough to maintain balance. Step 3: Execute Tenkan Movement When your partner thrusts their right fist towards you, pivot smoothly on your left foot using a tenkan movement. Rotate your entire body approximately 90 degrees counterclockwise while simultaneously lowering your center of gravity. As you perform the tenkan motion, guide your partner's outstretched arm across your body using your left hand. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Focus on maintaining good posture during the turn; keep your back straight and avoid hunching over. * Use minimal force when guiding your partner's arm - rely more on proper technique than brute strength. Step 4: Complete Irimi Nage Technique Continue rotating your body until you face your original direction again, pulling your partner along with you due to the grip established on their wrist. Simultaneously lift your right leg and plant it firmly next to your left foot, causing your partner to lose their balance further. Lower your bodyweight onto your planted right foot, lifting your partner off the ground and executing irimi nage (entering throw) by extending your arms fully and tossing them behind you. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Timing is crucial here - ensure that you initiate the throw once your partner has committed to the tsuki (punch) and lost balance during the tenkan movement. * Practice smooth transitions between movements to maximize effectiveness and minimize strain on your own body. Step 5: Return to the Starting Position After completing the throw, release your grip on your partner's wrist and return to the starting position described in Step 1. Be ready to respond to another attack or reset as needed. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always remain aware of your surroundings and maintain control over your movements. * Continuously work on improving your form and refining your execution of the kata. To make the most out of this period, it's essential to be well-prepared, organized, and engaged in both your studies and extracurricular activities. This comprehensive guide will provide you with actionable steps and valuable insights to help ensure a successful middle school year. ### Step 1: Set Clear Goals Explanation: ------------- Start by setting clear and realistic goals for yourself. These objectives can range from improving grades, developing new skills, or participating in clubs and sports teams. Having specific targets will give direction to your efforts throughout the year and motivate you to stay focused. Key Tips & Guidelines: --------------------- * Make sure your goals are SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) * Break down larger goals into smaller milestones * Regularly review progress towards these goals and adjust as necessary ### Step 2: Develop a Strong Study Routine Explanation: ------------- Establishing a consistent study routine is crucial for maintaining good grades and mastering challenging concepts. By dedicating set times for studying every day, you create structure that promotes productivity and efficient learning. Key Tips & Guidelines: --------------------- * Allocate dedicated blocks of time for studying each subject based on their difficulty level and workload * Find a quiet space free from distractions where you can focus solely on academics * Use tools such as calendars, planners, or apps to keep track of assignments, deadlines, and exam dates ### Step 3: Improve Organizational Skills [MOT trying to remember the exact wording of MAR's dialogue]. [goes to bring balls back to the couch.] Title: How to Build Walls Efficiently in Age of Empires 3 Introduction: Walls are essential structures in Age of Empires 3 that provide defense, resources, and organization for your empire. Building them efficiently can significantly enhance your gameplay experience by saving time, reducing costs, and improving overall strategic positioning. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every crucial aspect of building walls effectively, offering expert advice and actionable steps along the way. Step 1: Choosing the Right Location Select an ideal location for constructing your wall, keeping in mind factors such as terrain features (hills, forests), resource distribution (gold mines, woodcutting sites), and proximity to enemy bases or expansion points. A well-placed wall can offer significant advantages early in the game when resources are scarce and military units may be limited. Moreover, placing walls near natural barriers like cliffs and rivers provides additional protection without requiring extra resources. Key Tip: Avoid placing walls too close to your Town Center, as this could hinder its growth and limit future expansions. Step 2: Selecting the Appropriate Wall Type There are two main types of walls available in Age of Empires 3 - Stone Walls and Palisade Walls. Each has unique strengths and weaknesses depending upon various circumstances. Stone Walls: These sturdier structures have higher hitpoints than palisades but take longer to build and cost more lumber. They're best suited for late-game scenarios where economy is stable and stronger defenses are needed against heavy artillery attacks. Palisade Walls: Quick and cheap to produce, these wooden fences serve as excellent temporary defensive measures during the initial stages of the game. However, they lack durability compared to stone walls and succumb quickly under sustained attack from siege weapons. Guideline: Generally speaking, start with palisade walls at the beginning of the match and gradually replace them with stone ones once you reach later ages and amass sufficient resources. Step 3: Optimizing Resource Management To optimize resource management while erecting walls, make sure to queue multiple wall segments simultaneously. This strategy allows workers to continue gathering resources uninterrupted while others handle construction tasks. Additionally, ensure there's always enough idle labor force ready to begin new constructions immediately after completing previous ones. Key Tips: - Keep track of both current and potential threats. If no immediate threat exists, focus on expanding economic infrastructure instead of investing heavily in defense. - Consider utilizing Trading Posts strategically for income generation, which can help fund larger scale wall projects. Step 4: Strategic Positioning and Design Design your walls considering their purpose, whether protecting vital resources, controlling access points, or creating kill zones. For instance, surrounding valuable mining camps or production buildings enhances security, whereas gating choke points restricts opponents' movement and facilitates coordinated counterattacks. Remember, though, overusing walls might lead to unnecessary expenditure, limiting technological advancement and army size. Thus, strive for balance between offense and defense throughout the game. Step 5: Timely Upgrades and Maintenance Upgrading walls increases their hitpoints and resistance against different unit types. Regular maintenance ensures maximum efficiency of your defenses. Neglecting either may result in weakened defenses susceptible to breaches. Conclusion: Efficient wall building involves careful planning, smart resourcing, strategic placement, timely upgrades, and regular maintenance. By following our detailed guide, players should now possess a solid understanding of how to incorporate efficient wall building techniques into their Age of Empires 3 strategies. Happy conquering! One garden for you and one garden for me said Tam. Mean, Median, Mode, and Range The mean is the same as the average. Add up the series of numbers and divide by the number of numbers in the list. Example: Find the mean of 4, 6, 10, 4. Step 1: Add all the numbers = 24 Step 2: dividing 24 by the number of numbers in the list. 24 4=6 the mean or average of 4, 6, 10, 4 is 6 The "median" is the "middle" value in the list of numbers. To find the median, your numbers have to be listed in numerical order,(from least to greatest) so you may have to rewrite your list first. Example : A student has gotten the following grades on his tests: 60, 70, 80, 90, and 100 The median grade is 80 The "mode" is the value that occurs most often. If no number is repeated, then there is no mode for the list. Find the mode of 4,6,4 and 10 Example . First you list the numbers in order . 4, 4, 6, 10 Then you look to see which number repeats the most . 4 repeats twice, which means it's the mode is 4. The range of a set of data(numbers) is the difference between the highest and lowest values in the set. Example: find the range of 4, 6, 4, 10 step-1 Arrange the numbers in order by size 4, 4, 6, 10 step-2 Subtract the smallest number from the largest number. 10- 4 = 6 6 is the Range You're going to go play with Devon okay? Current Size: 100% Find the books that are just right for you. Here are two good ways to figure out the reading level of a book and find the best books for your age and reading ability. |The "Goldilocks Method" for K-3rd grade |The "Five Finger Method" for 4th through 6th grades Do you remember Goldilocks' quest to find the porridge, chair and bed that were "just right"? Selecting a book can sometimes feel the same way. Just as Goldilocks found that some porridges were too hot or too cold and others were just right, we often have difficulty finding books that are "just right" and not too hard or too easy. When you are reading a book you can try asking yourself these questions to help you find a book that is "just right" for you. TOO EASY BOOKS - Have you read this book many times before? - Do you know and understand almost every word? - Can you read it smoothly and fluently without much practice or effort? JUST RIGHT BOOKS - Is this book new to you? - Are there a few words per page that you don't recognize or know the meaning of instantly? - Can someone help you if you reach a tough spot? TOO HARD BOOKS - Are there more than a few words on a page that you don't recognize or know the meaning of? - When you read, are you struggling and does it sound choppy? - Is everyone busy and unable to help you if you hit a tough spot? The Five Finger Method is one way to test a book before you spend too much time with it and get frustrated. - First choose the book you think you would like to read. - Find a page of text somewhere in the middle of the book. Find a page with lots of text (words) and few or no pictures. - Begin to read the page. It is best to read the page aloud or in a whisper if possible while doing the test so you can hear the places where you have difficulty. - Each time you come to a word you don't know, hold one finger up. - If you have all five fingers up before you get to the end of the page, wave the book "good-bye". It is probably too difficult for you right now. If you need help finding a book, ask your librarian. - If you have no fingers up when you finish the page, then the book may be an easy read for you. - If you have less than five fingers but more than one or two fingers up when you finish reading the page, the book may be just what you need to grow as a reader. And the same thing would happen. - If I were to apply a force at this point, maybe I could - break the nail off the back of the clock, or something, but I - won't rotate this needle or this rule, or whatever you - want to call it. - But if I would apply a force here, I would rotate the ruler - around the pivot point. - And this force that's applied a distance away from the pivot - point, or we could say from the axis of rotation, or the - center of mass. - That's called torque. - And torque, the letter for torque is this Greek, I think - that's tau, it's a curvy T. - And torque is defined as force times distance. - And what force and what distance is it? - It's the force that's perpendicular to the object. - I guess you could say to the distance vector. - If this is the distance vector-- let me do it in a - different color. - If this is the distance vector, the component of the - force is perpendicular to this distance vector. - And this is torque. - And so what are its units? - Well, force is newtons, and distance is meters, so this is - newton meters. - And you're saying, hey Sal, newtons times meters, force - times distance, that looks an awful lot like work. - And it's very important to realize that this isn't work, - and that's why we won't call this joules. - Because in work, what are we doing? - We are translating an object. - If this is an object, and I'm applying a force, I'm taking - the force over the distance in the same - direction as the force. - Here the distance and the force are - parallel to each other. - You could say the distance vector and the force vector - are in the same direction. - Of course, that's translational. - The whole object is just moving. - It's not rotating or anything. - In the situation of torque, let me switch colors. - The distance vector, this is the distance from the fulcrum - or the pivot point of the center of mass, to where I'm - applying the force. - This distance vector is perpendicular to the force - that's being applied. - So torque and work are fundamentally two different - things, even though their units are the same. - And this is a little bit of notational. - This distance is often called the moment arm distance. - And I don't know where that came from. - Maybe one of you all can write me a message saying where it - did come from. - And often in some of your physics classes they'll often - call torque as a moment. - But we'll deal with the term torque. - And that's more fun, because eventually we can understand - concepts like torque horsepower in cars. - So let's do a little bit of math, hopefully I've given you - a little bit of intuition. - So let's say I had this ruler. - And let's say that this is its pivot point right here. - So it would rotate around that point. - It's nailed to the wall or something. - And let's say that I apply a force-- Let's say the moment - arm distance. - So let's say this distance, let me do it - in different color. - Let's say that this distance right here is 10 meters. - And I were to apply a force of 5 newtons perpendicular to the - distance vector, or to dimension of the moment arm, - you could view it either way. - So torque is pretty easy in this situation. - Torque is going to be equal to the force, 5 newtons, times - the distance, 10. - So it would be 50 newton meters. - And you're probably saying, well, Sal, how do I know if - this torque is going to be positive or negative? - And this is where there's just a general arbitrary convention - in physics. - And it's good to know. - If you're rotating clockwise torque is negative. - Let me go the other way. - If you were rotating counterclockwise, like we were - in this example, rotating counterclockwise, the opposite - direction of which a clock would move in. - Torque is positive. - And if you rotate clockwise the other - way, torque is negative. - So clockwise is negative. - And I'm not going to go into the whole cross product and - the linear algebra of torque right now, because I think - that's a little bit beyond the scope. - But we'll do that once we do more - mathematically intensive physics. - But, so, good enough. - There's a torque of 50 newton meters. - And that's all of the torque that is acting - on this object . - So it's going to rotate in this direction. - And we don't have the tools yet to figure out how quickly - it will rotate. - But we know it will rotate. - And that's vaguely useful. - At present the little ape's innate aptitude for mimicry would be sufficient to familiarize him with Tarzan's ways and weapons, and so the ape-man swung off into the jungle, his new rope coiled over one shoulder, while little Gazan hopped about the clearing dragging the old one after him in childish glee. [mother continues to make loud noises with the kitchen appliance] Title: How to Find a "How To" on Wikihow Introduction: Wikihow is an online community-created database of how-to guides covering a wide variety of topics. From cooking to technology, you can find just about anything on this platform. This tutorial will guide you through finding a specific "how to" article on Wikihow with ease. By following these steps, you'll be able to access valuable information quickly and efficiently. Step 1: Open your web browser Start by launching your preferred web browser such as Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge or Safari. Using a reliable internet connection ensures smooth navigation throughout the process. Step 2: Navigate to Wikihow homepage Type "[www.wikihow.com](http://www.wikihow.com)" into the address bar at the top of your browser window and press Enter. The Wikihow home page should load almost immediately if your internet speed is good enough. Key Tip: If you want to go directly to a particular language version (for example Spanish), replace 'www.' with 'es.', like so: '[es.wikihow.com](http://es.wikihow.com)'. Step 3: Use the search function On the upper right corner of the page, there is a magnifying glass icon labeled "Search". Click on it. A text box will appear where you can type what you are looking for. For instance, if you need help learning how to tie a tie, simply enter that phrase. Guideline: Be as specific as possible when entering keywords. Vague terms may yield irrelevant results. Step 4: Analyze the suggested articles As soon as you hit Search, several related "how tos" will pop up underneath the search bar. Browse through them briefly to see if any match exactly what you're looking for. Step 5: Access the full list of results If none of the suggestions meet your needs, look below the initial results for a link reading "See more results...". Clicking on it will take you to a new page displaying a comprehensive set of instructional articles relevant to your query. Step 6: Refine Your Results (Optional) Should the returned list still seem too broad, refine your search using filters provided above the result headings. You can filter based on categories, difficulty level, length of the article, rating etc., making your quest even more targeted. Step 7: Select your desired "how to" From here, scan the titles and brief descriptions until you find the most fitting one. Once decided, click on the title to proceed to the actual guide. Key Tip: Pay attention to the number of steps, time required, and ratings given by other users. These factors can give insight into whether the article suits your preference and available time frame. Step 8: Read and Apply Instructions Once redirected to the chosen guide, read carefully and thoroughly from start to finish before attempting any actions described. Remember, practice makes perfect! Conclusion: By now, you should have successfully found a "how to" guide tailored specifically to your needs on Wikihow. Always remember to crosscheck multiple sources to ensure accuracy and comprehensiveness of the information obtained. "Nationality" can take on different meanings depending on the country, language and culture in question. For example, someone of Italian descent may be called "Italian" even if the individual, a member of the Italian diaspora, has no right to an Italian passport and citizenship. In past centuries, the usual meaning of "nationality" was not political, but rather meant membership in an ethnic group. It's about building genuine relationships, contributing positively to your community, and developing strong interpersonal skills that will benefit you throughout your life. This guide provides practical steps towards becoming a well-liked and respected student, focusing on authentic actions rather than superficial popularity tactics. Title: How to Choose a Good Lab Partner Introduction: Lab work is an essential part of many academic programs, particularly in the sciences. A good lab partner can make this process enjoyable, productive, and educational, while a bad one can cause stress, confusion, and poor grades. Choosing a suitable lab partner requires careful consideration and communication. This tutorial will guide you through selecting a competent, reliable, and compatible lab partner. Step 1: Assess Your Learning Style Before choosing a lab partner, understand your learning preferences. Are you more visual or auditory? Do you prefer working alone or with others? Identifying these aspects allows you to find someone who complements your approach rather than clashes with it. By understanding yourself first, you'll be better equipped to assess potential partners. Key Tip: Take advantage of personality tests like Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) or VARK questionnaire to gain insights into your learning style. Step 2: Evaluate Potential Partners Observe your classmates during lectures and discussions to identify those displaying strong scientific knowledge, critical thinking skills, and enthusiasm for the subject matter. These traits are crucial indicators of a responsible and dedicated lab partner. Key Guideline: Pay attention to students asking insightful questions and engaging actively in conversations related to laboratory topics. Step 3: Consider Compatibility Consider whether a potential partner shares similar goals, values, and ethics as yours. It is easier to collaborate when both parties agree upon objectives and methods. Moreover, ensure that they respect deadlines and maintain academic integrity. Key Tip: Discuss previous group projects or study sessions to gauge their commitment level and ethical standards. Step 4: Communication Skills Effective verbal and written communication forms the backbone of any successful partnership. Observe if your prospective partner articulates ideas clearly, listens attentively, and responds thoughtfully. Also, check if they have solid note-taking abilities and record data meticulously. Key Guideline: Listen carefully to how they express themselves during group activities or casual interactions - do they speak concisely yet thoroughly? Step 5: Schedule Coordination Timely completion of tasks relies heavily on coordinating schedules effectively. Determine whether your possible partner has consistent availability, especially around major assignment due dates and exam periods. Key Tip: Establish upfront what times work best for meetings and experiment setups; then stick to them consistently. Step 6: Divide Responsibilities Fairly Discuss dividing responsibilities early on based on individual strengths and interests but aim for balance. Both members should contribute equally towards achieving shared targets. Key Guideline: Create a list detailing everyone's roles and expectations at the beginning of each project. Regularly review and adjust as necessary throughout the semester. Step 7: Initiate Conversation Approach your chosen candidate directly and start a conversation about teaming up. Express your intentions regarding the collaboration and ask for their thoughts. If they seem hesitant or uninterested, consider moving onto another option. Key Tip: Be polite and positive during initial talks; remember, first impressions matter! Conclusion: Selecting a good lab partner entails thorough self-reflection, observation, evaluation, and communication. While finding the perfect match might take time, following these steps increases chances of forming a fruitful alliance leading to enhanced learning outcomes and enriching experiences. [activity is essentially ended as MOT and BLA leave momentarily] Title: How to Adjust Opacity in InDesign Adobe InDesign is a powerful desktop publishing application that allows you to create stunning designs for print and digital media. One essential skill when working with this software is adjusting opacity - the level of transparency applied to an object or text. This tutorial will guide you through the process of adjusting opacity in InDesign in detail, providing helpful insights along the way. **Step 1:** Open Adobe InDesign Start by launching your installed version of Adobe InDesign. If you don't have it yet, download and install from the official Adobe website. Once opened, select "Create New" under the "Welcome Screen," or choose "File" > "New" > "Document." Set up your document as needed. **Step 2:** Create or Select Objects/Texts To begin adjusting opacity, first, create or select objects (shapes, frames) or texts within your layout. For existing elements, simply click them using the Selection Tool (black arrow). To add new ones, pick tools like Rectangle Frame Tool, Ellipse Frame Tool, Text Tool, etc., then draw or type over your canvas. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - When creating multiple objects, ensure they are grouped if you want to change their opacity simultaneously ("Object" > "Group"). - Keep track of layers containing these elements; changing opacities may affect other parts of the design depending on layer positioning. **Step 3:** Access Transparency Panel With your element(s) selected, head towards the top menu bar and click on "Window" > "Transparency." Alternatively, press `Shift + F10` shortcut keys together to open the Transparency panel directly. Here, you can modify various attributes including Fill Color, Stroke Color, Blending Mode, and most importantly, Opacity. **Step 4:** Locate the Opacity Slider Inside the Transparency panel, look for the 'Opacity' section which contains a slider ranging from 0% (fully transparent) to 100% (completely solid). The default value is usually set at 100%. **Step 5:** Adjust Opacity Level Drag the slider leftwards to decrease the opacity percentage or rightwards to increase it. As you do so, observe changes happening live in your workspace - lower values make the selected item more translucent while higher values enhance its visibility. **Step 6:** Input Specific Value (Optional) If required, input specific numerical values instead of dragging the slider. Click inside the white box beside the word 'Opacity,' then enter any number between 0 and 100 followed by '%'. Press Enter or Return afterward. **Step 7:** Verify Changes & Finalize Design After setting your preferred opacity level, check whether everything looks good across all pages and layers. Remember, reduced opacity might reveal underlying content, affecting overall readability and aesthetics. Make final tweaks accordingly before saving or exporting your project. By mastering the art of adjusting opacity in Adobe InDesign, you gain greater control over designing visually appealing documents that stand out! This goin' is an e o n. [Mother and Nina had walked down a steep hill and Nina had slipped down] Creating your own desktop backgrounds with Microsoft Paint is an easy way to personalize your computer and make it truly unique. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating custom desktop backgrounds using this built-in Windows tool. **Step 1: Open Microsoft Paint** To begin, click on the Start menu or press the Windows key on your keyboard, type "Paint" into the search bar, and then select the Paint app from the list of results. **Step 2: Set up the canvas size** Before you start creating your design, you need to set up the canvas size that matches your screen resolution. To do so, go to the Home tab, click on 'Resize' under Image, change the units to pixels, and input the width and height according to your display settings (for example, 1920 x 1080). Keep the 'Maintain aspect ratio' box checked if you want to preserve the original proportions. Click OK once done. *Key tip:* If you are unsure about your screen resolution, right-click anywhere on your desktop > Display Settings > Resolution. The recommended resolution should be displayed there. **Step 3: Choose your background color** Select the Fill bucket icon from the Tools section and choose the color you would like as your background by clicking on the Color 1 square at the bottom left corner of the window. Once chosen, click anywhere on the canvas to fill it with your selected color. **Step 4: Create your design** Now comes the fun part - designing! You can utilize various tools available in Microsoft Paint such as brushes, pencils, shapes, text, and more. Experiment with different colors, sizes, and opacities to create your masterpiece. Here are some ideas to get started: - Draw simple shapes or lines - Add text with custom font styles, colors, and sizes - Use gradients for smooth transitions between hues - Import images (via Insert > Picture) to incorporate them into your design Remember, less can often be more when it comes to desktop backgrounds; ensure that any elements added don't obstruct visibility while working on other applications. **Step 5: Save your work properly** Once satisfied with your creation, save it appropriately for use as a desktop background. Go to File > Save As and navigate to your preferred directory where you wish to store the image file. Select .jpg or .png format since these formats support transparent backgrounds if needed. Name your file accordingly, check the correct location, and hit Save. *Guideline*: Always keep an editable version of your artwork saved separately in case you need to modify it later. **Step 6: Apply your new desktop background** Finally, to apply your newly created desktop background, open the Display Settings either via Control Panel or directly from the Settings app (Win + I shortcut), scroll down to find 'Background', click on Browse, locate your freshly made image, and confirm changes. Congratulations! With these steps, you have successfully crafted your own unique desktop background using Microsoft Paint. Enjoy showing off your creativity every time you turn on your PC! This addictive game challenges you to find as many words as possible on a 4x4 grid of letters within a given time limit. To get Wordament for Windows 8, simply follow these steps: Step 1: Open the Microsoft Store To start, open the Microsoft Store app on your Windows 8 device. The store provides various apps, games, music, movies, and TV shows. It's an all-in-one platform where you can discover new applications and manage existing ones. By opening this application first, you are one step closer to getting Wordament installed on your device. Step 2: Search for Wordament Click or tap on the search icon (magnifying glass) located at the top right corner of the window. Type 'Wordament' into the search bar without any spaces, then hit Enter or click the magnifying glass icon next to the search box. Make sure there are no typos while searching; otherwise, you may end up with irrelevant results. Careful attention to detail will save you time and effort by avoiding potential errors. Key Tip: If you cannot remember the exact name of the app, try using broader terms like 'word puzzle games.' You might stumble upon other interesting alternatives during your quest for Wordament! Step 3: Select Wordament from Results Once you have entered the correct term, you should see multiple suggestions appearing under the 'Apps' section. Locate and select 'Wordament - Free Online Multiplayer Crossword Game' developed by Microsoft Studios. Remember, selecting the wrong item could lead you astray from our goal. Be attentive when browsing through the list of options presented by the Microsoft Store. Step 4: Install Wordament Now that you've found the correct app, click on its title or press Enter to proceed to its information page. Here, you will find details about the game, such as screenshots, reviews, ratings, and more importantly, an install button. Click on the blue 'Install' button near the center of the screen to download and install Wordament on your device. Patience is essential here since the installation process depends on factors like internet speed and file size. Step 5: Launch Installed App After successful installation, locate the newly added tile named 'Wordament' among your other tiles on the Start Screen. Don't worry if you don't see it immediately after installation - sometimes, it takes a moment for the system to update the display. Once visible, click or tap on the Wordament tile to launch the game. Now you're ready to test your vocabulary skills against players worldwide! Title: How to Clean a Stainless Steel Stove **Things You'll Need:** * Dish soap * Soft sponge or cloth * White vinegar * Olive oil (optional) * Baking soda (optional) * Microfiber cloth **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** Before you start cleaning your stainless steel stove, gather all the necessary materials. This will make the process more efficient and ensure that you don't miss any steps. **Step 2: Remove Loose Debris** Start by removing loose debris from the stove. Use a dry microfiber cloth or soft brush to wipe away crumbs, dust, and other particles. This initial cleanup will make the following steps easier and more effective. **Step 3: Apply Dish Soap** Apply a few drops of dish soap to a damp soft sponge or cloth. Avoid using abrasive scrubbers as they can scratch the surface of the stove. The dish soap will help break down grease and grime. **Step 4: Scrub the Stovetop** Gently scrub the stovetop with the soapy sponge or cloth. Pay special attention to areas with heavy buildup. Remember, gentle pressure is enough; there's no need to press hard. **Step 5: Rinse With Water** Rinely the stovetop with clean water to remove the soap suds. Be sure to remove all soap residue, as it can leave streaks behind. **Step 6: Dry Thoroughly** Dry the stove thoroughly with a clean towel or microfiber cloth. A wet stove can quickly accumulate water spots and fingerprints. **Step 7: Deal With Tough Stains** For stubborn stains, create a paste made from baking soda and warm water. Apply this paste directly onto the stain and let it sit for about 10-15 minutes before wiping it off. If needed, repeat this process until the stain disappears. **Step 8: Shine With Vinegar** To give your stove an extra shine, spray some white vinegar onto its surface. Then, immediately wipe it off with a clean, dry microfiber cloth. The acidity in the vinegar will help restore the original luster of the stainless steel. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always work in the direction of the grain when cleaning a stainless steel stove. Going against the grain could result in visible scratches. * Never use bleach or harsh chemicals on a stainless steel stove, as these substances can cause discoloration and damage. * Regular maintenance is crucial in keeping your stainless steel stove looking new. Try to wipe down the stove after every use. * For daily touch-ups, simply use a dampened microfiber cloth. This quick routine will prevent dirt and grime from building up over time. * To maintain the longevity of your appliance, consider applying a thin layer of olive oil once a month. This protective coating will shield the stove from smudges and corrosion. Remember we caught him one day, and he was roaring at the people RRR, huh? Did you sing any songs in school today? Title: How to Attract Barn Owls Introduction: Barn owls are fascinating creatures that can be beneficial to have around your property. They are skilled hunters that can help control pests such as rodents. Attracting barn owls involves providing them with suitable habitats and food sources. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the steps to create an inviting environment for these remarkable birds. Step 1: Understand Barn Owl Biology and Behavior Before attempting to attract barn owls, it is essential to understand their biology and behavior. Barn owls are medium-sized nocturnal birds with heart-shaped faces and distinctive white underparts. They primarily feed on small mammals like mice and voles. Knowing this information will help you provide appropriate habitats and food sources for them. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Learn about the local barn owl population and their habitat preferences. * Familiarize yourself with their calls and behaviors to distinguish them from other owl species. Step 2: Provide Suitable Habitats To attract barn owls, you need to offer them a safe place to roost and nest. Installing a barn owl box is an effective way to provide a suitable habitat. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Place the box at least ten feet above ground level, ideally on a tree or pole. * Face the entrance hole southward to avoid prevailing winds and direct sunlight. * Install the box before the breeding season (usually between February and July) to give the owls enough time to claim it as their territory. Step 3: Create a Foraging Area Barn owls require open spaces to hunt for prey. Creating a foraging area near your property can increase the chances of attracting them. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Maintain short grassy areas or meadows adjacent to wooded or shrubby habitats. * Remove vegetation that obstructs visibility, making it difficult for owls to spot prey. * Avoid using pesticides and rodenticides, which can harm both the owls and their primary food source. Step 4: Protect Barn Owls From Predators Predator protection is crucial for ensuring the safety of barn owls and their young ones. Erecting barriers around the nesting site can deter predators such as raccoons and snakes. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Use sheet metal or hardware cloth to cover potential entry points for predators. * Keep pets away from the nesting site to prevent disturbances and attacks. * Monitor the nesting site regularly to ensure its integrity and address any issues promptly. Step 5: Observe and Enjoy Your New Visitors Once you've created a welcoming environment for barn owls, sit back and enjoy observing these magnificent birds. Remember to maintain a respectful distance to minimize disturbance. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Invest in binoculars or a spotting scope for closer views without intrusion. * Limit artificial lighting at night to preserve the owls' natural hunting patterns. * Share your experience with friends and family but discourage them from approaching the nesting site. Conclusion: Attracting barn owls requires patience, dedication, and understanding of their needs. By following the steps outlined in this guide, you can contribute to preserving these incredible birds while also reaping the benefits they bring to your property. Happy birdwatching! Do Buddhists believe in god or gods? The Buddha would not say if gods exist or not, although gods play a part in some Buddhist stories. If someone asked the Buddha, "Do gods exist?" he maintained a "noble silence". That is, he would not confirm or deny. Buddhists do not believe that people should look to gods to save them or bring them enlightenment. Rather individuals should work out their own path the best they can. Reading to Learn: Comprehension Strategies Rationale: In order to gain insight while reading one must be able to comprehend. However, students often fail to comprehend (and remember) what they have read. Because of this, teachers have been given comprehension strategies that can be taught to children in order to give them a helping hand. One such strategy is called story-grammar. The following activity will show children how to use the story-grammar strategy to help them comprehend what they are reading. Materials: You will need two copies of ten different conventional stories (The Orphan Kittens by Margaret Wise Brown, Oliver Finds a Home by Justin Korman, Bambi by Felix Salten, Paul Revere by Irwin Shapiro, etc.) that the children will find interesting, Charlotte's Web by E.B. White (with highlighted passages for modeling), a question-answer sheet, and a pencil. Procedure: 1. Explain to the boy's and girl's that today they will be learning a strategy that will show them how to go about comprehending what they read. 2. Have each child come up and choose a book from your selection (There should be two children throughout the room with the same book). Next pass out two question-answer sheets to each child. Tell the children to put the book and one of the question-answer sheets under their desk to be used at a later date. 3. Explain to the children that you want them to listen to you read Charlotte's Web. Tell them that after you read for a few minutes, you will stop, think about the first question on the sheet, and then answer it. Explain that you want them to answer the same question at their desk. You will do this throughout the reading of the book. 4. Now begin reading. After reading a few paragraphs (Read only the highlighted passages for modeling) ask the children to answer the first question: Who are the main characters? Give them two or three minutes to answer, and then read on. After you have read a few more passages, ask them to answer the second question: Where and when did the story take place? Continue this until you have read the entire story and the children have answered the other three questions consisting of What did the main characters do?, How did the story end?, and How did the main character feel? Be sure to let the children know there is no right or wrong answer for the question How did the main character feel? because it s is an open-ended question (Depends on readers interpretation of the story). Now have a class discussion about their answers. 5. The second part of the lesson requires the children to read silently (which is very good for a lot of reading skills such as fluency and comprehension) at their desk for ten minutes. (Model reading silent by telling the students to read by thinking the words in their head without saying them out loud). Ask the children to take out the book that they choose earlier along with the second question-answer sheet. Explain to them how you will set a timer for ten minutes, during which time they are to be reading the book they choose. When the ten minutes are up, ask the children to answer the first question on the sheet in front of them. Give them ample time to reflect on what they have read and then set the timer again. Do this throughout the entire book. (You may want to choose shorter stories or treat this as a daily but weekly, meaning they work on the same books all week, assignment). 7. When the children have finished the book and answered all the questions, have them pair up with the other person in the room who read the same book. Explain how you want them to discuss their answers with each other. If there is a disagreement among them, tell them to talk it over to see why. This will help them see how someone else came to their conclusions. Don't forget to read silently along with the children. (You may want to have one of your students read the same book that you are reading and then discuss it with them. If you do this, write your answers in their language). (This will keep them from feeling over-powered). 8. For a review you can ask the children what five questions they should ask themselves, while reading, to help them comprehend what they have read. References: Pressley, M., Johnson, C. J., Symons, S., McGoldrick, J. A., & Kurity, J. A. (1989). "Strategies That Improve Children s Memory and Comprehension of Text. The Elementary School Journal, (1990, Pp. 3-32). Click here to return to Elucidations [mother helps Nina put on the dress.] Why we can't stay behind that girl? You want to hear a little shy cat? Everybody's awful crowded on that side of the table. Adding or subtracting fractions using Vedic math is a simple and efficient method that can help you perform calculations quickly and accurately. This ancient Indian technique has been passed down through generations and has gained popularity due to its simplicity and effectiveness. The following steps will guide you through adding or subtracting fractions using Vedic math: **Step 1:** Identify the type of operation Determine whether you need to add or subtract the given fractions. This initial step might seem obvious; however, ensuring you are performing the correct mathematical operation is crucial before proceeding with further calculations. **Step 2:** Verify same denominators Check if both fractions have the same denominator (the bottom number). If they do, continue to Step 3. Otherwise, find a common multiple between the two denominators by applying Nikhilam method (a fundamental concept in Vedic math) and convert them into equivalent fractions having the same denominator. For example, consider the fractions 5/6 and 7/9. Their least common multiple is 18. So, we convert these fractions as follows: * 5/6 becomes (5 x 3)/(6 x 3) = 15/18 * 7/9 remains unchanged since 9 is already a factor of our chosen LCM (18) Now, we have identical denominators, which makes the addition process more straightforward. **Key tip**: Keep an eye out for cancelable factors while converting the fractions during this step - this could simplify your subsequent work! **Step 3:** Perform the required arithmetic operation Once you've confirmed equal denominators, simply perform the specified operation (addition or subtraction) on the numerators (top numbers), leaving the denominator untouched. For instance, let us add the converted fractions from Step 2: 15/18 + 7/18 = 22/18 **Step 4:** Simplify the resultant fraction If possible, reduce the resulting fraction to its lowest terms by dividing both the numerator and denominator by their greatest common divisor (GCD). You may also recognize some familiar patterns like doubles, halves, etc., which would enable quicker simplification. In our previous example, we got 22/18. We notice that the GCD of 22 and 18 is 2. Hence, we divide both parts to get the final answer: 22 2 / 18 2 = 11/9 So, 5/6 + 7/9 equals 11/9 when expressed in its most straightforward form. **Guidelines**: * Always ensure you're clear about whether you should be adding or subtracting the given fractions. * Memorize basic multiplication tables up to 10x10 to make identifying common multiples easier. * Look for opportunities to simplify fractions throughout the calculation process, saving time and effort later on. * Familiarize yourself with various Vedic math techniques such as Nikhilam, Anurupyena, Ekadhika, Striktau, and Paravartya yojayet for enhanced understanding and mastery over the subject matter. By diligently practicing and incorporating these steps and guidelines, you'll become proficient at adding or subtracting fractions using Vedic math. Over time, this skill will allow you to solve complex problems swiftly and confidently. And you just play way down here. I'm going to do my a b c. Introduction to Particles Particles may seem a little foreign to you at first, but for the most part, they aren't too difficult to grasp. These particles are placed after a word (or phrase) and show its relationship (grammatical function) to the rest of the sentence. In other words, the particle itself isn't really translatable, but it tells you a lot about the function of the word it follows. The best way to learn to use them is to memorize useful examples and try them out for size! shows the main topic of the conversation. It may be helpful to think of it as "As for..." - anata wa yasashii. - You are nice. Makes "you" the main topic: "As for YOU, you are nice." sometimes the difference between wa and ga is hard to tell. Sometimes they can be used interchangeably with only a slight change in meaning. See next entry for more on this. - neko ga hen. - The cat is strange. Makes the "cat" the subject Comparing and (by Paul_b) The topic particle can easily be confused with the subject particle . That is because overrides , in other words, in a sentence something can very easily be both the topic and the subject of that sentence. In such cases the "disappears" and it looks like the is acting as a subject marker. Take this simple sentence. - watashi wa kurei desu. - I am Clay. "I" (that is the speaker, Clay) is the topic and now this is known, it won't be repeated unless the topic changes. What is the subject of the sentence? That's right - "I" watashi is. But because "I" is also the topic only the topic marker is used. Now we'll let Clay continue and say another sentence ... - neko ga suki desu. - (I) like cats. "cats" is the subject here. "I" is still the topic. He could have said "watashi wa neko ga suki desu." but that is unnecessary because he has already said "watashi wa" establishing the topic in the previous sentence. - hon o yomimashita. - (I) read a book. it makes "book" the object. If we were to say "I" it would be watashi wa at the beginning. - nihon ni ikimashou! - Let's go to Japan! There is movement going to Japan or shows time (at) - roku ji ni ikimashou! - Let's go at 6. - nihon de asobimashou! - Let's play (have fun) in Japan! Notice there is no movement See the "Particles and Conjunctions" guide for more on this. Do you have an iPhone/iPod Touch? You may be interested in this iPhone App for mastering Japanese Particles. It is produced by TJP's good friends over at JapanNewbie.com. It covers the basic particles plus those found on the JLPT N5 and N4 tests. Use the Study Mode and Quiz to test yourself. The company emerged from bankruptcy in 1998 and began to diversify It's output, launching imprints aimed at a variety of demographics and expanding It's cinematic offerings under the Marvel Studios banner. In 2007, Marvel began creating digital comics. In 2009, the Walt Disney Company purchased the owner company of Marvel Comics. oh, this [flat yellow triangle] is a magnet. Every day, the farmer used it to work in the fields, but one day after he finished, the farmer accidentally left the pitchfork in the field! The farmer was very worried, so he asked his neighbor, "Would you lend me your pitchfork, so I can finish my work?" The neighbor was very nice and said, "Yes, I will lend it to you." The farmer was very thankful. He returned the pitchfork the next day and said, "Next time, I will remember to bring my pitchfork with me." The neighbor nodded and said, "It's important to be prepared, and to be a good neighbor by lending and helping each other. We should all lend a helping hand, just as I did today." The moral of the story is that it is important to be prepared, but it is also important to be a good neighbor and lend a helping hand when others are in need! Title: How to Do Laundry When You've Never Done It Before Introduction: Laundry might seem like a daunting task if you're new to it, but with this comprehensive guide, you'll be able to tackle laundry confidently and efficiently. This article will walk you through sorting clothes, choosing the right settings on your washing machine, using detergent and fabric softener, drying clothes, and folding or hanging them up to put away. By following these steps, you can ensure that your clothes are clean, fresh, and well-cared for. Step 1: Sort Clothes Why: Sorting clothes by color and fabric type is essential to prevent colors from bleeding onto lighter garments and damaging delicate fabrics during the wash cycle. 1. Gather all the dirty clothes in one place. 2. Separate whites from colored clothing. If possible, further separate lights (pastels) from darks (navy blue, black). Delicate items such as lingerie and silk should also be separated into their own pile. 3. Check care labels on garments for specific washing instructions. Some items may require hand washing or dry cleaning only. 4. Remember to turn any garments with embellishments, such as sequins or embroidery, inside out to protect the decorative elements during washing. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Avoid mixing reds, purples, and other bright colors with white or light-colored garments, as they tend to bleed more than other hues. * Always read and adhere to the care label instructions on every item before laundering. Step 2: Choose the Right Settings on Your Washing Machine Why: Different types of loads and stains benefit from varying water temperatures and spin speeds. Understanding these options ensures optimal results while preserving the life of your garments. 1. Select the appropriate wash cycle based on load size and fabric type: * Regular Cycle: Suitable for cotton, denim, and durable synthetics; uses warm to hot water and high agitation. * Permanent Press Cycle: Ideal for blends containing some synthetic fibers; offers moderate agitation and warm water temperature. Foods are usually either slowly cooked or stewed. A typical Belarusian eats a light breakfast and two hearty meals, with dinner being the largest meal of the day. Wheat and rye breads are eaten in Belarus. Rye is more plentiful because conditions are too harsh for growing wheat. To show hospitality, a host will give an offering of bread and salt when greeting a guest or visitor. Popular drinks in Belarus include Russian wheat vodka and "kvass", Kvass is a drink made from fermented malted brown bread or rye flour. "Kvass" may also be added with sliced vegetables to create a cold soup called "okroshka". Kate bravely sustained it; she made it out. He decides that if every person does what is best for themself and their little circle of family and friends, then the country will do better. This is because every person knows a lot about their own situation (what they need and what they want, what works and what does not work), much more than the government knows. Now that you've gotten everything setup, let's write a program! Open up your favorite text editor and type in the following: Save your program (yes, that's a program!) as calc.rb (the .rb is what we usually put at the end of programs written in Ruby). Now run your program by typing ruby calc.rb into your command line. It should have put a 3 on your screen. See, programming isn't so hard, now is it? So what's going on in that program? I'm sure you can guess what the 1+2 does; our program is basically the same as: puts simply writes onto the screen whatever comes after it. In most programming languages (and Ruby is no exception) numbers without decimal points are called integers, and numbers with decimal points are usually called floating-point numbers, or more simply, floats. Here are some integers: 5 -205 9999999999999999999999999 0 And here are some floats: 54.321 0.001 -205.3884 0.0 In practice, most programs don't use floats; only integers. (After all, no one wants to look at 7.4 emails, or browse 1.8 webpages, or listen to 5.24 of their favorite songs...) Floats are used more for academic purposes (physics experiments and such) and for 3D graphics. Even most money programs use integers; they just keep track of the number of pennies! So far, we've got all the makings of a simple calculator. (Calculators always use floats, so if you want your computer to act just like a calculator, you should also use floats.) For addition and subtraction, we use + and -, as we saw. For multiplication, we use *, and for division we use /. Most keyboards have these keys in the numeric keypad on the far right side. If you have a smaller keyboard or a laptop, though, you can just use Shift 8 and / (same key as the ? key). Let's try to expand our calc.rb program a little. Type in the following and then run it. puts 1.0 + 2.0 puts 2.0 * 3.0 puts 5.0 - 8.0 puts 9.0 / 2.0 This is what the program returns: 3.0 6.0 -3.0 4.5 (The spaces in the program are not important; they just make the code easier to read.) Well, that wasn't too surprising. Now let's try it with integers: puts 1+2 puts 2*3 puts 5-8 puts 9/2 Mostly the same, right? 3 6 -3 4 Uh... except for that last one! But when you do arithmetic with integers, you'll get integer answers. When your computer can't get the "right" answer, it always rounds down. (Of course, 4 is the right answer in integer arithmetic for 9/2; just maybe not the answer you were expecting.) Perhaps you're wondering what integer division is good for. Well, let's say you're going to the movies, but you only have $9. Here in Portland, you can see a movie at the Bagdad for 2 bucks. How many movies can you see there? 9/2... 4 movies. 4.5 is definitely not the right answer in this case; they will not let you watch half of a movie, or let half of you in to see a whole movie... some things just aren't divisible. So now experiment with some programs of your own! If you want to write more complex expressions, you can use parentheses. For example: puts 5 * (12-8) + -15 puts 98 + (59872 / (13*8)) * -52 Write a program which tells you: Here's a tougher question: When you're done playing around with numbers, let's have a look at some letters. 2003-2009 Chris Pine She needs to have the boots on? You are racing a child that you are twice as fast as and you want to give him a head start in a race from one side of a field to the other. How much of a head start should you allow so that you will come close to finishing the race even? Draw a picture and label the length of the field as L and the head start distance as H. What is the length that the child needs to run? If your speed is S what is the child's speed? You want to finish at nearly equal times; the time that it takes you would be L/S (the distancerun divided by the speed run), what would it be for the child? Set those equal and solve for H. A young Arabian hunter had enticed a big mountain lion into one of the strong-meshed nets of stout palm fibres, then used for such purposes. His trained leopard or cheetah had drawn the beast from his lair, and by cunning devices had led him on until the unfortunate lion was half-entrapped. Just then, with a sudden swoop, a great golden eagle dashed down upon the preoccupied cheetah, and buried his talons in the leopard's head. But the weight of his victim was more than he had bargained for; the cheetah with a quick upward dash dislodged one of the great bird's talons, and, turning as quickly, caught the disengaged leg in his sharp teeth. At that instant the lion, springing at the struggling pair, started the fastenings of the net, which, falling upon the group, held all three prisoners. In many sentences with passive verbs, the actual direct object of the verb becomes the subject of the verb in a passive sentence: Someone is stealing Bill's car! (active) -----> is being stolen! (passive) Someone stole Bill's car! (active) -----> was stolen! (passive) Someone has stolen Bill's car! (active) -----> has been stolen! (passive) The passive can also be used in sentences that have two objects--a direct object and an indirect object. Because with both a direct and an indirect object can be written in two different ways (with and without to or for), the corresponding passive sentences can also be written in two Someone gave a million dollars to Bill. -----> A million dollars was given to Bill. Someone gave Bill a million Bill was given a million dollars a crazy letter to Cathy. -----> A crazy letter was sent to Cathy. Someone sent Cathy a crazy Cathy was sent a crazy letter. Someone will lend the money to you. -----> The money will be lent to you. Someone will lend you the You will be lent the money. Someone is passing a note to John. -----> A note is being passed to John. Someone is passing John a John is being passed a note. Someone has bought a present for our boss. -----> A present has been bought for our boss. Someone has bought our boss a present. -----> Our boss has been bought a present. Someone will leave the directions for you. -----> The directions will be left for you. Someone will leave you the You will be left the directions. Someone will draw a map for A map will be drawn for you. Someone will draw you a map.-----> You will be drawn a map. Some verbs show indirect objects with to, other verbs show with for, and a few verbs show indirect objects with both to and for. Here a few examples: |to + I.O. ||for + I.O. ||to / for + I.O. bring (something) to (someone) means 'bring and give it to (someone).' bring something for (someone) means 'bring that is intended for (someone). leave (something) to (someone) means for (someone) to have (something) as an inheritance.' leave (something) for (someone) means that is intended for (someone).' Disable or Delete Recent Document List in Microsoft Word or Excel Microsoft Word and Excel both have a feature that displays a list of recently opened documents. This can be convenient if you frequently work with the same files, but it may also pose a privacy concern if others have access to your computer. If you want to disable this feature or delete specific items from the recent document list, follow these steps: **For Microsoft Word:** 1. Open Microsoft Word. The first step is to launch the application where you wish to modify the settings. 2. Click on the "File" tab located at the top left corner of the window. This action will open the backstage view, which provides various options related to file management. 3. Look for "Recent Documents" on the left side panel. It should be near the bottom of the menu. 4. Hover over the "Recent Documents" option. Doing so will reveal two additional options - "Manage Recent Documents" and "Clear Unpinned Documents". 5. To disable the display of recent documents, click on "Options" at the bottom of the left side panel. A new dialog box labeled "Word Options" will appear. 6. In the "Word Options" dialog box, select "Advanced" from the list on the left side. 7. Scroll down until you find the "Display" section. Under this heading, uncheck the box next to "Show this number of Recent Documents" (the default value is 10). Setting this value to zero effectively disables the display of recent documents. 8. Click "OK" to save your changes and close the dialog box. Now, the recent documents list should no longer appear when you click on the File tab. If instead, you'd like to remove specific documents from the list while keeping the feature enabled, proceed as follows: 9a. After hovering over "Recent Documents" in step 4, click on "Clear Unpinned Documents". All entries in the list will disappear momentarily. 9b. However, any documents you had previously chosen to pin (by clicking the pushpin icon associated with them) will reappear almost instantly. This indicates that they are saved separately from other non-pinned entries. **For Microsoft Excel:** The process for Excel is quite similar to Word: 1. Launch Microsoft Excel. Start by opening the program where you intend to edit the settings. 2. Access the "File" tab, just as described above for Word. 3. Locate "Recent Workbooks" in the left side panel under Backstage View. 4. Again, similar to Word, there will be an 'X' button beside each entry. Clicking this will immediately delete that particular item from the list without confirmation. 5. To manage more settings related to recent workbooks, look towards the lower part of the left side panel and click on "Excel Options". 6. In the newly opened dialog box titled "Excel Options", choose "Advanced" from the categories listed on the left side. 7. Find the "Display" options and locate the setting called "Show this number of Recent Workbooks". Adjusting this value works identically to its equivalent in Word; setting it to zero hides the entire recent workbook history. Remember to confirm all changes by pressing "OK". With these steps completed, you now know how to control what appears in the recent document lists of both Microsoft Word and Excel! Recently he has decided that he likes books. [pours the people out of the bus and starts on the puzzle] Title: How to Beat a Video Game Boss Defeating a video game boss can be one of the most challenging yet rewarding experiences in gaming. This guide will provide you with a comprehensive set of steps to help you conquer even the toughest boss battles. ### Step 1: Understand Your Enemy Before engaging in battle, take time to learn about your opponent. Read through their backstory, understand their motivations, and identify any patterns or weaknesses they might have. Familiarize yourself with their moveset, attack patterns, and telegraphs (visual cues that indicate an upcoming attack). The more you know about the boss, the better prepared you'll be to face them. Key Tip: Some games offer pre-battle character dialogue or cutscenes which can give valuable insights into the boss's behavior. Pay attention to these! ### Step 2: Equip Yourself Properly Make sure you're equipped with the right gear before heading into battle. Choose weapons and armor that boost your stats and provide necessary protections against the boss's attacks. Consider elemental resistances if applicable; some bosses are weak or strong against certain elements. Guideline: Always aim to enter a boss fight at maximum health and mana/stamina/etc., as appropriate for your game. ### Step 3: Upgrade Your Skills and Stats The most popular scones are soda scones, wholemeal scones, rich white scones, treacle scones, potato scones, ballater scones and drop scones. The way to do this is to divide the graph into many very small pieces, and then draw very thin rectangles under each piece. As the rectangles become thinner and thinner, the rectangles cover the area underneath the graph better and better. The area of a rectangle is easy to calculate, so we can calculate the total area of all the rectangles. For thinner rectangles, this total area value "approaches" the area underneath the graph. The final value of the area is called the "integral" of the function. An official from Rome was brought over and a trial was held. Let me try my best to simplify this concept for grade-school students. Imagine you have a big box of different colored balls. Each ball has two numbers written on it, one number on each side. So, for example, you might have a blue ball with a "3" on one side and a "7" on the other, or a red ball with a "5" on one side and a "2" on the other. Now, let's say we want to find all the pairs of numbers on the balls that meet a special condition. In particular, we want to find any pair where the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the two numbers is equal to 1 and the second number is not equal to 2 plus the first number. The GCD is just the largest number that divides both of the numbers evenly, with no remainder left over. To do this, we need to go through the whole box of balls and check each pair of numbers against our condition. We can think of this process like sorting through a bunch of cards with different numbers on them, looking for the ones that fit our criteria. In programming, there's a tool called "select" that helps us do exactly this. We give select a list of things (in this case, our list of ball pairs), and tell it which condition to check (our GCD and modulus conditions). Then, it goes through the list and returns back only the items that meet our condition. Here's what the code looks like to do this: Select[[Tuples[Range[10], 2]], Function[{x, y}, GCD[x, y] == 1 && Mod[x, y] != 2]] Let's break down what each part means: * Tuples[Range[10], 2]: This makes a list of all possible pairs of numbers between 1 and 10. Range[10] creates a list of numbers from 1 to 10, and then Tuples takes that list and combines each element with every other element, creating pairs. * Select: This tells Mathematica that we want to choose only some of the items from the list based on a specific condition. * Function[{x, y}, ...]: This defines a new function that takes in two inputs, named x and y. * GCD[x, y] == 1 && Mod[x, y] != 2: These are the conditions we want to check for each pair of numbers. GCD finds the greatest common divisor, while Mod checks whether one number is equal to another number plus a third number. So when we run this code, it will go through all the pairs of numbers between 1 and 10 and return back only the ones where the GCD is 1 and the second number is not equal to 2 plus the first number. Just like sorting through a deck of cards to find the ones that meet our criteria, the select command lets us quickly and easily search through lists of data to find the parts we care about! TenMarks teaches you how to subtract using clock fractions. Read the full transcript Learn about Subtracting with Clock Fractions In this lesson, let's learn how to subtract two fractions using the clock fraction method. All the clock fraction method does is works on fractions that have a denominator that is 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, 30 or 60. Defensibly any factors of 60. Here's how here it works. If we want to subtract two fractions, let's say 1/3 and from that we need to take away 1/6 or 1/3-1/6. We can represent both of these as clock fractions. What do we do? Well we know that in a clock, one turn is 60 minutes, which is one hour. We're talking about the big hand. So if one turn is 60 minutes, if I wanted to do 1/3 or 1/3 hour, what is 1/3 hour? 1/3 of an hour is this much, which is the same as 20 minutes. So 1/3 is the same as 20 minutes when we convert that to an hour. Similarly, 1/6 of an hour is ten minutes. So ten minutes can be represented using two of these gradients. So each gradient has five minutes if you remember. So this is 12-1, 2, 3. So I have the big needle move from 2 to 3, it's gone 5 minutes so 10 minutes is two of these. So if I have something that is 1/3 and from that I want to take away 1/6. Instead of 1/3 I could say, I have 20 minutes and I want to take away 10 minutes. What will we be left with? If from 20 minutes, we took away 10 minutes, we're left with 10 minutes. 20 minutes-10 minutes=10 minutes. What is 10 minutes? Well 10 minutes is 1/6 hour. So what do we have? We can say, 1/3 and from that if I subtract 1/6, I'm left with 1/6. And we did that by just converting each of these fractions into an hour clock and we can do that for any fractions that have these denominators. But the school bus has already left.
