Hello young builders! Today we're going to learn about building special kinds of structures called "CW complexes." We'll talk about how to make sure everything stays neat and tidy inside our structure, just like keeping your toys organized in their toy box. Imagine you have a big box (which we'll call "X"), and you've put some of your favorite toys in it. Some toys are bigger than others, and sometimes when you play, you might accidentally leave a smaller toy near a larger one. To keep things organized, you decide to create special zones for different types of toys - these will be similar to the "skeleta" mentioned in the text you read. Now, imagine someone gives you a new toy (set "A") but doesn't tell you which zone it belongs in. You need to find a way to place this new toy somewhere without causing chaos or having it get lost among all the other toys. So, first, let's give it its own little space next to the toys it shares qualities with (the ones on the same skeleton). But wait, why would this small area around the new toy be considered a nice, orderly spot? Well, remember those tiny individual toy boxes you have for each figurine or car? That's exactly what this space looks like - just enough room for one item! In math language, this teeny-tiny region is called "open," because no other toys can squeeze into that special spot. Next up, let's think about growing our organization system step-by-step (this part relates to the "induction" idea brought up in the text). As you add more toys to your collection, you may realize that some toys could fit together perfectly, side by side. When adding a new toy to your existing group, you must ensure it has plenty of breathing room while still being close to compatible toys. Sometimes, though, a newly introduced toy won't match anything else; however, you still need to provide it with its own space to avoid future confusion. So, here's your mission: Using the rules above, draw your very own CW complex using shapes instead of toys. Make sure every shape has its designated space within your complex, even if it doesn't intersect with another shape yet. Just like organizing your toys, planning ahead keeps your creation clean and easy to navigate! Happy building! Creating an animated GIF in Adobe Photoshop Elements is a fun and engaging way to share your creativity with others. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the process of creating an eye-catching animation using this popular software. **Things You'll Need:** 1. Adobe Photoshop Elements (version 9 or later) 2. A series of images or layers that you want to animate 3. Basic understanding of image editing concepts ### Step 1: Prepare Your Images To create an animated GIF, first gather all the images you wish to include in the sequence. These can be separate files or different layers within one file. Make sure they are numbered or named in the order you intend them to appear in the final GIF. If necessary, resize and crop the images so they have consistent dimensions. ### Step 2: Open Photoshop Elements Launch Adobe Photoshop Elements by double-clicking its icon. Choose "Edit" mode from the welcome screen to access advanced photo editing features. ### Step 3: Create New Document (if needed) If starting with individual image files, go to File > New > Blank File. Set width and height according to the size of your source images and choose a resolution (usually 72 pixels per inch). Click "OK." Now drag and drop the prepared images onto the new document as layers. ### Step 4: Timeline Creation Go to Window > Timeline to open the timeline panel. At the bottom right corner of the timeline window, click on the 'Create Video Timeline' button and select "Convert Layers To Frames". This changes each layer into a frame in the timeline. ### Step 5: Frame Delay Settings Select all frames in the timeline by clicking anywhere along the top bar then choosing Select > All Frames. For each frame, set the duration under the 'Frame Delay' column based on how long you want that specific slide to display before moving onto the next one. Shorter durations result in faster animations while longer ones slow down the pace. Remember consistency is key here; maintaining similar delays between consecutive frames ensures smooth transitions. ### Step 6: Add Transitions (Optional) For adding visual appeal, insert transition effects between frames. With two adjacent frames selected, navigate to Layer > New > Layer via Copy. Drag this newly created copy over the second frame in the timeline. Then apply a transition effect like Fade Between Frames or Cross Dissolve at the end of the first frame and beginning of the second frame respectively. ### Step 7: Looping Options Choose whether your GIF should loop continuously or only once. Under the timeline header, find the repeat method dropdown menu. Here you can opt for "Forever" if you desire continuous playback or "Once" for a single run-through. ### Step 8: Exporting the Animation With everything set up correctly, head over to File > Export > Save for Web (Legacy). From the dialog box, ensure GIF is chosen as the format type. Tick 'Looping options', ensuring it matches what was previously selected. Lastly, hit 'Save'. Navigate to where you wish to save the file, name it appropriately, and press 'Save.' And there you have it! By following these steps meticulously, you now know how to make captivating animated GIFs using Adobe Photoshop Elements. Practice makes perfect - keep experimenting until you master this skill and unleash your inner artist! We haven't gone to the zoo like since she's been talking so. Synthetic Division by x a IN ARITHMETIC we write, for example, 47 = 9 5 + 2 5 is called the divisor, 47 is the dividend, 9 is the quotient, and 2 is the remainder. Dividend = Quotient Divisor + Remainder. In algebra, if we divide a polynomial P(x) by a polynomial D(x) (where the degree of D is less than the degree of P), we would find P(x) = Q(x) D(x) + R(x). P(x) is the dividend, Q(x) is the quotient, and R(x) is the remainder. For example, if, by long division, we divided x - 5x + 3 x - 7 by x - 2, we would find = (x - 3x - 3)(x - 2) - 13. x3 - 5x + 3x - 7 is the dividend, x - 3x -3 is the quotient, and -13 is the remainder. Here is how to do this problem by synthetic division. First, to use synthetic division, the divisor must be of the first degree and must have the form x - a. In this example, the divisor is x - 2, with a = 2. Here again is the problem: Proceed as follows: 1. Write the coefficients of the dividend: 1 - 5 + 3 - 7 2. Put a, in this case 2, in a box to the right, leave a space, and draw a 3. Bring down the leading coefficient (1), multiply it with a (2), and 5. Repeat the process. -3 2 = -6. And so on, until all the coefficients The first three numbers, 1 - 3 - 3, are the coefficients of the quotient, and the final number, -13, is the remainder. x - 5x + 3 x - 7 = (x - 3x -3)(x - 2) - 13. Example 1. Use synthetic division to divide 2x5 + 3x4 + 25x - 1 by x + 3. Solution. There are a couple of points here. First, we must account for all six coefficients of the general form. 2 + 3 + 0 + 25 + 0 - 1 The coefficient of x is 0, as is the coefficient of x. Next, the divisor is x + 3. But the divisor must have the form x - a. x + 3 = x - (-3). Therefore, a = -3. Here is the synthetic division: This tells us Note: The degree of the quotient is one less than the degree of the dividend. And the degree of the remainder is less than the degree of the divisor, x + 3, which in this case is 1. The remainder therefore is of degree 0, which is a number. In general, if we divide a polynomial of degree n by a polynomial of degree 1, then the degree of the quotient will be n - 1. And the remainder will be a number. Problem 1. Use synthetic division to divide x3 - 8x2 + x + 2 by x - 7. Write your answer in the form P(x) = Q(x) D(x) + R. To see the answer, pass your mouse over the colored area. x3 - 8x2 + x + 2 = (x - x - 6)(x - 7) - 40 The remainder theorem The value of a polynomial P(x) at x = a, That is, when P(x) = Q(x)(x - a) + R, where Q(x) is the quotient and R is the remainder, then P(a) = R. Example 2. The Factor Theorem. Let f(x) = x - 3x - 13x + 15. We will use synthetic division to divide f(x) by x + 4. Now, what does the remainder theorem tell us? The value of f(x) at x = -4, is equal to the remainder: f(-4) = -45. Now let us divide f(x) by x - 5: What does the remainder theorem tell us here? f(5) = 0. But this means that 5 is a root of f(x)! Moreover, since the remainder is 0 -- there is no remainder -- then (x - 5) is a factor of f(x). The synthetic division shows: x - 3x - 13x + 15 = (x + 2x - 3)(x - 5) This illustrates the Factor Theorem: The Factor Theorem. x - r is a factor of a polynomial P(x) if and only if r is a root of P(x). Problem 2. Let f(x) = x - 5x - 4x + 7. Use synthetic division to divide f(x) by x - 7. Therefore, according to the remainder theorem, f(7) = 77. Problem 3. Let g(x) = 3x 4 + 17x + 16x - 10x + 4. Use synthetic division to divide g(x) by x + 2. According to the remainder theorem, g(-2) = 0. Therefore, what do you conclude about -2? -2 is a root of g(x). What do you conclude about (x + 2)? (x + 2) is a factor of g(x). Problem 4. Use synthetic division to divide x3 + 125 by x + 5. x3 + 125 = (x - 5x + 25)(x + 5) Please make a donation to keep TheMathPage online. Will you open this so we can have bread? Title: How to Delete a Facebook Video Facebook is a popular social media platform that allows users to share various types of content, including videos. However, there may be situations where you want to delete a video you have uploaded. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on how to delete a Facebook video, along with key tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practicality. **Step 1:** Log into your Facebook account Visit <https://www.facebook.com/> and enter your email address or phone number and password associated with your account. Click 'Log In' to access your Facebook profile. It is essential to sign in before attempting any changes to your account, such as deleting a video. **Step 2:** Navigate to your profile Click on your profile picture at the top left corner of the screen (next to the Facebook logo) or click on the 'See Your Profile' button from the navigation menu on the left side of the page. Accessing your profile gives you an overview of all your posts, photos, and other shared content, making it easy to locate the video you wish to remove. **Step 3:** Find the video you want to delete Scroll down through your timeline until you find the video you would like to remove. If you remember when you posted the video, you can filter your timeline by using the tabs ('Recent Posts', 'Photos', 'Videos', etc.) above the timeline view. Once located, proceed to the next step. **Key Tips:** * Videos are typically displayed under the 'Videos' tab but might also appear among your regular posts. * You cannot delete another user's video from their timeline; only the original poster has this option. **Step 4:** Select the 'Options' dropdown menu Next to the date and time stamp below your video post, click on the three dots icon to reveal additional options related to the specific post. Choosing these options enables you to make adjustments without affecting your entire profile settings. **Step 5:** Choose 'Delete Video' From the list of available actions within the Options dropdown menu, select 'Delete Video'. By choosing this action, you instruct Facebook to permanently erase the selected video file from its servers, ensuring removal from public viewing. ![Video Deleted Successfully]( yeah, LE(t's) see look at the bottom. Not many plants and animals are lucky enough be turned into fossils. When an animal or plant dies its remains usually rot away to nothing. Sometimes though, when the conditions are just right and its remains can be buried quickly, it may be fossilised. There are several different ways fossils are formed. Here we go through the five steps of fossilisation to make a typical 'mould and cast' fossil. An animal dies, its skeleton settles on the sea floor and is buried by sediment. An animal dies and its body sinks to the sea floor. The soft parts of the animal rot away, leaving only its skeleton. The skeleton is buried by sediment (like mud or sand) falling from the ocean above. The sea floor is an ideal place for fossilisation, which explains why many fossils are marine (from animals that lived in the sea). Land animals may die and be swept out to sea to be buried in the same way. The sediment surrounding the skeleton thickens and begins to turn to stone. The skeleton continues to be buried as sediment is added to the surface of the sea floor. As the sea floor sinks, pressure increases in the lower layers of sediment and it turns it into hard rock. The skeleton dissolves and a mould is formed. Now buried at depth and surrounded by stone, the skeleton is dissolved by ground water. This leaves a cavity (or hole) preserving the shape of the original skeleton. This cavity is known as a natural mould. Minerals crystallise inside the mould and a cast is formed. Water rich in minerals enters the mould, and fills the cavity. The minerals deposited in the mould form a cast of the mould. This cast has the same shape as the original skeleton, but none of its internal features. The fossil is exposed on the Earth's surface. Millions of years later, the rock surrounding the skeleton rises to the Earth's surface (this happens during mountain building, earthquakes and other earth processes). The rock is worn away by wind and rain, and the fossil is now exposed, waiting to be found! Fossils form when the conditions are right. Why not find out more about fossils? See how difficult it is to become a fossil by playing our game, A twist in time! If you read these pages you should get a pretty good idea of what a fossil is. Introducing the basics What can fossils tell us? How do fossils form? Different types of fossils Find out more about fossils by visiting Invertebrate I.D. or the History of life Animals living in the Sahara include gerbil, jerboa, cape hare and desert hedgehog, barbary sheep, oryx, gazelle, deer, wild ass, baboon, hyena, jackal, sand fox, weasel and mongoose. The bird life counts more than 300 species. Reptiles, including 4 species of snake also live here. The most venomous scorpion in the world lives here. Title: How to Cancel Your Adobe Photoshop Subscription Introduction: Adobe Photoshop is a powerful image editing software used by professionals and hobbyists alike. However, there might come a time when you decide to cancel your subscription. This tutorial will guide you through the process of cancelling your Adobe Photoshop subscription with clear, step-by-step instructions. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Before cancelling, consider whether you want to keep using any other Adobe services or apps, as this may affect your cancellation options. * Make sure to save all your work before initiating the cancellation process. * Keep your login information handy, as you'll need it to access your account settings. * Be aware that if you have an annual plan paid monthly, you must cancel at least 25 days before renewal to avoid being charged for the next billing cycle. If you have a month-to-month plan, you can cancel anytime without penalty. Step 1: Log into your Adobe Account Visit the Adobe website (www.adobe.com) and click on the 'Sign In' button located at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your Adobe ID. Once logged in, proceed to Step 2. Step 2: Navigate to My Plans Page After successfully logging in, hover over your profile picture (or initials) at the top right corner of the webpage. A dropdown menu will appear; select 'My Plans'. You will be redirected to the Manage Account page where you can view your current subscriptions. Step 3: Locate Your Photoshop Subscription On the Manage Account page, scroll down until you find the section titled 'Your Plan(s)'. Underneath, locate your Adobe Photoshop subscription. It should display details such as payment status, billing period, and price per cycle. Step 4: Initiate the Cancellation Process To begin the cancellation process, click on the 'Manage Plan' button found next to your Photoshop subscription listing. On the following screen, click on the 'Cancel Plan' option. Confirm that you wish to continue with the cancellation by clicking on 'Yes, cancel now' in the popup window. Step 5: Complete the Survey (Optional) At this point, Adobe may prompt you to complete a short survey regarding why you are choosing to cancel. While filling out this survey is optional, providing feedback could help improve their service offerings. After completing the survey (if applicable), press the 'Continue' button. Step 6: Review Final Details & Confirm Cancellation You will be presented with a summary of your upcoming changes, including the date your Photoshop access will end. Double-check these details carefully. If everything looks correct, click on the 'Complete Cancellation' button to finalize the cancellation process. Step 7: Download Any Necessary Files Although rare, some features within Photoshop may become unavailable immediately upon cancellation. To ensure continued access to your projects, download necessary files before closing your account. Conclusion: Cancelling your Adobe Photoshop subscription doesn't have to be complicated once you know what steps to take. By following this tutorial closely, you can confidently manage your account and make adjustments according to your needs. Remember to always save your work prior to making any changes and review the terms of your agreement before confirming cancellation. The Ministry for State Security (in German: "Ministerium f r Staatssicherheit"; often called "MfS" or "Stasi") was the East German secret police. [Nina is banging the egg against the floor.] How does the brightness of a star depend on its distance from us? The apparent brightness of a star is proportional to 1 divided by its distance squared. That is, if you took a star and moved it twice as far away, it would appear 1/4 as bright; if you moved it four times the distance, it would appear 1/16 as bright. The reason this happens is simple. Imagine that you are the star, spitting out imaginary light rays in all directions. Now take each of your hands and touch your thumb to your forefinger to make a circle that you can look through. Hold both hands up in front of your face and imagine that each circle is a "telescope" that someone is using to "look" at you (in other words, to collect the light rays that you are spitting out). Now move one hand twice as far away from your eyes as the other and look at the faraway "telescope" through the nearby one. You should see that it is only about 1/4 the size. (What has happened is that each of its two dimensions has shrunk by 1/2, so when you multiply them together to get the area it has gone down by 1/4.) This means that the second circle would only collect 1/4 as many of the light rays that you're throwing at it, so it would measure you (the star) to only be 1/4 as bright. Okay you find a barrel and then let's get dressed. Becoming an outstanding point guard in basketball requires a combination of skill, knowledge, and mental toughness. The point guard is often referred to as the "floor general" because they are responsible for leading their team both offensively and defensively. Here's a detailed guide on how to become an exceptional point guard: **Step 1: Master the Basics** Before you can excel at any position, especially point guard, you need to have strong fundamentals. This includes dribbling, passing, shooting, and footwork. Practice these skills regularly until they become second nature. *Dribbling*: Work on your ball handling by using both hands equally. Try different moves like crossovers, behind-the-backs, between-the-legs dribbles to improve your handle. Remember, speed kills but so does control; keep your head up while dribbling to see the entire court. *Passing*: Learn various types of passes such as chest pass, bounce pass, overhead pass, baseball pass etc., and understand when to use which one. Accuracy is crucial here - make sure your passes reach your intended recipient directly without getting intercepted or going out of bounds. *Shooting*: Develop a consistent form and work on increasing your range. As a point guard, most of your shots will likely be three-pointers or mid-range jumpers, but being able to shoot from anywhere on the court gives you more options during games. *Footwork*: Good footwork allows you to move quickly and change direction rapidly. It also helps with setting screens, defending, and creating space for shots or drives. **Step 2: Understand Your Role** A point guard's primary role is to run the offense effectively. They should know their teammates' strengths and weaknesses, making sure everyone gets involved in the game. A great point guard makes others better through smart decision-making and timely assists. **Step 3: Improve Court Vision** Court vision refers to a player's ability to perceive the entire court and understand where every player is positioned. To enhance this skill, practice scanning the floor constantly, even when you don't have the ball. Look for open teammates, potential cuts, and defensive mismatches that could lead to scoring opportunities. **Step 4: Develop Leadership Qualities** As the leader of the team, a point guard needs to exhibit strong leadership qualities. Communication is key here - direct your teammates verbally and nonverbally, encouraging them and offering constructive criticism when necessary. Show respect towards referees and opponents, maintaining composure under pressure. **Step 5: Study the Game** Watch professional basketball games and observe how top point guards operate. Note how they manage the clock, read defenses, create plays, and communicate with their teams. You can learn a lot just by observing experienced players. **Step 6: Train Physically** Basketball is physically demanding, requiring stamina, strength, agility, and quickness. Regular conditioning exercises (like running sprints, doing plyometrics, lifting weights) can help improve your physical fitness level significantly. **Step 7: Prepare Mentally** Mental preparation is vital for success in sports. Visualize yourself performing well before games, stay focused throughout the match, maintain a positive attitude, and never get too high or low emotionally based on results. Remember, becoming an excellent point guard takes time and dedication. Stay committed to improving daily, seek feedback from coaches and peers, and most importantly, enjoy the process! [removes a black crayon from the container] Snakes jaws are not fused together. This means that unlike humans, snakes jaws are not hooked together at the back of their mouth. Snakes don't have eyelids. They have a clear scale that covers their eye called a spectacle or brille. Snakes do not have skin like a human. They have scales which create armor so that they are able to move around on rough surfaces without getting hurt. Most snakes have teeth, but only venomous snakes have fangs. All snakes swallow their food whole. Although they have teeth, they are not actually used to chew up their food, but rather to grab and hold their prey. Snakes don't need to eat like humans. Some snakes only eat a few times a year. It can take up to a couple of months for some snakes to digest their food. Snakes are cold-blooded, and cannot live in places where it is cold year round. During the cold months snakes hibernate, which is like sleeping for an extended period of time. Snakes are born with an egg tooth that is used to tear out of their egg or the membrane they are born in. They will lose this tooth soon after hatching. Some snakes will stay with their eggs until they hatch, while others lay their eggs and never return to them. Snakes reproduce once every one to three years. Snake eggs are not hard like a bird's, but rather soft and leathery. Although snakes are often described as being "slimy," they are actually very dry. A snake's tongue is not used to taste or aid in swallowing like a human's tongue. It is actually an accessory smelling organ. Some snakes have a "sixth sense" that mammals and other reptiles do not. They have special pits between their eyes and their nostrils that sense heat from heat sources that are close to the snake. This helps them with locating prey. Snakes that have these pits are members of a family of snakes referred to as 'pit vipers.' If you ever see a snake being charmed/hypnotized by someone playing the flute, it doesn't actually work like that. Snakes don't hear sound like a human does. They use a small ear bone called the "columella" to detect vibrations of sound waves conducted through the ground. They can only pick up very low frequency sound waves through the air. Although snakes have two lungs, their left lung is a vestigial organ. Vestigial means that it is a small, non-functioning organ. Stir stir stir what are we going to stir should we put something in? Hide Contacts on LinkedIn: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide LinkedIn is an excellent platform for networking with professionals from various industries worldwide. However, there may be instances when you want to hide your contacts or connections from other users due to privacy concerns or competition. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of hiding your contacts on LinkedIn while providing insights into why these steps are necessary and offering valuable tips along the way. Part 1: Accessing Your Settings **Step 1:** Log in to your LinkedIn account using your email address and password. Make sure that you're accessing the correct version (desktop or mobile) as some settings might differ between platforms. *Why this step matters:* Before making any changes to your LinkedIn profile, logging in allows you to have full control over your account and ensures that you can make informed decisions about what information you wish to share or conceal. **Step 2:** Once logged in, navigate to the top right corner of the page where your profile picture should appear. Click on the dropdown menu next to it. *Why this step matters:* The dropdown menu provides quick access to important features such as Privacy & Settings, which houses all the options related to customizing your LinkedIn experience. **Step 3:** From the dropdown menu, select 'Settings & Privacy.' *Why this step matters:* Selecting 'Settings & Privacy' gives you insight into who sees what on your profile, data privacy, advertising preferences, and communication settings. It also enables you to adjust these settings according to your needs and comfort level. Part 2: Customizing Visibility of Connections **Step 4:** On the left sidebar under the 'Privacy' tab, click on 'Visibility of your profile & network.' *Why this step matters:* This section contains crucial settings regarding controlling visibility across different aspects of your LinkedIn presence, including your connections. **Step 5:** Under the subheading 'Connections,' find the option titled 'Who can see your list of connections?' By default, it should read 'Your connections.' To change this setting, click on the edit icon (a small pencil symbol) beside it. *Why this step matters:* Modifying this particular setting determines whether others can view your entire connection list directly from your profile. Changing this to 'Only you' effectively hides your contacts from everyone else. **Step 6:** After clicking the edit icon, choose 'Only you' from the dropdown menu. Then click 'Save Changes.' *Why this step matters:* Choosing 'Only you' limits the visibility of your connections only to yourself, ensuring maximum privacy for your contact list. Saving the changes finalizes the new setting. Key Tips and Guidelines * Regularly review your privacy settings to ensure they align with current needs and concerns. * Consider limiting the amount of personal information shared publicly on your LinkedIn profile. * Remember that even if you hide your contacts, certain activities like endorsements and recommendations could still indirectly reveal mutual connections. * Keep in mind that completely restricting access to your connections might limit potential opportunities offered by introductions or referrals via mutual acquaintances. In the 1970s and 1980s, some people gave a new, specific meaning to "information". At that time, the first computer databases were built. In computer science, "data" often means a kind of information that has not been checked. That means "data" has not been changed or fixed, and you may not be able to trust it. With the new meaning, "information" means data that has been checked and passed tests for what it must be. A person can trust that "information" is correct. pumpkin,large bowl or pan,paper,crayons Have a large pumpkin put in a large bowl or pan. Google uses this word because the company wants to make lots of stuff on the Web easy to find and use. Andy Bechtolsheim thought of the name. There are so many ways to have fun with your kids and encourage learning at the same time. Some might call it 'making learning fun'. We call it PlaySavvy. Here are just a few recommendations for games to play at home. What's My Line? Arrange some objects in a line. Ask how many there are and count them to check. Then ask if there will be the same number if you start counting from the middle of the line. Many children don't realize the number doesn't change regardless of where you start to count. The next time you're reading a book, point to the page numbers. Ask your child to predict the next number when you turn the page. Tough sequences include the teens or turning into a new decade (e.g., from 29 to 30) and from 109 to 110. I Wish I Had Grab a few raisins from a bag and count them. Then say: "I have 7 but I wish I had 12." Your child predicts how many you need; then check by counting. Listen for how they figured it out. Let your child pose the question and you solve it. Hide and Peek Together with your child, count out eight or more objects, depending on what's appropriate around the house. Hide some of the objects under a bowl and keep some of them visible. Ask how many are under the bowl. Listen for how your child figured it out. Solve the Mystery This is a calculator game. Enter your mystery number into the calculator; then add an agreed upon number to it (we'll add five). Show your child the new number and see if they can "solve the mystery" by figuring out the original number. Ask how they know. Three Blind Dice Okay, so they're not blind, but take turns with your child rolling three dice and adding up the totals. Each of you keeps a running total on a calculator. The first one to 50 wins. Hold up some fingers for a brief moment and see if your child can tell you how many were there. Begin with one hand. Then move to two hands. Eventually both of you can flash two hands ("1, 2, 3, Go!") and see who can come up with the combined totals first. Collect some clutter from the floor and ask: "How many do you think there are?" Count to check. Then ask "How many do you think there would be if we counted by 2s? 3s? 5s?" Many children don't realize that the amount doesn't change regardless of how it's counted. Cut the Deck Take a deck of playing cards (2 to 10) and cut off the numbers so that only the hearts, clubs, spades, or diamonds remain. Play games with your new cards. When you lay down a card, say the number. Without the number symbol to read, your child has to figure out how many. It's a Snap! With your cut-up deck of cards, play "Snap". You and your child each flip over a card. If there is a match you shout out the number. Whoever says the correct number first gets both cards. A variation on the game is to say "Snap!" whenever the two cards combine to make ten. I Got More Divide your cut-up deck between you and your child. Turn over a card. The person with the greater amount takes both cards; if they're equal, the winner of the next card takes both. How Much More A game to play with your cut-up deck. Gather 30-40 raisins as counters. Each player flips over a card. The player whose card is greater wins as many raisins as the difference between the two cards. When the raisins run out, the game is over. Then eat the raisins. Playing with Numbers Go on a "Number Walk" and find all the places where there are numbers. As you find a number, say it. Look for something to collect and count how many you find. You can also hunt for a particular number (e.g., 6) or quantity. Play "I Spy with my Math Eye something that is 6." Trip to the Library Go to the library and read some counting books. Good ones include The Very Hungry Caterpillar (Carle), Two Ways to Count to Ten (Dee), and The King's Commissioners (Friedman). Talk about different combinations that equal ten and notice the different ways we can count ten. Setting the Table Ask your child to set the table for your family. If he or she grabs a bunch of forks without counting, ask, "How could you know how many forks you need?" At dinner, "count the table". How many knives, forks, napkins, plates, etc. How Much Longer Look at a calendar and count how many days until a special event. Have your child name the numerals that they see on the calendar. Emergency room doctors are in charge of sudden important or life-threatening emergencies. In addition to dealing with heart attacks, strokes, traumas, issues that require immediate medical attention or surgeries, they also deal with a wide range of other health conditions, such as mental health and drug overdoses. Their training is broad and diverse as anyone can walk through the door seeking help. They see patients of all ages and walks of life. However, unlike a general practitioner or family doctor, their immediate goal is to make sure the patient is stable and exclude any serious or life-threatening diseases or conditions. The GNU General Public License (GPL) is a computer software copyleft license. This license lets the user of the software use a program in many of the same ways as if it were public domain. They can use it, change it, and copy it. They can also sell or give away copies of the program with or without any changes they made to it. The license lets them do this as long as they agree to follow the terms of the license. The GPL was created by Richard Stallman. The current version is version 3, created in 2007, although some software still uses version 2, created in 1991. Tourism is one of the most important national incomes in Egypt. Place the leaves in boiling water for six minutes to soften the cells, and then in ethanol (alcohol). When the leaves are drained of colour, spread them out on a flat surface. Soak the leaves in iodine and the green parts will turn blue/black and the non green parts will stay the yellowish brown colour of iodine liquid. The green parts contain starch, which makes iodine turn blue/black. The blue/black stain is what shows starch is present. You could take "before & after" photos to prove the black areas were green at the start. Meat-eating, or carnivorous, plants can trap and digest insects and other small animals. They do this to obtain the vital nitrogen that they need to grow. Most plants absorb enough nitrogen from nitrates in the soil. Carnivorous plants live in bogs, where nitrates are in short supply, so they need to obtain their nitrogen by digesting prey instead. Carnivorous plants have developed unique ways to catch insects, such as fluid-filled PITCHERS and spring-loaded traps. The Venus flytrap's leaves are hinged so that they can snap shut. Sensitive trigger hairs detect any insect that lands on the surface of an open leaf. At the slightest movement, the two halves of the leaf spring shut. As the sides of the trap close around the victim, the plant releases digestive juices. These break down the soft parts of the insect. Sundews are small bog plants that have hair-covered leaves. They produce a droplet of sticky "dew" at the tip of each hair. Insects are attracted to the fluid, but become stuck. Next, the hairs slowly bend inwards until the whole leaf has folded over the insect. Chemicals released from the hairs digest the insect's body, and nutrients are taken into the plant. The pitcher plant is named for the juglike traps that hang below its leaves or grow up from the ground. Each trap has its own lid to keep off the rain and contains special fluid at the bottom. Insects are attracted by the trap's red markings and the sweet nectar produced around its rim. If the insect lands to drink the nectar, it slips and falls into the trap. It drowns in the fluid at the bottom and its nutrients are slowly absorbed by the plant. An insect body has to be broken down before its nutrients can be absorbed into the plant. Carnivorous plants such as pitchers use enzymes, similar to the ones that break down food in an animal's gut. Acids help the enzymes to break down the body. A pitcher plant can digest a small insect within a few hours, but larger ones take days. I fall down on Paul bed and missed it. Most of these earthquakes were magnitude 1.0 or less; some as strong as magnitude 3.2 were centered 25 kilometers below the surface. The cause of these earthquakes is believed to be the rising of molten magma because there are no faults or tectonic plate boundaries. The source of this magma is from the Anahim hotspot and continues to this day. Walter Cannon, a professor of physiology at Harvard University coined the term homeostasis in his book "The Wisdom of the Body" in 1932. In his work with animals Cannon observed that any change of emotional state in the beast, such as anxiety, distress, or rage was accompanied by total cessation of movements of the stomach (Bodily Changes in Pain, Hunger, Fear and Rage, 1915). In the United Kingdom, road signs that show how high a vehicle can be in order to pass through a tunnel are required to be in feet and inches. Theme parks and drive thru signs usually show it in metres. People regularly measure their height in feet and inches. Title: How to Build a Jungle Gym Introduction: A jungle gym is an excellent addition to any backyard, providing children with countless hours of physical activity and imaginative play. Building your own jungle gym can be both rewarding and cost-effective. This comprehensive guide will take you through every step necessary to construct a safe and durable outdoor playset that meets industry standards. Always prioritize safety when building and using this equipment. Materials Required: 1. Pressure-treated lumber (cedar or redwood recommended) 2. Galvanized bolts, washers, and nuts 3. Lag screws 4. Deck screws 5. Ground anchors 6. Safety surfacing material (e.g., rubber mulch, wood chips) 7. Post hole digger or power auger 8. Level 9. Tape measure 10. Circular saw 11. Drill and drill bits 12. Socket wrench set 13. Adjustable wrenches 14. Ratchet tie down straps 15. Speed square 16. Framing square 17. Carpenter's pencil 1 The original "CQR" was invented in 1933 in the United Kingdom. Many copies of it now exist. Plows used to be popular with sailors and private boaters. They are good but not excellent in most seabeds. Another more recent design is the "Delta" which is better. Sheep Shearing Rule Date: 03/20/2003 at 23:42:13 From: Mitch Subject: Shearing sheep Eric the sheep is waiting in line to be shorn. Each time a sheep at the front of the line gets shorn, Eric sneaks up line four places. Describe a rule you could use to find the number of sheep shorn before Eric for any number of sheep in front of him. Date: 03/21/2003 at 11:25:36 From: Doctor Ian Subject: Re: Shearing sheep Hi Mitch, Interesting problem! Have you tried simulating it to see what happens? That's often a good way to get a feel for what's happening, which is the first step in finding a rule. We start with some number of sheep in front of Eric: S S S ... S S S S S S S E \_____________________/ n sheep The use of '...' and a variable for the number of sheep is a convenient way to avoid having to choose a particular number of sheep. Now, one gets shorn, S S S ... S S S S S S E \___________________/ (n-1) sheep and Eric sneaks up four places: S S S ... S S E S S S S \___________________/ (n-1) sheep But we're only interested in the sheep in front of Eric, so we have S S S ... S S E S \___________/ (n-5) sheep So if there were n sheep in front of him, there are (n-5) after the first sheep is shorn. After the second sheep is shorn, there will be (n-5) - 5 sheep, and after the third sheep is shorn, there will be ((n-5) - 5) - 5 sheep, and so on. Is this enough to get started? Note that there is another way to think about it, which is this: From Eric's point of view, there's no difference between a sheep that is shorn ahead of him, and a sheep that moves to a place in line behind him. So another way to phrase the problem is this: Eric the sheep is waiting in line to be shorn. Each time a sheep at the front of the line gets shorn, four more get shorn at the same time (in a single 'turn'). How many turns will it take Eric to get to the front of the line? The number of 'turns' in this problem is the same as the number of sheep shorn in the original problem. Does that make sense? - Doctor Ian, The Math Forum http://mathforum.org/dr.math/ Search the Dr. Math Library: Ask Dr. MathTM 1994-2013 The Math Forum And what did daddy do to you? [takes a shape and places it on Dor's head] I'll aim it all the way back... But I'm not going to go in the car with you. Stars are brilliant beacons of light shining through the vast cosmic darkness. But just how brilliant they are, and how far they shine across the darkness, varies by a huge amount. In our Milky Way galaxy, for example, the most brilliant stars are several billion times brighter than the faintest ones. What the faint ones lack in power, though, they make up for with numbers -- they account for most of the galaxy's stars, and most of our stellar neighbors, including the closest one of all. Yet not a single one of them is bright enough to see from Earth with the eye alone. These stars are known as red dwarfs. Their surfaces are so cool that they shine reddish-orange, like dull cosmic embers. They range from about half down to less than one-tenth the mass of the Sun. With such little material, the nuclear furnaces in their cores chug along at a leisurely rate, so they produce little energy. In fact, those at the low end of the scale are so feeble that it takes them a year to emit as much energy as the Sun produces in just an hour. These stars are quite turbulent, though. A red dwarf's surface layers bubble like a boiling tea kettle. All of that motion generates powerful magnetic "storms": dark "starspots" that can cover close to half of the star's surface, and powerful explosions that shower space with X-rays. Yet red dwarfs should be good targets to search for planets. We'll explain why tomorrow. Ice hockey is a sport that is played by two teams on ice. The players wear ice skates on their feet and can skate across the ice at very high speeds. They hold hockey sticks, which they use to push, shoot or pass a puck around the ice. The players score by shooting the puck into a net; the goaltenders try to stop them. Six players on each team play at once, but a whole team has over 20 players. Each team has 2 defenders, 3 forwards, and a goalie on the ice at a time. When a player breaks a rule, a referee calls a penalty, and the player has to sit in a penalty box for 2-4 minutes. While the player sits in the penalty box, his team has to play without him, and will have fewer players on the ice until the penalty is over. They have six legs; they have hard parts on the outside of the body (an exoskeleton); they have eyes formed of many separate chambers, and so on. Biologists explain this with evolution. All insects are the descendants of a group of animals who lived a long time ago. They still keep the basic plan (six legs and so on) but the details change. They look different now because they changed in different ways: this is evolution. Do you run away when you see a dog? Here look I give you another one. Ch vez was born near Yuma, Arizona and went to over 37 schools. He graduated from the eighth grade. He did most of his organizing in California, especially near Bakersfield. Fred Ross taught him to lead unions. Fred Ross was a student of Saul Alinsky. In your science class, you will learn about Newton's laws of motion. Do you know why these laws are so important? When you throw a baseball or you bounce a basketball on the court, you can use Newton's laws to explain the motion of the baseball or basketball. In fact, almost all motion in the world can be explained by these laws. Newton's laws are useful when people design airplanes, trains, cars, sports equipment, toys, and many things that have to do with motion. Some people have trouble understanding Newton's laws of motion because it's hard to see how the laws work without any examples. One great way to understand these laws is to look at how they relate to cars in everyday life. The First Law of Motion "Every object persists in its state of rest or uniform motion in a straight line unless it is compelled to change that state by forces impressed upon it." Can you see why it's hard for some people to understand the first law? Those are some complicated words. To help you to understand better, let's break it down and use the example of a car. The first part deals with an object in a "state of rest". This means that the object is not moving. When your mom or dad parks the car in the garage or the shopping mall, the car is said to be in a "state of rest" because it is not moving. The car will not move until the engine is started and somebody drives it away. The second part deals with an object in "uniform motion". This means that the object is moving at the same speed. When you mom or dad is using "cruise control" on a highway at 60 miles per hour, the car will be moving at that speed in a "straight line" until something happens to change it. What is this change? It depends. By using the brakes, your mom or dad can slow the car down or bring it to a stop. On the other hand, the car's direction will be changed if it is hit by another motor vehicle. The Second Law of Motion "Force is equal to the change in momentum (mV) per change in time. For a constant mass, force equals mass times acceleration." F = ma This is one of the most important formulas in physics. Again, some people may find it hard to understand this law because of the use of some big words. However, the idea is very simple because you may already know how it works in real life. Do you need more energy (force) to move a book or a large screen TV? Surely, you know that you need more energy to move a large screen TV, right? That's what the second law of motion means. Using our example of cars, you should know that it takes more force to move an 18 wheeler truck than a car. This is why the engine in an 18 wheeler is more powerful than the engine in a car. To move a heavier object, you need to use more force. The Third Law of Motion "For every action, there is an equal and opposite re-action." Finally, here's a law without any complicated words. As you will see, it's quite easy to understand Newton's third law of motion. Do you like to go skating? The next time you go for a skating trip, try to this simple experiment. With your skates on, find someone and place your palms against the other person's palms. Then, push off and you will see the both of you going in opposite directions. Do you know how cars move? Now, you may think that a car moves because of the engine. Well, the engine is the source of power but it's not what makes a car move forward or backward. When you press on the accelerator, the wheels spin and the force of the tires push off against the road, causing the car to move. This is Newton's third law of motion in action. In the early days of quantum mechanics, Albert Einstein suggested that if it were right then quantum mechanics would mean that there would be "spooky action at a distance." It turned out that quantum mechanics was right, and that what Einstein had used as a reason to reject quantum mechanics actually happened. This kind of "spooky connection" between certain quantum events is now called "quantum entanglement". I give him like grilled cheese sandwiches. The transistor became cheaper in the 1960s and was much smaller, worked on lower voltages, and used less power. In addition, unlike vacuum tubes, they were much less likely to be damaged by being dropped and had extremely long life. Eventually, they were also much cheaper than glass vacuum tubes. At this time, most radios, television sets, and amplifiers began using transistors instead. High powered electronics such as broadcasting transmitters were transistorized more slowly. Television receivers continued using the cathode ray tube until the mid-2000s. I don't know if dinosaurs are really really really purple. For children to excel in reading they must master the ability to read fluently. - White board - Lee and the Team for every two students - Fluency Field checklists for each student 3rd base 1st Base Read with expression Remembered more words - One-minute reads class record sheet 1. "We have been learning many things that make us expert readers. We have enough knowledge to become move from beginning readers to fluent readers. Fluent readers read smoothly and with expression making their stories much more interesting than beginning readers. Fluency also helps us understand our stories better. 2. Let's review some skills that we have learned which help us decode words. To read fluently we need to be able to limit our decoding and recognize words quickly, but if we do need to decode we must be able to know how to do so quickly. If I come to a word like team (write on board), and am stuck, what's the first thing I should do? That's right, get out my cover up. With my cover up I will get the vowel by itself covering up all the other letters (model). My vowel sound in team is ea and I know that ea=/E/. We are then going to uncover the beginning letter t. I know t=/t/ so know we have /t/ /E/. Then uncover the last letter m. m=/m/ /t/ /E/ /m/ team! Let's all join the speed TEAM! (Write on the board) 3. If we try to decode and the word still does not make sense, let's say I said tem and not team, what should we do? That's right, cross-check. Cross-checking means reading ahead to try and find clues in the sentence which make the most sense for the word to be. Let's practice. (Read) 'Let's all join the speed tem' speed tem that doesn't sound right oh Let's all join the speed team! After decoding if your sentence doesn't make sense cross-check and REread. 4. I'm going to practice reading our sentence three times. I want you to listen very carefully and tell me which sentence you think was read the most fluent." Read one sentence sounding out each phoneme in every word. Read the second sentence more smoothly but without expression. And read the last sentence smoothly and with expression. "Which sentence sound the most fluent to you? That's right, the third one. When I read the third sentence I knew every word, without having to sound out each phoneme, and I read with lots of expression or excitement. The third time I read you could probably understand what I said a lot better than the first time. 6. Give book talk. Lee is on a baseball team. His team is supposed to be practicing but they are being a little lazy. A big bee sneaks up on the team. What do you think Lee's team mates will do when they see the bee? You and your reading buddy will have to read "Lee and the Team" to find out! 7. When you are done reading your book three times, share with your buddy how their fluency has improved remember to only say improvements your buddy has made. 8. For assessment call students up one at a time to the back of the room to complete one-minute reads. Students will use a book of their choice at an appropriate readability level. Time each student for one-minute three times, starting over at the beginning of the book each time. Record the time on the class record sheet their first, second, and third time. Share with students their fluency improvement as their times increased each with each time. The one-minute reads and the Fluency checklists will all be indicators in the progress each student is making in their fluency. Title: How to Dress Cute for School ===================================== Are you tired of wearing the same old uniform or outfit to school every day? Want to add some flair and personality to your daily look while still adhering to the dress code? Look no further! This comprehensive guide will provide you with in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines to help you dress cute for school without compromising comfort or appropriateness. ### Step 1: Understand Your School's Dress Code Before diving into fashion choices, familiarize yourself with your school's dress code policy. Some schools may have strict regulations regarding colors, lengths, and styles, so it is essential to be aware of these rules before making any purchases or alterations. By understanding the limitations, you can ensure that your outfits are both stylish and compliant. *Key Tip:* Opt for versatile pieces that can be mixed and matched to create various looks within the boundaries of your school's dress code. ### Step 2: Plan Outfit Combinations Ahead of Time (Submitted March 02, 1998) Is there any night time on the Moon? Is the Moon's daylight the same as the Earth's daylight or is it brighter? The moon orbits the earth with a period of four weeks ( a month) and during the orbit it always has the same side facing the earth. So this means that on the moon there is day and night, but they are both two weeks long instead of 24 hours. The Moon's daylight is brighter and harsher than the Earth's. There is no atmosphere to scatter the light, no clouds to shade it, and no ozone layer to block the sunburning ultraviolet light. The nights are also brighter, at least on the side of the Moon near to us. The night is lit up by sunlight reflected from Earth, while the night on Earth is lit up by sunlight reflected from the Moon. Earth is much bigger than the Moon, and Earth is also more reflective (with its clouds and oceans, it reflects more light than the dark Moon rocks). Earthlight on the Moon is much brighter than Moonlight on the Earth. Hope this helps, Jeff Silvis and David Palmer For Ask an Astrophysicist Questions on this topic are no longer responded to by the "Ask an Astrophysicist" service. Clear Blackberry Curve 8520 Event Log: A Comprehensive Guide The Blackberry Curve 8520 is a popular smartphone that offers various features, including an event log. The event log records all activities performed on the device, which can be helpful when troubleshooting issues or monitoring user behavior. However, if the event log becomes too large, it may affect the phone's performance. Therefore, clearing the event log periodically is recommended. This article will provide you with a step-by-step guide on how to clear the Blackberry Curve 8520 event log. Before we begin, let's discuss what the event log is and why you might want to clear it. What is the Event Log? --------------------- The event log is a record of all events that occur on your Blackberry Curve 8520. Events include application crashes, system errors, security warnings, and other significant actions taken by the device. By examining the event log, you can gain insight into potential problems with the phone and identify steps to resolve them. Why Clear the Event Log? ----------------------- Over time, the event log can become quite extensive, taking up valuable storage space and potentially slowing down the phone's performance. Clearing the event log can help improve the phone's speed and responsiveness while also making it easier to identify critical events. Additionally, some users prefer to clear the event log regularly as part of their routine maintenance procedures. Step-by-Step Instructions ------------------------- Now that we understand the purpose of the event log and why we should clear it let's move on to the actual process. Here are the steps to clear the Blackberry Curve 8520 event log: 1. **Access the Options Menu:** From the home screen, press the menu button (located just below the trackball) and select "Options." 2. **Select Advanced Options:** Scroll through the list of options until you find "Advanced Options" and click on it. 3. **Find and Select Event Log:** Within the advanced options menu, scroll down until you see "Event Log," then click on it. 4. **Review Current Entries:** You will now see a list of current entries within the event log. Take a moment to review these entries to ensure there are no critical events you need to address before clearing the log. If necessary, take note of any relevant information. 5. **Clear the Event Log:** To delete all current entries, simply highlight the first entry and press the menu button. Then, select "Delete All" from the dropdown menu. Confirm this action by clicking "Yes" when prompted. 6. **Exit Back to Home Screen:** Once the event log has been cleared, exit back to the home screen by pressing the escape key (located at the top left corner of the keyboard). Key Tips and Guidelines ---------------------- * It is essential to regularly check and clear the event log to maintain optimal phone performance. Consider setting a reminder to perform this task monthly or bi-monthly. * Before clearing the event log, always review the current entries to ensure there are no critical events requiring attention. Taking notes or saving important details before deletion can be beneficial if further investigation is needed later. * Remember that clearing the event log does not resolve underlying issues causing the recorded events; instead, it removes the visual clutter and allows for better organization moving forward. Addressing root causes remains crucial for maintaining a healthy and functioning Blackberry Curve 8520. A distant observer sees the material falling in slowly and then stop just above the event horizon because of gravitational time dilation. The light given off just before the event horizon is delayed an infinite amount of time. So the observer never sees the formation of the event horizon. Instead, the collapsing material seems to become dimmer and increasingly red-shifted, eventually fading away. Today, let's talk about a fun and exciting concept called "Quantum Expectation Calculation." You might think it sounds complicated, but don't worry! We will break it down into easy-to-understand ideas using things around us. Imagine having a bag containing two types of candies - red (represented by "$|0\rangle$") and blue ($|1\rangle$). The entire collection of candies represents our "Hilbert Space," which is just a fancy name for all possible states or combinations of these candies. In our example, there are only two distinct states, so our Hilbert Space has two dimensions, like a flat piece of paper. Now, suppose you have a special rule that allows you to pick only red candies out of your bag. This rule creates a unique area within your bag, exclusively holding only red candies; we call this a "subspace." Let's denote this particular subspace as $\mathcal{M_1}$. When you put your hand inside the bag without looking and randomly grab some candies, you expect to pull out red ones due to our special rule. To determine the probability ($\text{Pr}(\mathcal{M_1})$) of picking up a red candy, follow these steps: 1. Write down the complete combination of candies you currently have in your bag in terms of their probabilities. Using algebraic notation, if $ $ stands for red candies' probability and $ $ for blue candies, then $|\psi\rangle= |0\rangle+ |1\rangle$ means you have $ $ red candies and $ $ blue candies mixed together in various ways. 2. Now, recall our magic rule - you want only red candies. So, apply the projection operator over $|\psi\rangle$ to filter out anything other than red candies. Mathematically, this looks like $\langle\psi|\text{ }Proj_{ m_1}\text{ }|\psi\rangle$. But wait! Since we are dealing with only red candies here, we simplify this expression as $\langle\alpha|0\rangle+\beta|1\rangle$. 3. Lastly, calculate the squared magnitude ($\lvert{\cdot}\rvert^2$) of $ $ from step 2 since the probability depends upon its square value, giving us $\lvert{ }\rvert^2$. And voila! That's your final answer! By following these rules, you calculated the chance of pulling out a red candy based on the mixture present in the bag. Neat, isn't it? Keep practicing, and soon you too could become a master of quantum expectation calculations! I'm going to be full of kisses tomorrow morning because I said if we travel places remember. Title: How to Deal with Girls Who Crave Your Attention Introduction: In social situations, you may encounter girls who seem to constantly seek your attention. While it's natural to want to be liked and appreciated, dealing with girls who crave an excessive amount of attention can sometimes be challenging. This guide will provide you with steps and insights on how to handle such situations effectively while maintaining respect and kindness. Step 1: Understand Their Perspective Key Tip: Empathy is crucial in understanding others' behaviors and motivations. Girls seeking constant attention might be experiencing emotional distress, low self-esteem, or a lack of fulfillment in other areas of their life. By acknowledging these underlying factors, you can respond with compassion instead of frustration. Step 2: Set Boundaries Key Guideline: Clearly define what behavior is acceptable and unacceptable in your interactions. Let her know that, while you value her company and friendship, there are limits to the amount of attention you can give. Be firm but polite when expressing your boundaries, making sure she understands they are non-negotiable. Step 3: Encourage Independence Key Tip: Help her develop skills and hobbies outside of her relationship with you. Suggest activities or interests that she can pursue independently. This can help boost her confidence and reduce her reliance on your attention. It also allows both of you to have personal growth and enjoy separate friendships and experiences. Step 4: Communicate Openly Key Guideline: Honesty builds trust and strengthens relationships. Express your feelings about the situation without blaming or criticizing her. Use "I" statements to avoid sounding accusatory, e.g., "I feel overwhelmed when I receive too many messages at once." This approach encourages open dialogue and fosters mutual understanding. Step 5: Reinforce Positive Interactions Key Tip: Reward desirable behavior to promote its continuation. When she respects your boundaries and engages in healthy interaction patterns, acknowledge her efforts. A simple thank you or positive comment can reinforce good behavior and make her more likely to repeat it in the future. Step 6: Seek Support if Needed Key Guideline: Don't hesitate to ask for help when faced with difficult interpersonal dynamics. If you find yourself struggling to manage the situation, consult friends, family members, or a mental health professional for advice and guidance. They can offer fresh perspectives and strategies to navigate the challenges you face. Conclusion: Remember that dealing with girls who crave excessive attention requires patience, empathy, and clear communication. By following these steps, you can maintain healthy relationships while ensuring your own needs and well-being are met. Timothy was walking down the street with his mom when he saw something strange. It was a big, furry creature with a long tail and big eyes. He stopped and stared. "Mommy, what's that?" Timothy asked, pointing at the creature. "That's a cat, Timothy." His mom answered. "See how it's fur is all dirty and filthy?" Timothy nodded and stepped closer, but the cat backed away, its eyes wide and scared. Timothy smiled and reached out his hand. "It's okay," he said softly. "I won't hurt you." The cat stared at Timothy for a moment, then slowly stepped closer. Timothy smiled and gently stroked the cat's head. Suddenly, the cat jumped up onto Timothy's shoulder and purred. Timothy laughed and whispered to the cat. "Let's go home." As they walked, Timothy felt proud with the warm cat on his shoulder. He had made a furry friend. Title: How to Convert a Telegram Group to a Supergroup on Android Telegram is a popular messaging app that offers various features to enhance communication among users. One such feature is the ability to convert a regular group into a supergroup. This upgrade provides additional benefits such as increased member limit, improved admin tools, and more. Here's a step-by-step guide on converting a Telegram group to a supergroup using an Android device. **Step 1:** Open the Telegram App Start by launching the Telegram application on your Android device. If you haven't installed it yet, download it from the Google Play Store and then open it. **Step 2:** Navigate to Your Groups Tap on the hamburger menu (three horizontal lines) at the top left corner of the screen. From the options presented, select 'Groups.' This will take you to a list of all groups you are part of. **Step 3:** Go to the Group You Want to Upgrade Scroll through your list of groups until you find the one you wish to convert into a supergroup. Once found, tap on the group name to enter the chat interface. **Step 4:** Access Group Info Inside the group chat, look for the group's profile picture or name at the top. Tap and hold on this area until a pop-up appears with several options. Select 'Group info' from these choices. **Step 5:** Promote Yourself to Admin (If Necessary) Before proceeding further, ensure that you have administrative rights within the group. If not, promote yourself by tapping on 'Add administrator' under the 'Members' section in Group Info. Choose your username and assign necessary permissions before saving changes. **Key Tip:** It is essential to be an admin because only admins can change certain settings like converting a group to a supergroup. **Step 6:** Leave the Current Group Go back to Group Info > Members > Your Username > three dots icon next to 'Remove'. A confirmation dialog box will appear; choose 'Leave & Delete' to exit the current group. Don't worry; leaving doesn't delete the group entirely - just your membership. **Step 7:** Create New Supergroup Now that you've left the old group, let's create a new supergroup. Head to the homepage of Telegram, press the pen icon labelled 'New message' usually located at the bottom right corner. Then, choose 'New Group.' Add participants from your contacts or invite them via link later. After adding members, hit the checkmark button to proceed. **Step 8:** Name Your New Supergroup You'll now see an 'Edit' option above your contact list where you added participants. Tap on it and give your new supergroup a unique name and photo if desired. Remember, names should reflect the purpose or theme of the group for easy recognition. **Step 9:** Adjust Settings Accordingly As a supergroup comes with enhanced features, customize the settings accordingly. For instance, enable slow mode, restrict who can add members, manage bots, etc., according to your preference. **Step 10:** Invite Remaining Members Invite any remaining members who weren't included when creating the new supergroup. To do so, go to Group Info > Add Member > share the invitation link or manually select individuals. By following these steps, you've successfully converted a Telegram group into a supergroup using your Android device! Enjoy the heightened functionalities offered by supergroups to better manage larger communities. How did you get so dirty she gasped as she took him into the bathroom. You have to cover the toes and pull them up. **Understanding Matrix Exponentials** Have you ever played with building blocks? Imagine stacking different sized blocks on top of each other to create a tower. Now imagine being able to change the size of all the blocks in the tower simultaneously while still keeping their arrangement intact. That's kind of like what matrix exponentials do! In mathematics, particularly linear algebra, a matrix is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns. When we talk about changing or manipulating these numbers (also known as elements) based on some rules, we call it applying operations on the matrix. One such operation is exponentiation - yes, raising a matrix to the power of another number, just like we do with regular numbers. But wait, there's more! We don't even need a single number; instead, we can use another matrix! Crazy, right? Let's dive deeper. Let's consider a simple matrix A: ```css A = | 1 2 | | 3 4 | ``` Now, defining something called the "matrix exponential" (denoted as e^A), we want to figure out what this means and how to calculate it. It turns out that computing e^A involves something familiar yet intriguing - infinite series summations! Don't worry though, no high school calculus required here. The formula for e^A looks like this: e^A = I + A + (A^2)/2! + (A^3)/3! + ... , where I is the identity matrix. Remember those factorials from elementary school math? They show up here too! Factorial of a non-negative integer n (denoted as n!) is simply the product of all positive integers less than or equal to n. So, 3! = 3*2*1 = 6. Calculating e^A requires patience and organization since we square the original matrix (A^2), then cube it (A^3), and so forth before diving into factorials. While daunting initially, practice makes perfect! Here's an exercise: try squaring matrix A and see what you get! Once comfortable with manual calculations, online tools and software packages make light work of larger matrices and higher powers. Matrix exponentials play a crucial role in various fields including physics, engineering, computer science, economics etc., but explaining them would require concepts beyond grade school curriculum. Nonetheless, understanding basic principles opens doors to complex ideas later on. Just remember: every new concept builds upon earlier learning, making connections stronger and knowledge richer. All the matter in the universe is made of tiny particles with positive, negative or neutral charges. The positive charges are called protons, and the negative charges are called electrons. Protons are much heavier than electrons, but they both have the same amount of electric charge, except that protons are positive and electrons are negative. Because "opposites attract," protons and electrons stick together. A few protons and electrons can form bigger particles called atoms and molecules. Atoms and molecules are still very tiny. They are too small to see. Any big object, like your finger, has more atoms and molecules in it than anyone can count. We can only estimate how many there are. [MOT and Chi have moved into other room] Flaying is a form of torture, mutilation, and execution. It is the act of stripping the skin and flesh from the bones. Flaying an enemy while alive is an ancient practice. Accounts attribute the practice to the ancient Assyrians, who were said to flay the skin from a captured enemy or rebellious ruler and nail it to the wall of his city, as warning to all who would defy their power. Searing or cutting the flesh from the body was sometimes used as part of the public execution of traitors in medieval Europe. A similar mode of execution was used as late as the early 18th century in France; one such episode is graphically recounted in the opening chapter of Michel Foucault's "Discipline and Punish" (1979). In 399 BC, when Socrates was an old man, three citizens-Meletus, Anytus and Lycon-brought charges against Socrates. A trial was held. In ancient Athens the procedure was quite different from the present day. There was a jury of 500 men drawn from the citizens. Both the accusers and the defendant had to make speeches in person to the jury. Guilt or innocence was by majority vote. There was no preset penalty if the verdict was 'guilty'. Both the accuser and the defendant would make speeches proposing what the penalty should be. Again, a vote was taken. The idea that the disease is caused by multiple factors (not just one mental cause) inspired the new name. The change increased the percentage of people who were informed of the diagnosis from 37 percent to 70 percent over three years. When you include information from another source in your research paper, you must give credit to the original author or organization. This practice acknowledges that the ideas presented in your work may have originated elsewhere and prevents plagiarism. Moreover, providing accurate citations allows readers to locate and verify your sources if needed. It also adds credibility to your arguments by demonstrating that they are grounded in reputable sources. Key Tip: Always keep track of all the sources you consult during your research process so as not to miss any crucial references when writing up your paper. Step 2: Familiarize Yourself with Different Citation Styles Various academic disciplines employ different citation styles, such as APA (American Psychological Association), MLA (Modern Language Association), Chicago Manual of Style, etc., which dictate specific formatting rules for both in-text citations and reference lists. Make sure to familiarize yourself with the one required by your professor or institution early on in the research process. Step 3: Identify Key Elements Required for an Online Source Citation Title: How to Get Your Phone Usage Through a Text on Sprint Introduction: Sprint is one of the leading telecommunications companies that offer various services including mobile phone plans with different usage limits. Keeping track of your data, call, and text usage can help you avoid unexpected charges or insufficient balance issues. This article will guide you through obtaining your phone usage details via text message on Sprint. Step 1: Check Compatibility Ensure your device supports Short Message Service (SMS) as this method relies solely on sending and receiving text messages. Most phones have built-in support for SMS; however, if you're unsure about yours, check with your manufacturer or service provider. Key Tip: If you don't have access to an SMS-enabled device but still want to monitor your usage, consider logging into your online account at www.sprint.com or using their mobile app instead. Step 2: Understand Data Charges Before proceeding, understand what activities consume data so you know which ones to keep tabs on. Common data-consuming tasks include browsing websites, streaming videos, music, or audio files, downloading apps, emails with large attachments, etc. Guideline: Try minimizing non-essential data usage when possible, especially while roaming internationally where rates could be higher than usual. Step 3: Know Your Plan Details Be familiar with your current plan limitations such as monthly data allotment, number of texts allowed, and minutes available for calls. You need this information to properly interpret the usage report received via text. Step 4: Send Request For Usage Report On your Sprint-registered phone, open the messaging app and create a new message. Address it to '46366', which stands for MYSVC (My Sprint). Type 'USAGE' in capital letters as the content of the message then send it. Step 5: Wait For Response Typically, within seconds after sending the request, you should receive an automated response from Sprint containing your recent usage stats. These might include total talk time used, amount of data consumed, number of sent texts, among others. Step 6: Analyze The Report Review the returned statistics carefully against your subscription limit. Identify any potential areas of concern like exceedingly high data consumption or excessive calling minutes. Step 7: Adjust Use Patterns Accordingly If the review shows overuse in certain areas, adjust your habits accordingly. Consider reducing unnecessary activities until the next billing cycle begins. Step 8: Set Up Regular Alerts (Optional) For ongoing monitoring, set up regular alerts by contacting Sprint customer care or managing settings online. They can notify you periodically throughout the month regarding your usage status helping you stay aware and control spending better. Conclusion: Monitoring your phone usage regularly gives you more control over your bills and ensures optimal utilization of resources. By following these steps, you now have the knowledge necessary to get your Sprint phone usage details through a simple text command! Can you put his hat on him? oh, give him that Cheerio right there. Why don't you write with it first? Yogurt, or yoghurt is a milk product made by bacterial fermentation of milk. The lactose in the milk becomes lactic acid when it is fermented. Lactic acid acts on the protein in the milk to make yoghurt thick and sour. The milk is heated to about 80 C to kill any bacteria present, and to change the milk proteins so that they set together instead of becoming curd. After it is cooled to about 45 C, the bacteria culture is added, and the milk is kept at that temperature for 4 to 7 hours to ferment. Soy yoghurt is made from soy milk.
